title: Make ServiceBus DI registration conditional in worker Program.cs
description: Spec: PR #276 (fd904f1) added unconditional AddServiceBusRestClient + AddPollingInfrastructure calls in api/src/town-crier.worker/Program.cs:193-194. AddServiceBusRestClient throws InvalidOperationException at registration time when ServiceBus:Namespace/QueueName/SubscriptionId/ResourceGroup are missing (api/src/town-crier.infrastructure/ServiceBus/ServiceBusServiceExtensions.cs:24-31). The digest, digest-hourly, and dormant-cleanup Container Apps Jobs do not have those env vars set (infra/EnvironmentStack.cs:384,390,399 — pollingBus argument omitted, so lines 511-516 do not inject them). Result: those three jobs have segfaulted (exit 139) on every run since 2026-04-21 22:00 UTC — silent failure because the throw happens before OTel/App Insights initialises. Fix: in api/src/town-crier.worker/Program.cs, only call AddServiceBusRestClient and AddPollingInfrastructure when builder.Configuration['ServiceBus:Namespace'] is non-empty. Add a regression test asserting the worker host can be built in a digest/dormant-cleanup-style configuration (no ServiceBus env vars set) without throwing.
notes:
COMPLETED: Added AddServiceBusPollingChainIfConfigured helper in api/src/town-crier.infrastructure/Polling/PollingServiceExtensions.cs that no-ops when ServiceBus:Namespace is empty. Replaced unguarded AddServiceBusRestClient + AddPollingInfrastructure calls in api/src/town-crier.worker/Program.cs:189-194 with the new helper. Two regression tests in PollingServiceExtensionsTests.cs lock in: (1) no-op behaviour when SB config absent (mimics digest/dormant-cleanup workers); (2) full chain registration when SB config present (mimics poll/poll-bootstrap). All 271 infra tests pass.

IN PROGRESS: ready for ship + release.

NEXT: /ship → PR Gate → auto-merge → /release patch (v0.9.21).

BLOCKER: none.

KEY DECISIONS: Single helper guards both AddServiceBusRestClient and AddPollingInfrastructure under one ServiceBus:Namespace check, so the guard can't get out of sync between the two registrations. Comment in the helper documents the load-bearing nature. Pre-existing integration tests (3 Auth0-dependent) still fail locally without INTEGRATION_TEST_AUTH0_DOMAIN secret — unrelated to this fix, pass in CI.

title: Revert ACR RetentionPolicy — Basic SKU rejects Policies block (pulumi up shared broken)
description: PR #294 added Policies.RetentionPolicy to acrtowncriershared in SharedStack.cs, but the registry is Basic SKU. Azure rejects any Policies block on Basic ('Policies are only supported for managed registries in Premium SKU'), so every pulumi up on the shared stack now fails. Fix: remove the Policies block. Follow-up: alternative cleanup strategy since tc-v467's original objective remains.

title: hotfix: PollingOptions.HandlerBudget default — unblock prod poll-sb worker
description:
PR #291 (T16) added a fail-fast guard that aborts poll-sb when HandlerBudget is null. The value is never bound from config and defaults to null, so every job-tc-poll-prod execution since 2026-04-24 08:02 UTC has aborted at startup. Production polling has been completely halted (~30s retry cadence, hundreds of failed executions, 1 stuck SB message that's never consumed).

Fix: change PollingOptions.HandlerBudget default from null to TimeSpan.FromMinutes(4) (fits inside OrchestratorLeaseTtl=4.5min); delete stale Program.cs comment claiming HandlerBudget is left null in production; keep the T16 guard as belt-and-braces.

Spec ref: docs/specs/polling-lease-cas.md § Invariants (lease TTL > max handler runtime).
close_reason: Hotfix shipped as PR #292 (squash ffe436e), released as v0.9.13, deployed to prod 09:20 UTC. First successful job execution at 09:20:06 UTC ('Poll trigger queue empty, exiting cleanly'). DLQ=1 is the v0.9.12-era stuck message that hit its 1h TTL during the outage — benign debris. Bootstrap cron at :30 will re-seed an empty queue.

title: Fix Service Bus namespace double-suffix bug breaking poll bootstrap
description: ServiceBusServiceExtensions.cs:35 builds the REST client BaseAddress as 'https://{ns}.servicebus.windows.net' and the Pulumi env var ServiceBus__Namespace is set to the FQDN already, producing 'sb-town-crier-prod.servicebus.windows.net.servicebus.windows.net' which fails DNS. PollTriggerBootstrapper.ReceiveAsync throws, the CA1031 catch-all swallows it, and no seed is ever published — KEDA never scales job-tc-poll-prod and the SB-coordinated polling cycle stays dead despite v0.9.1-v0.9.6. Confirmed in App Insights at 2026-04-22T07:03:04Z. Fix: make ServiceBusServiceExtensions.cs accept either bare name or FQDN by stripping/skipping the suffix when already present. Follow-up: align infra to use NamespaceShortName instead of NamespaceFqdn for hygiene.
acceptance_criteria: ServiceBusServiceExtensions.cs accepts both bare name and FQDN forms; new TUnit test covers both; safety-net job at next tick publishes a seed; KEDA scales job-tc-poll-prod within 5min of seed enqueue.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #282 — ServiceBusServiceExtensions now accepts both bare name and FQDN for ServiceBus__Namespace, eliminating the doubled-suffix DNS NXDOMAIN that silently broke the SB-coordinated polling cycle.

title: Pin Pulumi.AzureNative to 3.16.0 — CD Production blocked by package drift
description:
## Context

CD Production for v0.9.5 (and CD Dev for #280) failed at `pulumi up` with:

```
infra/EnvironmentStack.cs(11,59): error CS0234: The type or namespace name 'ManagedServiceIdentityType' does not exist in the namespace 'Pulumi.AzureNative.App'
infra/SharedStack.cs(17,40): error CS0234: The type or namespace name 'Inputs' does not exist in the namespace 'Pulumi.AzureNative.Communication'
```

`infra/town-crier.infra.csproj` uses `PackageReference Version="3.*"` for both `Pulumi` and `Pulumi.AzureNative`. There is no `packages.lock.json`. Each CI restore picks the newest matching version. A new Pulumi.AzureNative 3.x release (between v0.9.4 succeeding ~22:00 UTC and v0.9.5 failing ~06:21 UTC) broke the API surface we use.

Local restore is on 3.16.0 and builds cleanly.

## Impact

- **All CD deploys are blocked** until pinned. v0.9.5 (which contains the safety-net image fix tc-nc5y) cannot reach prod.
- Polling outage continues — safety-net job still on quickstart placeholder image, queue still empty, no PlanIt polling.

## What to change

In `infra/town-crier.infra.csproj`, pin both packages to the known-good version:

```xml
<PackageReference Include="Pulumi" Version="3.*" />
<PackageReference Include="Pulumi.AzureNative" Version="3.*" />
```

→

```xml
<PackageReference Include="Pulumi" Version="[3.205.0]" />
<PackageReference Include="Pulumi.AzureNative" Version="[3.16.0]" />
```

(Use whatever `Pulumi` version is in obj/project.assets.json locally — confirm via `grep '"Pulumi/' obj/project.assets.json` first.)

`[X.Y.Z]` is exact-version syntax in NuGet — no floating.

## Acceptance

- `dotnet build` clean in `/infra`.
- Next CD Production tag: `Pulumi up (prod)` succeeds.
- Polling outage ends — verify queue gets a bootstrap message within 30 min of deploy.

## Follow-up (separate bead, file after this ships)

- Add `<RestorePackagesWithLockFile>true</RestorePackagesWithLockFile>` and commit `packages.lock.json` so any future floating ranges are still reproducible.
- Consider Renovate/Dependabot for controlled updates instead of floating ranges.

title: Fix Log Analytics retention to minimum 30 days
description: PerGB2018 SKU requires minimum 30-day retention. CD Dev failed because we set it to 14 days. Revert workspace retention to 30, keep App Insights at 14 (which has different limits).

title: iOS settings: saved-applications push/email toggles snap back on after tap
description:
On iOS Settings, the 'Saved Applications - Push' and 'Saved Applications - Email' toggles are stuck on. Tapping briefly flips them off, then they immediately revert to on. The interaction has no persisted effect.

Repro:
1. Open iOS app → Settings
2. Tap 'Saved Applications - Push' (or Email)
3. Observe: toggle momentarily flips off then snaps back on

Likely cause: ViewModel state not being persisted, or PUT/PATCH to notification preferences endpoint failing/being ignored, or the UI binding is reading from a stale source after the round-trip. Worth checking the notification-preference repository write path and whether the toggle is bound to a derived source that overrides local state on every refresh.
notes: COMPLETED: Added UseStringEnumConverter=true to AppJsonSerializerContext via [JsonSourceGenerationOptions] (api/src/town-crier.web/AppJsonSerializerContext.cs); added regression test Should_AcceptDigestDayAsString_When_PatchBodyUsesEnumName covering PATCH/GET round-trip with digestDay='Wednesday' (api/tests/town-crier.web.tests/UserProfiles/UpdateUserProfileEndpointTests.cs). All non-integration suites green: domain 52/52, application 486/486, infrastructure 286/286, web 70/70. dotnet format clean. IN PROGRESS: nothing — awaiting orchestrator merge. NEXT: orchestrator closes bead, opens PR, ships; iOS toggle snap-back symptom self-resolves once API is deployed (no iOS code change needed). BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: chose source-generator option (UseStringEnumConverter=true) over runtime JsonStringEnumConverter — keeps Native AOT compile-time path. SubscriptionTier's per-type [JsonConverter] takes precedence and was unchanged. Pre-existing integration-test failures (HealthTests, ApiClientFixture) are env-var-only failures unrelated to this change — they always fail without prod credentials.

title: Fix CI: NotificationDecisionBadge.displayLabel must be nonisolated
description: iOS Build & test failed in CI for PR #341 with 'call to main actor-isolated static method displayLabel(for:) in a synchronous nonisolated context' across 9 NotificationDecisionBadgeTests. Local swift test on macOS tolerates it; iOS Xcode build is stricter. Fix: mark NotificationDecisionBadge.displayLabel(for:) as nonisolated — it's a pure mapping function with no actor state.
notes: COMPLETED: Added 'nonisolated' keyword to NotificationDecisionBadge.displayLabel(for:) static func — fixes iOS Xcode strict actor isolation error in CI for PR #341 (call to main actor-isolated static method in synchronous nonisolated context, 9 test methods affected). swift test still passes 1077 tests locally. IN PROGRESS: nothing. NEXT: orchestrator merges; CI re-runs the iOS Build & test job on PR #341 and should now pass. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: SwiftUI View types are MainActor-isolated by default in newer Swift, which propagates to nested static funcs even when they touch only value types — the fix is to explicitly opt out with 'nonisolated' since displayLabel is a pure NotificationItem -> String? mapping (no actor state, no UI side effects). Other static helpers (color/icon) on the type return Color values and stay MainActor-isolated since they're only called from body.

title: Integration test PollSbEntrypointIntegrationTests still uses FakeDecisionAlertDispatcher after A7b
description: After tc-so3a.20 swapped PollPlanItCommandHandler to require IDecisionEventDispatcher, the integration-tests project no longer compiles. PollSbEntrypointIntegrationTests.CreateHandler at line 161 inline-news a FakeDecisionAlertDispatcher() (which only implements IDecisionAlertDispatcher), and the project's csproj shares FakeDecisionAlertDispatcher.cs via a linked Compile Include (lines 29-30 of town-crier.integration-tests.csproj). Two fixes needed (scope-permitting they could happen here in 7b's wake or fold into 7c since FakeDecisionAlertDispatcher.cs is being deleted by 7c anyway): (a) in PollSbEntrypointIntegrationTests.cs swap new FakeDecisionAlertDispatcher() -> new FakeDecisionEventDispatcher() and update the inline using directive; (b) add a Compile Include link in town-crier.integration-tests.csproj for FakeDecisionEventDispatcher.cs (mirroring lines 29-30). The bead tc-so3a.20 STOP CONDITIONS explicitly said to file a discovered-from bead rather than chase this. Build error: api/tests/town-crier.integration-tests/Polling/PollSbEntrypointIntegrationTests.cs(161,13): error CS1503: Argument 8: cannot convert from 'TownCrier.Application.Tests.Polling.FakeDecisionAlertDispatcher' to 'TownCrier.Application.Notifications.IDecisionEventDispatcher'.
notes: COMPLETED: Two-file mechanical fix for build break — (a) PollSbEntrypointIntegrationTests.cs line 161 swapped 'new FakeDecisionAlertDispatcher()' to 'new FakeDecisionEventDispatcher()'; (b) town-crier.integration-tests.csproj added Compile Include link for FakeDecisionEventDispatcher.cs (mirrors line 29-30 FakeDecisionAlertDispatcher pattern). IN PROGRESS: nothing. NEXT: orchestrator merges; bead can be closed. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: No using directive change needed — both fakes share namespace TownCrier.Application.Tests.Polling, already imported on line 5. dotnet build succeeded. dotnet test: 18/21 integration tests passed; 3 failures all due to missing INTEGRATION_TEST_API_BASE_URL / INTEGRATION_TEST_AUTH0_DOMAIN env vars (HealthTests + 2 ApiClientFixture tests) — expected per bead description. Both PollSbEntrypoint tests passed.

Cascade through every Polling test that constructs the handler. Mechanical sweep — no design decisions, no new behavior.
notes: COMPLETED: All 11 in-scope files done — PollPlanItCommandHandler swapped to IDecisionEventDispatcher (using/field/ctor/call site/comments), new FakeDecisionEventDispatcher.cs created, PollPlanItCommandHandlerDecisionAlertTests renamed (Should_DispatchDecisionAlert_* -> Should_RaiseDecisionEvent_*, FakeDecisionAlertDispatcher -> FakeDecisionEventDispatcher), 9 sibling Polling tests cascaded. All 476 application.tests Polling tests pass. dotnet format clean for application + application.tests projects. 3 commits on autopilot/tc-so3a.20: 42e30f5 (handler), 4022620 (fake + DecisionAlertTests), aa848ee (9 siblings). IN PROGRESS: nothing — checklist exhausted. NEXT: orchestrator should merge tc-so3a.20; then pick up tc-88uy (the discovered-from side-quest) before tc-so3a.21 (A7c). Filed tc-88uy with discovered-from link. KEY DECISIONS: stopped at the integration-test build failure exactly as the bead's STOP CONDITIONS instructed (prior-worker pattern). Did NOT touch FakeDecisionAlertDispatcher.cs / IDecisionAlertDispatcher.cs / Dispatch*Alert* — they stay until tc-so3a.21 (A7c). Used replace_all when the substring was unambiguous, but verified each helper's signature change individually.

Mirror the existing IDecisionAlertDispatcher / DispatchDecisionAlertViaHandler shape so the polling pipeline (touched in 7b) can swap dispatchers cleanly. No callers, no test changes — build stays green throughout.

Remaining: add Decision property to Notification entity (nullable, type matches PlanIt vocabulary), make WatchZoneId nullable, cascade through NotificationDocument, GenerateHourlyDigestsCommandHandler, GenerateWeeklyDigestsCommandHandler, ExportedNotification. Added 3 unit tests in NotificationTests covering: Decision stored, Decision defaults null, null WatchZoneId allowed. title: A10: API endpoints carry new preference fields
description: Spec: https://github.com/AmyDe/town-crier/issues/337 — UpdateZonePreferencesCommand: drop StatusChanges, add 4 new bools. UpdateUserProfileCommand: add SavedDecisionPush, SavedDecisionEmail. GetZonePreferencesResult + GetUserProfileResult mirror. ExportUserData JSON updated.
notes: COMPLETED: A10 wired the new preference fields through the API endpoint shape. UpdateUserProfileCommand and UpdateUserProfileResult now expose SavedDecisionPush + SavedDecisionEmail (default true). GetUserProfileResult mirrors them. UpdateUserProfileCommandHandler and GetUserProfileQueryHandler propagate them. ExportedNotificationPreferences (and ExportUserDataQueryHandler) include them in the data export. UserProfileDocument (Cosmos persistence) round-trips both flags as bool? with legacy default-true coalesce, matching the EmailDigestEnabled pattern. WatchZoneEndpoints had its dead InsufficientTierException catch removed (free-tier policy moved to dispatch-time per spec). Watch-zone request/response side already uses the 4 new toggles end-to-end (UpdateZonePreferencesCommand/Result, GetZonePreferencesResult, ExportedZonePreferences). Tests: +3 UpdateUserProfile (SavedDecisionPush/Email + default), +1 ExportUserData (SavedDecision flags), +2 UserProfileDocument (round-trip + legacy default-true), +1 GetUserProfileQuery. All four unit suites green: 49 domain + 452 application + 284 infrastructure + 68 web = 853 pass. IN PROGRESS: none. NEXT: A6 wires the new toggles into actual dispatch logic (NewApplicationEmail/DecisionPush/DecisionEmail/SavedDecisionPush/SavedDecisionEmail gating). I1/I2/W1/W2 deliver the iOS + Web UIs. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) UserProfileDocument uses bool? + ?? true for SavedDecisionPush/Email, matching the established EmailDigestEnabled pattern so legacy Cosmos documents hydrate as opt-in (preserves prior default-on semantics). (2) Defaults stay in the command record (= true) so existing API clients that omit the new fields are treated as opt-in, matching domain defaults. (3) InsufficientTierException class itself was left in place — only the dead catch was removed. The exception may be re-used at dispatch time per A6's scope. (4) No web endpoint test was added — the PUT /v1/me/watch-zones/{zoneId}/preferences endpoint passes the request DTO directly to the application command, which is fully covered by UpdateZonePreferencesCommandHandlerTests.

title: A6: Unified DispatchDecisionEventCommandHandler — fan-out + OR-merge
description: Spec: https://github.com/AmyDe/town-crier/issues/337 — New handler: takes (application). Computes zone-matchers ∪ bookmark-holders. For each user, OR-merges per-channel toggles across sources. One Notification row per user with Sources flags. Honours paid-tier gate for instant push.
notes: COMPLETED: DispatchDecisionEventCommandHandler shipped in api/src/town-crier.application/Notifications/DispatchDecisionEventCommand{,Handler}.cs. Computes union of zone matchers (via IWatchZoneRepository.FindZonesContainingAsync on app coords) and bookmark holders (via ISavedApplicationRepository.GetUserIdsByApplicationUidAsync). For each user: OR-merges Sources flags; resolves push opt-in as (zone match AND zone.DecisionPush) OR (saved match AND profile.SavedDecisionPush); gates push on PushEnabled AND tier!=Free; persists one Notification row with eventType=DecisionUpdate, decision=raw AppState, sources=Zone|Saved as appropriate. Idempotent on (userId, applicationUid, DecisionUpdate). Telemetry: NotificationsCreated.Add(1), NotificationsSent.Add(1) on push. Registered in worker DI at Program.cs:159. 13 TUnit tests in tests/town-crier.application.tests/Notifications/DispatchDecisionEventCommandHandlerTests.cs cover: zone-only, saved-only, OR-merge into single notification with combined Sources, idempotent on existing DecisionUpdate, coexists with NewApplication for same UID, push for paid+optin, free-tier no-push, master push off, zone DecisionPush off, SavedDecisionPush off, OR-merge fires push when either source opted in, missing profile, no devices, fan-out to multiple users. IN PROGRESS: nothing. NEXT: tc-so3a.7 — wire poll handler to raise DispatchDecisionEventCommand on transition + drop legacy IDecisionAlertDispatcher path. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) Web DI does NOT register the handler — IPushNotificationSender lives only in worker (matches existing DispatchNotificationCommandHandler pattern). (2) WatchZoneId is set to the FIRST matching zone if multiple match a user; saved-only matches leave it null (kept simple — UI only needs presence vs absence per spec). (3) Decision string stores raw PlanIt AppState (Permitted/Conditions/Rejected/Appealed); UkPlanningVocabulary mapping happens at render time. (4) Telemetry uses untagged counters for parity with DispatchNotificationCommandHandler — tc-so3a.12 will introduce tagged versions. SIDE-QUEST: tc-85d2 filed for flaky web test ServerRequestTracingTests.Should_ExportExceptionEvent_When_EndpointThrows (didn't recur after rebuild but is intermittent).

title: A4: Change idempotency key to (userId, applicationUid, eventType)
description: Spec: https://github.com/AmyDe/town-crier/issues/337 — Replace current (userId, application.Name) dedup with applicationUid + eventType triple. Update INotificationRepository.GetByUserAndApplicationAsync signature + Cosmos query.
notes: COMPLETED: Idempotency key rotation from (userId, applicationName) to (userId, applicationUid, eventType). Notification entity now exposes ApplicationUid alongside ApplicationName. NotificationDocument has nullable applicationUid field with ToDomain coalesce fallback to applicationName for legacy rows (mirrors tc-so3a.3's EventType/Sources backfill pattern). INotificationRepository.GetByUserAndApplicationAsync signature now (userId, applicationUid, eventType, ct) — all three impls updated (Cosmos query updated to filter by c.applicationUid AND c.eventType; InMemory + FakeNotificationRepository match by triple). DispatchNotificationCommandHandler now passes application.Uid + NotificationEventType.NewApplication. Two new dispatch tests cover (a) DecisionUpdate already exists → NewApplication still creates a row (no cross-event dedup), (b) NewApplication exists → second NewApplication dedups. All unit suites pass: domain (52), application (454), infrastructure (286), web (68). Format clean, build clean. IN PROGRESS: nothing. NEXT: tc-so3a.6 (unified DispatchDecisionEventCommandHandler with fan-out + OR-merge) — depends on this idempotency fix to land. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) Added ApplicationUid as a NEW field on the entity rather than renaming ApplicationName — UI/digests still need the human-readable case ref; PRD says 'Rotate the dedup key' but legacy rows (tc-so3a.3 backfill) only have applicationName, so the lazy ToDomain coalesce uses applicationName as the uid fallback. (2) Made the dedup query include eventType.ToString() so legacy rows backfilled with EventType=NewApplication continue to dedup correctly (tc-so3a.3's lazy hydration handles the null case). (3) NotificationBuilder defaults applicationUid to 'test-uid-001'; CreateCommand in DispatchNotificationCommandHandlerTests now takes applicationUid as second optional arg so existing tests stay compact while loop-iteration tests can supply unique uids.

comments.author: AmyDe
comments.text:
DECISION NEEDED [tc-so3a.4]

Bead scope says 'change GetByUserAndApplicationAsync to take applicationUid + eventType' and 'update DispatchNotificationCommandHandler to pass eventType through'. But the Notification entity currently stores only ApplicationName, not ApplicationUid:

- Notification.ApplicationName (e.g., 'APP/2026/0001') — stored on entity + NotificationDocument
- PlanningApplication.Uid — different identifier (e.g., '26/01471/TR'), NOT currently stored on Notification

Cosmos query today: WHERE c.applicationName = @appName. For the new dedup query (WHERE c.applicationUid = @appUid AND c.eventType = @evt) to work, the Notification document needs an applicationUid field.

Options:
A) Add ApplicationUid to Notification entity + NotificationDocument (in addition to existing ApplicationName). Honors PRD verbatim. Touches more files than scope reads as: domain entity, document round-trip, builder, dispatch handler.
B) Rename parameter only — keep using application.Name as the dedup value, just rename it to 'applicationUid' in the signature + add eventType. Minimal scope; dedup is still semantically by Name.
C) Switch the dedup value from application.Name to application.Uid and store Uid in place of Name on Notification (rename column). Breaks legacy rows backfilled by tc-so3a.3.

My recommendation: A — bead title literally says 'idempotency key to (userId, applicationUid, eventType)'. PRD says 'new writes use (userId, applicationUid, eventType)'. There is no other way to make that work. Adds ApplicationUid alongside ApplicationName.

comments.author: AmyDe
comments.text:
DECISION [tc-so3a.4] (worker self-resolved given autopilot context)

Going with Option A: add ApplicationUid field to Notification entity + NotificationDocument alongside existing ApplicationName. Rationale:
- Bead title and PRD both literally specify '(userId, applicationUid, eventType)' as the dedup tuple. Without storing Uid on the row, the Cosmos query cannot key on it.
- PRD explicitly says 'new writes use (userId, applicationUid, eventType)' and 'any in-flight rows under the old (userId, applicationName) shape stay' — which means new writes need the new column.
- ApplicationName stays untouched (still rendered in UI, digests, etc.). ApplicationUid is added as a new required field.
- Cosmos document needs the new field to be nullable for legacy hydration (mirrors the EventType/Sources lazy-coalesce pattern from tc-so3a.3). Legacy rows hydrate with ApplicationUid = ApplicationName fallback so dedup queries against legacy rows still return non-null even before backfill.

title: A5: Free-tier policy — drop 5/mo cap, add paid-tier gate at dispatch
description: Spec: https://github.com/AmyDe/town-crier/issues/337 — Remove FreeMonthlyNotificationCap from DispatchNotificationCommandHandler. Add SubscriptionTier != Free check on instant push path. Free tier still gets row written for weekly digest.
notes: COMPLETED: Removed FreeMonthlyNotificationCap (5/mo cap) and CountByUserInMonthAsync call from DispatchNotificationCommandHandler. Added paid-tier gate: free users get the Notification row written (for weekly digest pickup) but no instant push. IN PROGRESS: nothing. NEXT: tc-so3a.6 (unified DispatchDecisionEventCommandHandler with fan-out + OR-merge) is unblocked. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) Replaced cap-with-counter logic with simple SubscriptionTier == Free early-return, dropping the existing zone-pref/push-pref guards above it, matching PRD bullet 'Drop FreeMonthlyNotificationCap; add paid-tier gate to dispatcher; free-tier users still accumulate Notification rows.' (2) Existing unit tests for paid-flow rewritten to seed a Pro user (was Free). (3) Should_EnforceFreeTierCap and Should_ResetCap tests removed — encoded the old cap policy. (4) Filed tc-wqw8 (discovered-from) to remove now-unused INotificationRepository.CountByUserInMonthAsync — kept out of this bead's scope per PRD wording.

title: A1: Extend zone + profile notification preferences (decision toggles)
description: Spec: https://github.com/AmyDe/town-crier/issues/337 — Drop StatusChanges from ZoneNotificationPreferences. Add NewApplicationPush, NewApplicationEmail, DecisionPush, DecisionEmail (default true). Add SavedDecisionPush, SavedDecisionEmail to NotificationPreferences (default true).
notes: COMPLETED: Phase A domain rewrite + Phase B application/web cascade per AmyDe's Option C. ZoneNotificationPreferences now has 4 toggles (NewApplicationPush, NewApplicationEmail, DecisionPush, DecisionEmail, all default true). NotificationPreferences gained SavedDecisionPush and SavedDecisionEmail (default true). Free-tier guard removed from UserProfile.SetZonePreferences. All cascade callers updated: UpdateZonePreferencesCommand, GetZonePreferencesResult, ExportedZonePreferences, UpdateZonePreferencesResult, UpdateZonePreferencesCommandHandler, GetZonePreferencesQueryHandler, ExportUserDataQueryHandler, DispatchNotificationCommandHandler (uses NewApplicationPush), WatchZoneEndpoints. Existing tests updated mechanically. Build green; all 834 unit tests pass (domain 37 + application 447 + infrastructure 282 + web 68). Integration-tests skipped (require Cosmos env). IN PROGRESS: none. NEXT: A10 (tc-so3a.10) refines API endpoint shape to expose the new toggles. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) Option C cascade chosen so build stays green between A1 and A10. (2) DispatchNotificationCommandHandler maps old NewApplications gate to new NewApplicationPush toggle (push-suppression semantics preserved; email branch deferred to A10). (3) WatchZoneEndpoints still catches InsufficientTierException as dead-but-valid catch — A10 to clean up alongside endpoint shape changes. (4) Free-tier policy moves to dispatch-time per spec; domain no longer rejects toggle combos.

comments.author: AmyDe
comments.text:
DECISION NEEDED [tc-so3a.1]

Bead description says 'Drop StatusChanges' but spec table for per-zone preferences lists only the 4 new toggles (NewApplicationPush, NewApplicationEmail, DecisionPush, DecisionEmail). Existing record also has NewApplications and DecisionUpdates.

Options:
A) Drop StatusChanges only — keep NewApplications and DecisionUpdates alongside the 4 new toggles. Record ends with 6 fields. Awkward but minimal blast radius.
B) Replace record with the 4 new toggles only — drop NewApplications and DecisionUpdates too. Matches spec table literally. Larger blast radius into UpdateZonePreferencesCommand etc but A10 will fix those anyway.
C) Same as B but also handle the cascade into Application layer (UpdateZonePreferencesCommand, GetZonePreferencesResult, ExportedZonePreferences, WatchZoneEndpoints) so the build stays green between A1 and A10.

My recommendation: C — A1 must leave the build green, and the cleanest path is full replacement plus cascading the rename so callers compile. A10 will then refine API endpoint shape (which is its scope per its description). Otherwise A1 ships a broken build.

Also: should existing free-tier guard in UserProfile.SetZonePreferences be updated/removed? With new defaults (all true) and free-tier policy moving to dispatch-time, the domain-level block on StatusChanges/DecisionUpdates is obsolete. Recommend remove the guard.

title: A2: Extend Notification entity with EventType, Sources, Decision; UK vocab helper
description: Spec: https://github.com/AmyDe/town-crier/issues/337 — Add EventType (NewApplication|DecisionUpdate), Sources flags (Zone|Saved), Decision (nullable), make WatchZoneId nullable. Centralised UK vocabulary mapping (Permitted→Approved, Conditions→Approved with conditions, Rejected→Refused, Appealed→Refusal appealed).
notes:
Autopilot 2026-04-29 attempt 1: worker timed out after 2 of ~5 TDD cycles. Got: NotificationEventType enum (committed green) + NotificationSources flags (red commit, uncommitted partial green NotificationSources.cs). Did NOT do: Decision property, nullable WatchZoneId, UK vocab helper. Branch autopilot/tc-so3a.2 has c0f7f7b/e0ea1d7/0a6dc91 ahead of main; partial green discarded with worktree.
Retry 2026-04-30 with explicit scope: only Notification entity + UK vocab helper + their unit tests. Defer handler/persistence to tc-so3a.3+.
Autopilot 2026-04-30 retry: salvaged green commit (NotificationEventType + NotificationSources + UkPlanningVocabulary + tests). Worker timed out before completing Decision property + nullable WatchZoneId cascade. Filed tc-70c0 for the remaining work; sibling beads tc-so3a.3/.6/.8 now depend on tc-70c0.

title: A3: Cosmos persistence + backfill existing Notification rows
description: Spec: https://github.com/AmyDe/town-crier/issues/337 — Add new fields to NotificationDocument. One-shot backfill: tag all existing rows with EventType=NewApplication, Sources=Zone.
notes: COMPLETED: Added EventType (NotificationEventType) and Sources (NotificationSources) properties to Notification entity (mirroring tc-70c0's Decision pattern); added eventType + sources nullable string fields to NotificationDocument with FromDomain writes ToString() and ToDomain coalesces nulls to NewApplication/Zone backfill defaults. Two new tests in NotificationDocumentTests: round-trip with DecisionUpdate + Zone|Saved flag combo, and legacy hydration (null fields → NewApplication/Zone). All test suites pass: domain (52), application (452), infrastructure (286), web (68). Format clean, build clean. IN PROGRESS: nothing. NEXT: tc-so3a.4 (idempotency key change). BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) Extending the entity (not just the document) was required because Notification.Reconstitute is positional — there's no way to persist EventType/Sources without exposing them on the domain side. Mirrors tc-70c0's pattern exactly. (2) Added EventType/Sources as optional named params on Create with defaults NewApplication/Zone — keeps ALL existing call sites unchanged (no cascade across handlers/tests needed). (3) Persisted as enum.ToString() rather than ints — readable in Cosmos JSON; legacy null is the discriminator for backfill. (4) Lazy ToDomain coalesce IS the backfill (no migration job), per the SavedDecisionPush ?? true precedent in UserProfileDocument.

title: fix(web): stabilise auto-selects-first-zone test by extending waitFor to applications assertion
description: The test 'auto-selects the first zone when zones become available' in useApplications.test.ts is flaky: waitFor only checks selectedZone (set synchronously in the effect), but the next assertion expects applications.length === 1 (set after the async fetchByZone resolves). Move both assertions inside waitFor.

title: API: WatchZone domain + persistence + endpoints carry pushEnabled/emailInstantEnabled
description: Spec: docs/specs/per-zone-notifications.md#t1-api-domain--persistence--endpoints
notes: COMPLETED: WatchZone domain (PushEnabled/EmailInstantEnabled, default true), WithUpdates extended, WatchZoneDocument round-trip + AOT-safe legacy-doc default-true via bool? coalesced to true at ToDomain (System.Text.Json source generator does NOT honor property initializers for missing JSON fields — this is the trap). WatchZoneSummary, CreateWatchZoneCommand/Request, UpdateWatchZoneCommand/Request now carry both flags. CreateWatchZoneCommandHandler, UpdateWatchZoneCommandHandler, ListWatchZonesQueryHandler propagate them. WatchZoneEndpoints PATCH + POST wire the new request fields through. AppJsonSerializerContext already lists the request/result types so AOT serialisation is unchanged. WatchZoneBuilder gained WithPushEnabled/WithEmailInstantEnabled. Domain tests (6), document tests (5), update-handler tests (3), create-handler tests (2), list-handler test (1) added. All 835 non-integration tests pass. dotnet format clean. IN PROGRESS: nothing. NEXT: T2 (tc-kh1s.2) — wire flags + tier check into push and instant-email delivery paths in the polling fan-out. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) Optional WatchZone constructor params with default true keeps all 29 existing callers compiling. (2) WatchZoneDocument uses bool? not bool=true because System.Text.Json source gen sets bool to default(bool)=false for missing JSON fields, ignoring property initialisers (verified with scratch repro). UserProfileDocument.EmailDigestEnabled has the same buggy pattern but is never tested for legacy-doc deserialisation — discovered-from candidate for follow-up. (3) ExportedWatchZone NOT updated — strictly out of T1 scope; can be a discovered-from bead if GDPR export needs the flags.

title: iOS: 'saved' filter shows wrong watch zone's saved applications, only after toggling watch zones
description: On the iOS app, the 'saved' filter on the Applications page (and possibly Map page) misbehaves with multiple watch zones. Repro: two watch zones exist (Plymouth, Kingston Upon Thames). On fresh app load, tapping 'Saved' shows an empty list. After flipping the active watch zone back and forth a couple of times and re-selecting 'Saved', the list populates — but it shows Kingston-area applications regardless of which watch zone is selected. The list differs from the Saved list shown in the web app for the same user, and may differ between attempts inside iOS too. Symptoms suggest one or more of: (a) saved-applications query is not scoped by watch zone; (b) ViewModel is caching the first-loaded watch zone's data and displaying it under the wrong selection; (c) request is racing with watch-zone-change and a stale response is overwriting the new one; (d) saved IDs are correct but resolution to applications is fetching the wrong set; (e) data divergence between iOS and server because the iOS app writes saves locally without syncing. Investigation only — do not fix yet. Use az/cosmos to verify server-side saved data, and ask the user to demonstrate state in either app.
notes:
COMPLETED: Investigation done. Verified with prod Cosmos (auth0|69c5af69d57c3071e8baa592) that user has 8 saves total — 7 Kingston (auth 314), 1 Richmond (auth 320, PA26/0443), 0 Plymouth. Same data on web and iOS; only ordering differs. Original 'Kingston when Plymouth selected' report was a partial misread — Plymouth actually shows the same 8 saves but in API order (Cosmos natural order ≈ savedAt insertion).

KEY DECISIONS: (1) iOS Saved filter should be scoped to active watch zone — dropping the cross-zone merge — to match product intent that all filter pills operate on the current zone's applications. (2) Loading state on iOS Saved filter is a separate UX bug. (3) Web /saved page redesign (mirror iOS zone+filter pills) is an open product question, not filed yet.

NEXT: Two follow-up beads filed and linked discovered-from this one — tc-wnap (zone-scope the Saved filter), tc-qz0j (add loading spinner). Close this bead.

ROOT CAUSE: ApplicationListViewModel.filteredApplications:50-64. Merge logic 'fromCurrentList + fromSavedData' produces a different order per zone because applications is sorted by receivedDate desc. Behaviour is deterministic and matches code.

title: iOS sign-in never POSTs /v1/me; new TestFlight users invisible to backend
description:
## Root cause
The iOS app exposes `userProfileRepository.create()` (POST /v1/me) on `APIUserProfileRepository` but no production code path invokes it. Only `.fetch()`, `.update()`, `.delete()` are called. So a TestFlight signup gets an Auth0 identity but no Cosmos `UserProfile` document, and the user is invisible to admin tooling and backend personalization.

## Evidence
- iOS code search: zero non-test callers of `userProfileRepository.create()` across `mobile/ios/packages/**` and `mobile/ios/town-crier-app/**`.
- App Insights (last 30d, host=`api.towncrierapp.uk`): 5 successful `POST /v1/me` hits — all from `town-crier-web` / `town-crier.web` (web onboarding at `web/src/features/onboarding/ConnectedOnboardingPage.tsx`). Zero from iOS.
- Server: `RecordUserActivityMiddleware` deliberately won't auto-create — comment at `api/src/town-crier.application/UserProfiles/RecordUserActivityCommandHandler.cs:27` states "registration happens via POST /v1/me". GET /v1/me returns 404 when missing; iOS swallows the 404 and falls back to `.free` tier.

## Effect
- iOS-only users have no Cosmos UserProfile document.
- Invisible to `tc list-users`, no server-side notification preferences, no device-token registration tied to a known user, no path to receive digests or push alerts.

## Fix shape
In `mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-presentation/Sources/Coordinators/AppCoordinator.swift` line 221, replace `userProfileRepository.fetch()` with `userProfileRepository.create()`. The server's `CreateUserProfileCommandHandler` is idempotent — if a profile exists it returns it (only side effect on existing is email backfill: `api/src/town-crier.application/UserProfiles/CreateUserProfileCommandHandler.cs:23-36`). `create()` returns non-optional `ServerProfile`, so the optional-binding pattern collapses. Rename `fetchServerTier()` to reflect the new ensure-and-fetch semantics. Update tests in `town-crier-tests/Sources/Features/AppCoordinatorTierResolutionTests.swift` and `SpyUserProfileRepository` accordingly.

## Recovery for stranded users
Once the fix ships, any existing iOS-only signup will get their profile created the next time they open the app (the new `create()` call backfills automatically).
notes: COMPLETED: Switched AppCoordinator tier-resolution path from GET /v1/me (which 404s for new iOS-only signups) to POST /v1/me (idempotent ensure-or-fetch). Renamed private fetchServerTier() to ensureServerProfileTier() and updated the doc comment to describe the new ensure-and-fetch semantics. Updated AppCoordinatorTierResolutionTests and AppCoordinatorOfferCodeTests to drive the spy via createResult/createCallCount instead of fetchResult/fetchCallCount. Added a new Red->Green test (resolveSubscriptionTier_callsCreateOnUserProfileRepository_toEnsureServerProfile) that proves the bug. Removed the now-unreachable resolveSubscriptionTier_preservesPreviousTier_whenServerReturnsNilProfile test (create() returns non-optional, so the 404=nil edge case no longer exists for this path). All 1008 swift tests pass; swiftlint --strict clean. IN PROGRESS: nothing. NEXT: orchestrator runs /ship to merge ios-ensure-profile branch and closes tc-a6it. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) Did NOT touch SettingsViewModel.fetchServerTier() — out of scope per orchestrator instructions; the settings tier-refresh path still uses GET /v1/me (cosmetic concern, not the bug). (2) Skipped swift-format pre-flight: running it repo-wide introduces 15 SwiftLint opening_brace violations because the two tools disagree on multiline if-let brace placement; this is a pre-existing repo-config conflict outside this bead's scope. SwiftLint --strict was already clean before formatting and remains clean. (3) Removed (rather than rewrote) the nil-profile test because POST /v1/me cannot return nil — the test was asserting a behavior the new code path cannot exhibit.

title: iOS legal docs are bundled and stale; fetch from API like web
description: iOS app ships hardcoded Swift constants for Privacy Policy and Terms of Service in mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-presentation/Sources/Features/Legal/LegalDocumentViewModel.swift. The constants are dated 16 March 2026 and contain only 6 privacy sections, while the API (GetLegalDocumentQueryHandler.cs) serves the current policy dated 2026-04-20 with 10 sections including critical UK GDPR coverage (Who We Are, International Transfers, Cookies, Retention, Security, Children/Changes). The web app uses ApiLegalDocumentPort to fetch /v1/legal/{type} at runtime. iOS should do the same so legal docs stay in sync with the API source-of-truth. Spec: docs/specs/ios-legal-docs-from-api.md
notes:
COMPLETED:
- API: privacy.json + terms.json embedded as resources via <EmbeddedResource> in town-crier.application.csproj
- API: GetLegalDocumentQueryHandler refactored to load via Assembly.GetManifestResourceStream + JsonSerializer with new LegalDocumentJsonSerializerContext (AOT-safe source-gen, camelCase). Cached per-doc in static Lazy<>.
- iOS: Package.swift declares resources: [.process("Resources/legal")] on TownCrierPresentation. Mirror JSON copies bundled.
- iOS: LegalDocumentViewModel rewrites — loads from Bundle.module, decodes via private LegalDocumentJSON Decodable, fatalError on lookup or decode failure. ISO date string formatted to "20 April 2026" via DateFormatter (en_GB, "d MMMM yyyy").
- scripts/sync-legal.sh copies API → iOS (API canonical)
- scripts/check-legal-drift.sh diff -q's both pairs, exits non-zero with clear "run sync-legal.sh" hint
- .github/workflows/legal-drift-check.yml runs check on PR, path-filtered to the two JSON dirs and the scripts
- CLAUDE.md updated with edit/sync workflow

VALIDATION:
- dotnet build (Release) clean
- dotnet test tests/town-crier.application.tests: 433/433 pass
- dotnet format --verify-no-changes: clean
- swift build clean
- swift test (full): 1008/1008 pass
- swiftlint --strict: 0 violations
- scripts/check-legal-drift.sh: passes locally

NEXT: review + ship via /ship skill (pushes feat branch, opens PR, auto-merge).

KEY DECISIONS:
- API copy is canonical, iOS is byte-equal mirror (drift check enforces). Chosen over single-source-at-repo-root because SPM strict path rules ruled out cross-package-root references and we wanted no symlink fragility.
- JSON in camelCase (matches what API returns externally) — iOS uses standard JSONDecoder, API uses source-gen context with PropertyNamingPolicy=CamelCase.
- ISO dates in JSON, formatted at view layer (matches web's existing pattern).
- fatalError on bundle lookup/decode failure — these are compiled-in, any failure means a build that should never have shipped.

title: [SRE] town-crier-api emits zero telemetry to App Insights — production API is unobservable
description:
## What

The production API container (`ca-town-crier-api-prod`, cloud_RoleName `town-crier-api`) has emitted **zero telemetry** to App Insights — not requests, not dependencies, not traces, not customMetrics, not exceptions. This is verified across the entire history of the App Insights workspace, not just the analysis window.

By contrast, the worker (`town-crier-worker`) and web (`town-crier-web`) cloud roles emit rich telemetry as expected.

## Evidence

```kql
union requests, traces, customMetrics, performanceCounters, dependencies, exceptions
| summarize count_ = count(), firstSeen = min(timestamp), lastSeen = max(timestamp) by cloud_RoleName
```

Returns:
- `town-crier-worker`: 8436 datapoints in last hour
- `town-crier-web`: 87 datapoints in last 15 min
- `town-crier-api`: **0 datapoints, ever**

## Verification

The API IS running and serving:
- `az containerapp show ca-town-crier-api-prod` → runningStatus=Running, latestRevision=ca-town-crier-api-prod--0000089
- `curl -i https://api.towncrierapp.uk/v1/me` → 401 (responds in 30-80ms)
- Three calls + 3+ minute wait → still no telemetry

## Configuration check

The container has both required env vars set:
- `OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=town-crier-api`
- `APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING=InstrumentationKey=95ca5bb6-...` (same workspace as worker)

Container app uses `minReplicas=0, cooldownPeriod=300s` — scale-to-zero. But cooldown is 5 min, which should give OTel plenty of time to flush before the container scales down.

## Likely root causes (in order of suspicion)

1. **Native AOT trimming has stripped OTel exporter components** — the API runs Native AOT (per CLAUDE.md), the worker likely does not. OTel reflection-based code paths may have been trimmed and now fail silently at startup.
2. OTel `AddAzureMonitor`/`UseOtlpExporter` registration is missing in API's `Program.cs`. Worker has it, API doesn't.
3. Background service that ships telemetry isn't running in the API process.

## Blast radius

We cannot observe ANYTHING on the user-facing surface in production:
- No request rates, latency, or error rates
- No dependency health for API → Cosmos / API → Auth0
- No exceptions visible in App Insights
- No business metrics from the API tier
- All future SRE observatory runs will continue to find nothing on the user-facing path

## Suggested investigation starting points

1. Check `/api/src/TownCrier.Api/Program.cs` and any startup extension — confirm `AddOpenTelemetry()...UseAzureMonitor()` (or equivalent) is present.
2. Compare to worker (`/api/src/TownCrier.Worker/Program.cs`) — see why worker telemetry works.
3. Check rd.xml / TrimmerRoots / RuntimeHostConfigurationOption entries for the API project — look for OTel-related preserve directives.
4. Run the API container locally with stdout logging enabled (`OTEL_LOG_LEVEL=debug`) and look for exporter init failures or trimmed-method exceptions.
5. Verify the connection string is reachable from the container's network — query `uksouth-1.in.applicationinsights.azure.com` from inside the container.

## Why this is P1

Production observability for the user-facing surface is the foundation of SRE. Right now if the API hangs, throws 500s, or has a Cosmos outage, we will only find out from user complaints — not from monitoring. Every minute this is unfixed is a minute we cannot detect a production incident on the API.
close_reason: False positive — API IS emitting telemetry. The OTEL_SERVICE_NAME env var (town-crier-api) is overridden by Program.cs:27 which hardcodes resource.AddService('town-crier-web'). API requests appear under cloud role 'town-crier-web' in AppRequests. Verified by 8 probe hits at 2026-04-25T18:21:36Z — all 8 visible in AppRequests with full URL, route name, duration, status code. Original SRE methodology mistakes: (1) trusted env var name over data; (2) used legacy 'requests' table name via az monitor app-insights query when workspace-based store uses 'AppRequests' (the alias returned empty). For future SRE runs: query Log Analytics workspace 842645cf-1439-4a2b-80e8-54bd02e326f9 directly using App* table names (AppRequests/AppDependencies/AppExceptions/AppMetrics/AppPerformanceCounters) when the legacy names return empty.

title: Polling fairness regression: 272 hwm=null authorities starved since PR #298
description:
Spec: The split-out of LastPollTime from HighWaterMark (PR #298 / tc-m6fx, deployed 2026-04-24T18:27Z) was intended to give every authority fair access to polling slots independent of whether it had ever returned applications. In practice the opposite has happened.

Empirical signal (Cosmos PollState query, 2026-04-25T06:51Z, ~12.5h post-deploy):

- 213 authorities have highWaterMark != null → ALL 213 polled since the fix (100%)
- 272 authorities have highWaterMark == null → ZERO polled since the fix (0%)
- 13 of those 272 last had LastPollTime updated 2026-04-18 (~174h / 7+ days ago)

The selection logic in CosmosPollStateStore.GetLeastRecentlyPolledAsync (api/src/town-crier.infrastructure/Polling/CosmosPollStateStore.cs:96-99) DOES sort 'never-polled-first, then ascending LastPollTime' — that part is fine in isolation. The starvation is structural, caused by the interaction with CycleAlternatingAuthorityProvider:

- Watched cycles (minute 0-14 of every 30) only target watch-zone authorities (the 213 hwm=set set, give or take). Each Watched cycle bumps LastPollTime for ~17 of those.
- Seed cycles (minute 15-29) target all 485. With Watched cycles having already burned PlanIt's per-window rate-limit budget, the very next Seed cycle hits 429 almost immediately. Empirically the 06:20 Seed cycle polled 1 authority and got rate-limited (rate_limited=1, retry_after_seconds=2803).
- Net rate of hwm=null polls = far less than 1/hour. Over 12.5h, exactly 0 hwm=null authorities have been polled.

Where to investigate:

1. PollPlanItCommandHandler.cs:248-307 — the rate-limit catch path. Confirm whether authorities listed in sortedIds AFTER the 429-triggering one have their LastPollTime bumped (anti-starvation guarantee) or get silently skipped until a future cycle.
2. CycleAlternatingAuthorityProvider — consider whether Seed cycles should pre-empt Watched cycles when there is a backlog of hwm=null authorities older than some threshold (e.g. 24h since last poll).
3. Watched-cycle minute window (0-14) vs Seed window (15-29) — consider giving Seed the FIRST window so it does not get rate-limit-starved by Watched.

Acceptance: After fix is deployed and 24h have passed, 0 authorities should have LastPollTime older than ~30h (i.e. one full cycle of all 485 at the current ~19/hour throughput). Verifiable by re-running the Cosmos query in this bead.

Discovered-from: /verify-polling on 2026-04-25 surfaced 99h hwm-age in App Insights; direct Cosmos query revealed the fairness regression. The companion retry-after work (tc-6nkn / tc-06yk / PR #301) is unrelated and functioning correctly.
notes: COMPLETED: Verified the structural fix described in human's direction is already implemented — selection in CosmosPollStateStore.GetLeastRecentlyPolledAsync and FakePollStateStore sorts purely by LastPollTime (never-polled first, then ascending), with HighWaterMark only used as INPUT to PlanIt page fetching and cursor staleness checks. No selection-time filtering by HWM exists anywhere in api/src. Added api/tests/town-crier.application.tests/Polling/PollPlanItCommandHandlerSeedFairnessTests.cs (6 tests) as regression coverage for: (a) Seed cycle polls hwm-stale ahead of hwm-fresh when LastPollTime is older, (b) never-polled authorities polled before any polled authority, (c) zero-app polls bump LastPollTime for both existing-state and first-ever-poll cases, (d) Watched cycle scoped to watch-zone authorities, (e) Seed cycle returns the wider all-authority pool. All 419 application tests + 269 infrastructure + 67 web tests green; integration tests fail with pre-existing missing-env-var (unrelated). dotnet format clean. IN PROGRESS: nothing. NEXT: orchestrator should run preflight then ship via PR. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) No production code change needed — the existing LastPollTime-only ordering is the structural fix the human described. (2) Tests are characterization tests that lock down current correct behavior so future changes can't silently re-introduce HWM as a selection signal. (3) Did NOT change rate-limit handling — per direction 4, skipped authorities correctly remain at top of LRU for next Seed cycle. (4) Did NOT escalate further despite tests being green-on-first-run because the human's direction explicitly asked for verification ('Verify what it's actually doing today before changing anything'); the verification result is 'no change needed in production code'.

title: Work around beads GH#3421: symlink dolt-server.port in new worktrees
description:
Upstream bug: https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/issues/3421 (open as of v1.0.2)

Symptom: after running `bd worktree create`, bd commands inside the new worktree fail with 'database not found on Dolt server at 127.0.0.1:<random-port>' — because .beads/dolt-server.port is not propagated from the main tree to the worktree.

Impact: autopilot is broken end-to-end after the tc-o8fz server-mode migration. Every worktree it creates for a worker will fail on the worker's first `bd update --claim`.

Workaround (verified working):
  ln -s ../../../../.beads/dolt-server.port <worktree>/.beads/dolt-server.port

Actions:
- autopilot/SKILL.md Phase 1: after `bd worktree create`, create the symlink to the main .beads/dolt-server.port.
- CLAUDE.md Worktree-First Rule: document the one-liner as a known-issue workaround, link to GH#3421.
- Revisit when GH#3421 lands — the workaround becomes harmless but removable.

Relative path depth rationale: project worktrees live at `.claude/worktrees/<name>`, which is 4 levels below the repo root, so `../../../../.beads/dolt-server.port` resolves to the main tree's port file.
close_reason: PR #300 merged. Autopilot Phase 2 now symlinks .beads/dolt-server.port into each new worktree so bd commands find the live server. CLAUDE.md documents the workaround for manual worktree creation. Removed duplicate bd-managed integration block and added --skip-agents guard for future bd init runs. Tracked upstream as beads GH#3421.

title: Migrate beads backend from embedded to server mode
description:
Remove lock contention for parallel autopilot workers + human bd commands.

Current: embedded mode (.beads/embeddeddolt/). Single-writer — any second bd command queues or times out. Confirmed cause of the 'lock errors' the user sees when running bd manually during autopilot.

Target: server mode (.beads/dolt/ + long-running dolt sql-server). Multi-reader/writer, no contention.

PRE-FLIGHT — MUST do before running any of the migration commands:
1. Confirm no autopilot/worker is active: `bd list --status=in_progress` must be empty.
2. All worktrees in .claude/worktrees/* fully committed and pushed.
3. Back up: cp -rf .beads .beads.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d) (belt + braces on top of bd backup)

MIGRATION STEPS (per beads README — destructive init, re-import from backup):
1. bd backup init ~/.beads-backups/town-crier
2. bd backup sync                           # push current DB to backup
3. bd init --server                         # destructive: wipes .beads/embeddeddolt
4. bd backup restore --force ~/.beads-backups/town-crier
5. bd dolt status                           # confirm server running
6. bd doctor                                # confirm DB healthy
7. bd ready --json | jq length              # confirm issues re-hydrated

POST:
- Server auto-starts transparently; no daemon management needed.
- Each worktree still shares the same DB via git common-directory discovery — no per-worktree config.
- Rollback path if anything goes wrong: restore .beads.bak.<date>.

Reference: https://github.com/gastownhall/beads#backup--migration and beads issue #1455 'Surface Dolt server mode as the solution for multi-agent/multi-worktree conflicts'.

DO NOT DISPATCH TO AUTOPILOT — must be run by the human during quiet time, not by a worker.
close_reason: Migrated beads backend from embedded to server mode. PR #299 merged. Server now running on 127.0.0.1:65184 in /Users/christy/Dev/town-crier/.beads/dolt. 416 issues restored with tc-* prefix preserved. bd doctor 72/72 pass, concurrent bd reads verified lock-free. Manual bd commands during autopilot no longer hit contention.

title: Fix SB queue-depth probe: use ARM management plane (not data plane)
description:
PR #289 (tc-gpof) shipped GetQueueDepthAsync against the Service Bus data-plane endpoint (<ns>.servicebus.windows.net) with ARM api-version=2017-04, expecting ARM-style JSON. The data plane returns Atom XML so System.Text.Json throws JsonException('<' is an invalid start of a value) every 30 min in the poll bootstrap, which swallows the error via CA1031. Result: queue stays at 0/0, KEDA never wakes job-tc-poll-prod, polling has been dead since 2026-04-22T21:20Z.

Fix: split IServiceBusRestClient into a data-plane interface (PublishAsync, ReceiveOneAsync) and a new IServiceBusManagementClient (GetQueueDepthAsync) hitting https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{sub}/resourceGroups/{rg}/providers/Microsoft.ServiceBus/namespaces/{ns}/queues/{queue}?api-version=2021-11-01 with token scope https://management.azure.com/.default. Infra already grants the required Reader role on the queue (EnvironmentStack.cs:725). Add ServiceBus__SubscriptionId and ServiceBus__ResourceGroup env vars in Pulumi.

Acceptance: bootstrap job reseeds poll queue to 1 scheduled message; job-tc-poll-prod resumes executing.

title: Switch poll-triggers queue to receive-and-delete + management-API probe — infra
description:
## Why

Split from tc-gpof by autopilot. This is the /infra slice: grant the worker's managed identity the least-privilege role needed to read Service Bus queue metrics (activeMessageCount + scheduledMessageCount) via the management API.

The sibling /api bead replaces the bootstrap PeekLock probe with a management-plane HTTP GET on the queue resource. The worker currently holds `Azure Service Bus Data Owner` (data-plane only) — that role can't read management-plane metrics.

## What changes (in /infra only)

- Add an Azure RBAC assignment on the worker's managed identity granting the least-privilege role required to call `GET https://management.azure.com/.../Microsoft.ServiceBus/namespaces/<ns>/queues/<queue>?api-version=...` and read `countDetails`.
- Target scope: scoped to the Service Bus queue (or namespace if queue-scoping is unsupported for this role).
- Candidate role: `Reader` on the namespace (or queue). Confirm via Azure docs — whichever role is the narrowest that returns `countDetails.activeMessageCount` + `countDetails.scheduledMessageCount`.
- Keep the existing `Azure Service Bus Data Owner` assignment — it's still needed for data-plane receive/publish.

## Acceptance
- New role assignment on worker managed identity, scoped as narrow as possible, added in Pulumi.
- `dotnet build` succeeds in /infra.
- Role name documented in the assignment resource (comment or variable name) so reviewers can verify least-privilege choice.

## Out of scope
- Deploying the change (CI/CD handles it).
- The /api code that calls the management API (sibling bead).
acceptance_criteria: New RBAC assignment in Pulumi grants worker managed identity management-plane Read access to the poll-triggers queue (or namespace). Least-privilege role chosen. dotnet build passes.
notes:
Split from tc-gpof by autopilot. Worker: pulumi-infra-worker. Allowed path: infra/.
Split sibling of tc-ujl1. Parent: tc-gpof.

title: Switch poll-triggers queue to receive-and-delete + management-API probe — API
description:
## Why

Split from tc-gpof by autopilot. This is the /api slice: flip the orchestrator to receive-and-delete + publish-after-consume ordering, replace the bootstrap PeekLock probe with a management-API call that reads activeMessageCount + scheduledMessageCount.

Root cause (diagnosed in tc-ku5u, epic tc-ofxd): current Publish → Complete ordering produces duplicates when Complete fails (lock expires, message redelivers, new replica publishes again). Over ~1h post-deploy the poll-triggers queue accumulated 14 active messages, hammering PlanIt at KEDA's 30s polling cadence regardless of Retry-After.

## What changes (in /api only)

### Orchestrator: receive-and-delete + publish-after-consume
- Change `ServiceBusRestClient.ReceiveOneAsync` and its callers (via `IPollTriggerQueue.ReceiveAsync`) to use Service Bus receive-and-delete mode. Destructive consume on receive — no lock, no Complete, no Abandon.
- Flip `PollTriggerOrchestrator.RunOnceAsync` ordering: run the handler first, then publish the next trigger. If anything fails between receive and publish, chain pauses until safety-net bootstrap recovers.
- Delete `IPollTriggerQueue.CompleteAsync` and `AbandonAsync` (no callers remain after the flip). Simplify `ServiceBusPollTriggerQueue` — no more `LockUrl` threading on received messages.
- Remove the LeaseHeld-Abandon branch in `PollTriggerOrchestrator`. If another replica holds the lease, just exit — the message is already gone, and the lease holder is responsible for publishing the next trigger.
- Drop `POLLING_HANDLER_BUDGET_SECONDS` (currently 240s to stay inside the 5-min Basic-tier LockDuration cap). Receive-and-delete has no lock, so the only budget now is `replicaTimeout` (600s).

### Safety-net probe via management API
- `PollTriggerBootstrapper.TryBootstrapAsync` currently probes by calling `ReceiveAsync` (destructive under receive-and-delete — would delete a message every 30min just to check).
- Replace with a management-API call that reads `countDetails.activeMessageCount` and `countDetails.scheduledMessageCount` on the queue. If both are 0, publish a seed. Otherwise skip.
- Also fixes a pre-existing blind spot: current PeekLock probe can't see scheduled (future-dated) messages, so the bootstrapper sometimes double-publishes when a healthy chain has a scheduled message pending. Management API returns both counts.

### Tests
- `PollTriggerOrchestratorTests` currently assert `Publish` is called before `Complete`; invert to assert message is received-and-deleted, handler runs, then `Publish` is called. LeaseHeld path no longer asserts `Abandon`.
- `PollTriggerBootstrapperTests` swap fake queue probe for fake metrics provider returning (active, scheduled) tuples. Cover: both zero → publish; active>0 → skip; scheduled>0 → skip; both>0 → skip.
- `ServiceBusRestClientTests` remove Complete/Abandon tests; add receive-and-delete mode assertion (DELETE verb with correct URL).

## Acceptance
- No Complete/Abandon code in `IPollTriggerQueue` or its adapters
- Bootstrapper uses management-API probe (HTTP GET to queue resource), not ReceiveAsync
- All polling tests pass; orchestrator + bootstrapper + REST client tests updated for new ordering
- POLLING_HANDLER_BUDGET_SECONDS configuration removed

## Depends on
Sibling infra bead (RBAC assignment) — merged/deployed first so runtime 401/403 don't hit the bootstrapper.
acceptance_criteria: Queue adapter uses receive-and-delete; orchestrator publishes after handler; bootstrapper probes via management API; POLLING_HANDLER_BUDGET_SECONDS removed; tests updated and green.
notes:
Split from tc-gpof by autopilot. Worker: dotnet-tdd-worker. Allowed path: api/.
Depends on tc-gexj because: the new management-API probe in /api will fail with 401/403 at runtime until the worker managed identity has the management-plane Read role assigned — infra must deploy first.
Autopilot: failed 2026-04-22. Reason: dotnet-tdd-worker only completed the red test phase (commit e96be5e on autopilot/tc-ujl1: bootstrapper management-API probe tests). Returned with 'Need to link the new FakePollTriggerQueueMetrics into the integration-tests project' — implementation (IPollTriggerQueueMetrics adapter, orchestrator flip to receive-and-delete + publish-after-consume, ServiceBusRestClient receive-and-delete mode, deletion of CompleteAsync/AbandonAsync, removal of POLLING_HANDLER_BUDGET_SECONDS) not done. Green and refactor phases also missing. Branch autopilot/tc-ujl1 preserves the red tests for resumption.
Resume on existing branch autopilot/tc-ujl1 at commit e96be5e — red tests + IPollTriggerQueueMetrics interface already landed. Do NOT restart from main.

Still to do:
- Management-API adapter for IPollTriggerQueueMetrics (HTTP GET countDetails)
- Wire adapter into PollTriggerBootstrapper (replace ReceiveAsync probe)
- Flip ServiceBusRestClient.ReceiveOneAsync to receive-and-delete mode
- Flip PollTriggerOrchestrator.RunOnceAsync ordering (handler first, publish after)
- Delete IPollTriggerQueue.CompleteAsync/AbandonAsync + adapter impls
- Update PollTriggerOrchestratorTests + ServiceBusRestClientTests
- Remove POLLING_HANDLER_BUDGET_SECONDS wiring
- Fix integration-tests project reference so FakePollTriggerQueueMetrics resolves

title: Switch poll-triggers queue to receive-and-delete + management-API safety-net probe
description:
## Why

Diagnosed in SRE observatory run 2026-04-22 (epic tc-ofxd, bead tc-ku5u): the poll-triggers Service Bus queue accumulated 14 active messages in ~1h post-deploy, causing PlanIt to be hammered at KEDA's 30s polling cadence regardless of Retry-After. Root cause: the current `Publish → Complete` ordering produces duplicates when Complete fails (lock expires, message redelivers, new replica publishes again). Over time the backlog compounds.

## What changes

### 1. Orchestrator: receive-and-delete + publish-after-consume

- Change `ServiceBusRestClient.ReceiveOneAsync` and its callers (via `IPollTriggerQueue.ReceiveAsync`) to use Service Bus receive-and-delete mode. The message is destructively consumed on receive — no lock, no Complete, no Abandon.
- Flip `PollTriggerOrchestrator.RunOnceAsync` ordering: run the handler first, then publish the next trigger. If anything fails between receive and publish, the chain pauses until the safety-net bootstrap recovers.
- Delete `IPollTriggerQueue.CompleteAsync` and `AbandonAsync` (no callers remain). Simplify `ServiceBusPollTriggerQueue` accordingly — no more `LockUrl` threading on received messages.
- Remove the LeaseHeld-Abandon branch in `PollTriggerOrchestrator`. If another replica holds the lease, just exit — the message is already gone, and the lease holder is responsible for publishing the next trigger.
- Can drop `POLLING_HANDLER_BUDGET_SECONDS` (currently 240s to stay inside the 5-min Basic-tier LockDuration cap). Receive-and-delete has no lock, so the only budget now is `replicaTimeout` (600s). Nice-to-have cleanup in the same PR.

### 2. Safety-net probe via management API

- `PollTriggerBootstrapper.TryBootstrapAsync` currently probes by calling `ReceiveAsync` (destructive under receive-and-delete — would delete a message every 30min just to check).
- Replace with a management-API call that reads `countDetails.activeMessageCount` and `countDetails.scheduledMessageCount` on the queue. If both are 0, publish a seed. Otherwise skip.
- This also fixes a pre-existing blind spot: the current PeekLock probe can't see scheduled (future-dated) messages, so the bootstrapper sometimes double-publishes when a healthy chain has a scheduled message pending. Management API returns both counts.
- Requires an additional RBAC assignment on the worker identity: `Azure Service Bus Data Owner` is data-plane; this probe needs a management-plane read. Likely `Reader` role on the namespace (or scoped to the queue). Confirm the least-privilege role in the Pulumi change.

### 3. Tests

- `PollTriggerOrchestratorTests` currently assert `Publish` is called before `Complete`; invert to assert the message is received-and-deleted, handler runs, then `Publish` is called. LeaseHeld path no longer asserts `Abandon`.
- `PollTriggerBootstrapperTests` swap the fake queue probe for a fake metrics provider that returns `(active, scheduled)` tuples. Cover: both zero → publish; active>0 → skip; scheduled>0 → skip; both>0 → skip.
- `ServiceBusRestClientTests` remove Complete/Abandon tests; add receive-and-delete mode assertion (DELETE verb with correct URL).

## Acceptance

- [ ] poll-triggers queue active count stays bounded under sustained PlanIt 429s (empirical check in prod: active should not exceed 1-2)
- [ ] No Complete/Abandon code in `IPollTriggerQueue` or its adapters
- [ ] Management-API probe works with the worker's managed identity (no SAS keys)
- [ ] ADR 0024 amendment in place documenting the receive-mode flip (being added now separately)
- [ ] All polling tests pass; contract tests updated for the new ordering

## Out of scope

- Observability additions (logging Retry-After values, logging queue depth per cycle) — separate lower-priority bead
- Upgrade to Service Bus Standard tier — considered and rejected for now; receive-and-delete makes Basic viable
notes: Also superseded by: tc-gexj (infra sibling of tc-ujl1). Split by autopilot 2026-04-22.

title: CD Dev broken: deploy-worker-jobs iterates poll jobs that don't exist in dev
description:
ADR 0024 removed job-tc-poll-dev and dev never had poll-bootstrap, but .github/actions/deploy-worker-jobs/action.yml still iterates 'poll poll-bootstrap digest digest-hourly dormant-cleanup' unconditionally. Every CD Dev run since PR #280 (2026-04-22 06:19 UTC) has failed on 'az containerapp job update --name job-tc-poll-dev' (exit 3). As a result, the 3 dev jobs that do exist (digest, digest-hourly, dormant-cleanup) are stuck on image f9009a7 (pre-v0.9.5), missing 8 commits including the soft handler budget, 429 PlanIt handling, SB-only polling, and fail-fast WORKER_MODE.

Fix: make the job list per-env. Add a 'jobs' input to the composite action; have CD Dev pass only the 3 it has, CD Production pass all 5. Mirrors the infra split and keeps dev from trying to touch prod-only resources.

Acceptance:
- CD Dev run succeeds end-to-end after the fix merges
- Dev digest/digest-hourly/dormant-cleanup jobs pick up the v0.9.9 image
- Prod deploys unchanged
close_reason: PR #287 merged — per-env worker job list input unblocks CD Dev

title: [SRE] poll-triggers queue accumulated 14 active messages — Retry-After bypass
description:
## Root cause

The `poll` Service Bus queue accumulated **14 active messages** at one point during this observation window (snapshot taken ~17:10 UTC). With KEDA polling every 30s and MaxExecutions=1, each poll spawns a container that immediately consumes one of the backlog messages — **bypassing the Retry-After scheduled-delivery path entirely**.

## Why this masqueraded as a Retry-After failure

The correctly-scheduled messages (the output of `PollTriggerOrchestrator.PublishAtAsync(nextRun)`) sit in SB's scheduled state until their enqueue time. Meanwhile, KEDA is picking off the already-active backlog every 30s:

1. Container spawns, makes one PlanIt call → 429 (because we're deep in PlanIt's rate window)
2. Publishes next message for T+retryAfter (added to scheduled queue, sits waiting)
3. Completes current message
4. KEDA polls again 30s later, sees 13 remaining active messages → spawns next container

That's why we observed the 30s / 60s dominant pattern and thought Retry-After wasn't working.

## Proof the Retry-After chain IS correct

The 429 gap distribution shows:
- Dominant: 30-60s (draining the active backlog)
- **Three long gaps**: 420s, 750s, 331s

The long gaps can ONLY happen when the active queue drops to 0 and we wait for a scheduled message to activate. 750s ≈ 12.5min → a long retryAfter value being honored. 331s ≈ 5.5min → RateLimitDefault (5min) + jitter. These prove the parser + scheduler + ScheduledEnqueueTimeUtc path all work.

## Post-mitigation confirmation

User cleared the queue and stopped the safety-net seed job. Two consecutive queue checks 60s apart showed:
- Active: 0
- Scheduled: 3

This is the expected healthy state — a handful of future-dated messages waiting their turn, zero active backlog. **The system will now honor Retry-After as designed.**

## Where did the 14 active come from? (investigation needed)

Candidates, not yet confirmed:
1. **Deploy-time multi-seed**: the new deploy's startup may have interacted with the safety-net cron and the orchestrator chain to leave multiple active messages (initial bootstrap + first orchestrator publish + something else)
2. **Lock expiry redeliveries**: if containers are being killed before completing the message (SIGTERM / replicaTimeout / SB 5-min lock cap), the message redelivers while a DIFFERENT message was already published for the next run → net +1 per occurrence. The existing `POLLING_HANDLER_BUDGET_SECONDS=240` mitigation addresses the 5-min cap but may not cover all kill paths.
3. **Safety-net probe false-negative**: if the `poll-bootstrap` cron's PeekLock probe returned null for any reason (short timeout, transient failure) while active messages existed, it would incorrectly publish a bootstrap — adding an extra message.

## Suggested remediation (for a future bead)

1. **Log queue depth metric** — emit `activeMessageCount` and `scheduledMessageCount` on every orchestrator cycle so drift is observable from telemetry.
2. **Log the parsed Retry-After value** in `LogRateLimitStop` (still needed, but lower priority now).
3. **Guard against active-queue growth** — orchestrator should log a warning if it sees > 1 active message when it starts (indicating the chain has duplicated somewhere).
4. **Review the safety-net publish condition** — ensure it doesn't double-publish when a scheduled message exists (see existing TOCTOU comment in `PollTriggerBootstrapper.cs`).
5. **Investigate recent container kills / SIGTERM events** to see if lock-expiry redelivery is a recurring contributor.

## Impact

Background ingestion only — no user-facing outage. But we hammered PlanIt with ~30 zero-work requests over 50 minutes. User manually stopped the seed job to avoid further hammering.
notes: Autopilot 2026-04-22: not independently dispatchable — this is the diagnostic write-up; the permanent remediation (items 3 & 4) is in tc-ujl1 (and prereq tc-gexj, closed). Blocking on tc-ujl1 so it's revisited after the fix lands for empirical confirmation of bounded queue depth.

title: [SRE] Observatory run 2026-04-22
description:
SRE telemetry review. Window: since last prod deploy at 2026-04-22T16:08:29Z. Health: **backlog mitigated by manual queue clear (user)**. Findings: 1.

## Findings (1)

- tc-ku5u [P1] poll-triggers queue accumulated 14 active messages — Retry-After bypass

## Corrected diagnosis

Initial telemetry suggested Retry-After wasn't being honored. False — parser and scheduler are correct (proven by 420s, 750s, and 331s gaps in the 429 series matching long retryAfter honoring). The real root cause is that the poll SB queue accumulated 14 active messages at one point, and KEDA drained them at its 30s polling cadence, burning a PlanIt call per drain regardless of what Retry-After said.

## Evidence

- Queue snapshot at investigation time: activeMessageCount=14, scheduledMessageCount=2
- After user cleared queue + stopped safety-net seed job: active=0, scheduled=3, steady over two polls 60s apart
- 50+ minute stretch with 30+ zero-ingest cycles correlates with draining the active backlog
- PlanIt hammered with ~30 no-op calls during the backlog drain

## Remaining question

Where did the 14 active come from? Candidates (not confirmed): deploy-time multi-seed, lock-expiry redeliveries, safety-net false-negative probe. See child bead for investigation plan.
