title: Poll job hits PlanIt 429 then SB lock expires, causing Failed execution
description:
## Context

First surfaced 2026-04-22 after v0.9.7 (tc-5s1f) restored the SB-coordinated polling cycle in prod. Observed during verification of parent tc-q8t5.

At 08:19:22 UTC, job-tc-poll-prod-tcb2c was triggered by KEDA, received the poll message, and Failed ~6.5 min later. Surrounding Succeeded runs (08:06, 08:12, 08:26) completed in ~1 min processing 587–640 applications from 9 authorities. The Failed run differed.

App Insights exceptions for operation_Id b22bd1c5e2a86ac403579aca70e18ccf:
- 08:25:54Z TownCrier.Application.PlanIt.PlanItRateLimitException: 'PlanIt API returned 429 Too Many Requests.'
- 08:25:55Z (x2) System.Net.Http.HttpRequestException: 'Service Bus Complete failed (404): The lock supplied is invalid. Either the lock expired, or the message has already been removed from the queue.' (TrackingId 8303fbf6-053d-4dd6-8d1f-831b4df2e656)

## Root cause

Queue LockDuration is 5 min (raised from default in tc-q8t5 spec, currently set to PT5M in the Pulumi queue definition — actual queue shows 5min; spec said 10min, infra ended up at 5 — separate hygiene item).

When the poll cycle hits PlanIt 429 and retries, the whole operation exceeds 5 min. Message lock expires. Complete() returns 404. The execution then exits with non-zero status → Failed. SB redelivers the message on next trigger, so the cycle recovers, but each 429 burst produces a Failed execution in the dashboard and an extra cycle of work.

## Fix options

1. Renew the message lock inside the handler during long-running polls (ServiceBusReceiver.RenewMessageLockAsync).
2. Raise LockDuration to the spec'd 10 min (PT10M) in ServiceBusQueue Pulumi resource.
3. Exit handler before Complete() if the request has exceeded safe duration — abandon/defer instead.
4. Back off PlanIt 429s more aggressively and bail early — publish next-run and return Succeeded even if partial.

Prefer 1+2 together: 10m lock with periodic renewal gives ~unlimited headroom while PlanIt rate-limits us.

## Acceptance

- LockDuration raised to 10m (align with tc-q8t5 spec).
- Handler renews lock periodically OR bails gracefully on 429 burst.
- No Failed poll-prod executions over a 24h window that are caused by lock-expiry-on-Complete.
- PlanIt 429 handling has a TUnit test covering the long-running poll path.

## Parent

Surfaced while verifying parent tc-q8t5 acceptance.
notes: Autopilot 2026-04-22: pulumi-infra-worker escalated. Raising LockDuration to 10m is not possible on Azure Service Bus (platform-wide 5m cap on all tiers, not tier-specific). Previously broke CD Production on v0.9.1 (tc-q2ts, PR #277, MessagingGatewayBadRequest). Handler-side mitigations (soft budget, drop 429 from retry) already landed via tc-267s/tc-5ryg/tc-rz3n, which likely address the 429-burst failure class without any LockDuration change. Recommended resolution: close with note that LockDuration criterion is obsolete (spec error in tc-q8t5), verify in prod whether handler soft-budget fix eliminated the original Failed executions over 24h. Needs human decision.
close_reason: Handler soft budget (4m) + ASB hard lock (5m) is the agreed fix. LockDuration cannot be raised — Azure Service Bus caps at 5m across all tiers. Handler-side mitigations (soft budget, drop 429 from retry, wire POLLING_HANDLER_BUDGET_SECONDS) shipped via tc-267s/tc-5ryg/tc-rz3n.

title: Pulumi/CD: safety-net job image stuck on quickstart placeholder — bootstrap never runs
description:
## Context

CD Production has run on v0.9.1, v0.9.2, v0.9.3, v0.9.4 without error. Verified 2026-04-21 22:00 UTC that:

- `job-tc-poll-prod`: image `acrtowncriershared.azurecr.io/town-crier-worker:eb7b460...` ✓
- `job-tc-poll-safety-net-prod`: image `mcr.microsoft.com/k8se/quickstart:latest` ❌

The safety-net job was created with the Pulumi default quickstart placeholder and has never been updated to the real worker image. Every scheduled execution (last seen 2026-04-21T21:30, 22:00) fails instantly because the image has no entrypoint that understands the polling workload.

This is why the tc-tdgf bootstrap reseed never fires in prod: the safety-net's host container crashes before any app code can run.

## Impact

- Service Bus-coordinated polling cycle is dormant indefinitely. Queue stays empty → KEDA never scales `job-tc-poll-prod` → no polling happens via SB.
- Safety-net failing also means no timer-driven polling happens via its own path.
- **As of 2026-04-21 22:00 UTC: no PlanIt polling is running in prod.** The last successful poll job was at 21:00 UTC (old Schedule-trigger executions before the v0.9.1 trigger swap).

## Root cause hypothesis

The CD `Deploy Worker` step (`.github/actions/deploy-worker-jobs`) likely runs `az containerapp job update --image <tag>` only for `job-tc-poll-prod` and does not touch `job-tc-poll-safety-net-prod`. Meanwhile the Pulumi definition for the safety-net job either doesn't set an image or sets a placeholder that never matches anything.

## What to change

Two candidate fixes — investigate both files to decide:

1. `.github/actions/deploy-worker-jobs/action.yml` (or `/action.yaml`) — add a second `az containerapp job update` for `job-tc-poll-safety-net-<env>` using the same image tag.
2. `infra/EnvironmentStack.cs` near the safety-net job (`CreateWorkerJob` call with `workerMode: null`) — verify it wires the same image-tag output as the main job. If it uses a placeholder, fix it to reference the real image output.

## Acceptance

- `az containerapp job show -n job-tc-poll-safety-net-prod -g rg-town-crier-prod --query 'properties.template.containers[0].image'` returns `acrtowncriershared.azurecr.io/town-crier-worker:<sha>` (same as the main poll job).
- Next safety-net scheduled execution status is `Succeeded`.
- Within 30 min of the fix deploying: queue `poll` has `activeMessageCount > 0` or `scheduledMessageCount > 0` (bootstrap published).
- `job-tc-poll-prod` has new Event-triggered executions (KEDA scaled up).

## Priority

**P1** — prod polling is down. The worker image is shipped, the SB infra is provisioned, but the safety-net is the only thing that can seed the cycle and it's broken.

## Related

- tc-q8t5 (prod apply — won't be acceptable until this ships).
- tc-tdgf (safety-net reseed — landed in v0.9.4 but never executes).

title: [SRE] Hourly digest and dormant-cleanup jobs running quickstart placeholder in prod
description:
**Observed:** Two Container App Jobs in rg-town-crier-prod are running `mcr.microsoft.com/k8se/quickstart:latest` instead of the real `town-crier-worker` image:

- `job-tc-digest-hourly-prod` (cron `0 * * * *`) — intended to run WORKER_MODE=hourly-digest
- `job-tc-dormant-cleanup-prod` (cron `30 3 * * *`) — intended to run UK GDPR Art.5(1)(e) erasure cascade for dormant UserProfiles

Same two jobs in dev are also on the placeholder.

**Root cause:** `.github/actions/deploy-worker-jobs/action.yml` only iterates over two of the four worker jobs:
```bash
for JOB in poll digest; do
  az containerapp job update --name "job-tc-${JOB}-${env-suffix}" --image ...
done
```
Pulumi (`infra/EnvironmentStack.cs:336-358`) creates four jobs (poll, digest, digest-hourly, dormant-cleanup) each seeded with the quickstart placeholder, expecting CD to update the image. CD does this for poll+digest only, so digest-hourly and dormant-cleanup have never received a real image.

**Blast radius:**
- Users receive no hourly push notifications / digests — core feature broken
- Dormant accounts are not being purged — GDPR Art.5(1)(e) compliance gap (see ADR on dormant cleanup)

**Evidence:**
```
az containerapp job list --resource-group rg-town-crier-prod
# job-tc-poll-prod            : acrtowncriershared.azurecr.io/town-crier-worker:4af243c...  ✅
# job-tc-digest-prod          : acrtowncriershared.azurecr.io/town-crier-worker:4af243c...  ✅
# job-tc-digest-hourly-prod   : mcr.microsoft.com/k8se/quickstart:latest                   🚨
# job-tc-dormant-cleanup-prod : mcr.microsoft.com/k8se/quickstart:latest                   🚨
```

**Suggested fix:** Extend the loop in `deploy-worker-jobs/action.yml` to `for JOB in poll digest digest-hourly dormant-cleanup`. Backfill: manually run `az containerapp job update` against the four prod+dev jobs before the next scheduled run, or wait for the next CD-prod pipeline.

**Verification after fix:** confirm `towncrier.notifications.*` metrics appear in customMetrics after an hourly-digest run and `towncrier.dormant.cleanup.*` metrics after the 03:30 UTC run.

title: [SRE] Observatory run 2026-04-21
description:
SRE telemetry review. Window: since last prod deploy at 2026-04-20T23:18:06Z (~7h). Health: DEGRADED. Findings: 2.

Top risks:
1. P1: Two scheduled jobs in prod running placeholder quickstart image (digest-hourly + dormant-cleanup)
2. P2: PlanIt API 21.54% failure rate (429 rate limiting)

Context (not filed):
- API Container App had zero HTTP requests in 30 days — consistent with pre-launch state (min=0 replicas, scales to zero). No action needed unless user traffic is expected.
- CLR exception counter non-zero but explained by the 6 exceptions (HttpRequestException 429, TaskCanceled) captured in the exceptions table — OTel pipeline is healthy.
- Cosmos DB healthy: 0 failures, 28ms avg, 46ms p99, RU within limits.
- Worker runtime healthy: CPU <1%, memory 45-70 MB, polling cycles completing in 37-46s (except one that retried PlanIt for 9.5 min).
notes: Children: tc-sdi4 (P1 placeholder jobs), tc-v7xe (P2 PlanIt 429s).

title: Scope Applications dedupe query to authority partition
description: Spec: CosmosPlanningApplicationRepository.GetByUidAsync uses partitionKey: null, causing a cross-partition fan-out on every PlanIt application pulled during poll cycles. Since PR #259 (alternating seed cycles) + #260 (replicaTimeout 120->600s) shipped on 2026-04-19, sustained Cosmos RU burn jumped from ~28K/hour to ~1.3M/hour. Applications container is 99.8% of the burn (13.2M RU in 10h on Apr 20). Fix: pass authorityCode as partitionKey (already known at the call site in PollPlanItCommandHandler's authority loop). Even better, switch dedupe to a point read by Id=application.Name + partitionKey=authorityCode for 1 RU per call instead of ~5-10. Expected savings: 60-80% of Applications RU burn, ~£130/month -> ~£30-50/month.

title: Handler tests for cursor lifecycle across pagination scenarios
description: Spec: docs/specs/polling-resumable-cursor.md#test-strategy — Add 10 new handler tests covering: start at page 1 (no cursor); resume at cursor page (same date); ignore stale cursor; save cursor on cap; clear cursor on natural end; save cursor on mid-page 429; resume spike across 3 cycles ending naturally; emit authority_total; emit cursor_advanced; emit cursor_cleared. Use updated FakePlanItClient + FakePollStateStore.
notes: Autopilot 2026-04-20: worker hit Anthropic API rate limit (resets 9pm Europe/London) before any work committed. Requeued for later retry.

title: Rewire PollPlanItCommandHandler to drive pages + cursor lifecycle
description: Spec: docs/specs/polling-resumable-cursor.md#handler-flow--pollplanitcommandhandlerhandleasync — Handler loops pages itself using FetchApplicationsPageAsync. Resume from cursor when CursorDifferentStart matches lastPollTime.Date (overlap by -1 page). On natural end: clear cursor + advance HWM. On cap or 429 mid-pagination: save cursor + freeze HWM. Preserve existing reindex-flood skip and partial-progress semantics.

title: Replace IPollStateStore methods with combined GetAsync/SaveAsync shape
description: Spec: docs/specs/polling-resumable-cursor.md#port-change--ipollstatestore — Replace GetLastPollTimeAsync/SaveLastPollTimeAsync with GetAsync returning PollState and SaveAsync taking (lastPollTime, PollCursor?). Update CosmosPollStateStore + round-trip tests (with and without cursor). Update FakePollStateStore. GetLeastRecentlyPolledAsync unchanged.

title: Replace IPlanItClient streaming method with page-level method
description: Spec: docs/specs/polling-resumable-cursor.md#port-change--iplanitclient — Replace FetchApplicationsAsync with FetchApplicationsPageAsync returning FetchPageResult. Update PlanItClient implementation (HasMorePages = Applications.Count >= DefaultPageSize). Update FakePlanItClient. Update existing PlanItClientTests to cover HasMorePages semantics. SearchApplicationsAsync unchanged.

title: Introduce PollState + PollCursor + FetchPageResult records
description: Spec: docs/specs/polling-resumable-cursor.md#port-change--ipollstatestore — Add PollState, PollCursor, FetchPageResult to application layer. No consumers yet; sets up the type system for the new port shapes.

title: Extend PollStateDocument with cursor fields + serializer context
description: Spec: docs/specs/polling-resumable-cursor.md#schema-change--extend-pollstatedocument — Add nullable CursorDifferentStart, CursorNextPage, CursorKnownTotal to PollStateDocument. Update CosmosJsonSerializerContext. No behaviour change; backward-compatible for existing docs.

title: Resumable pagination cursor for PlanIt polling
description: Spec: docs/specs/polling-resumable-cursor.md — Extend poll state with a page-level cursor so same-date maintenance spikes (e.g. PlanIt bulk last_different bumps) clear automatically instead of stalling the HWM. Also handles watch-zone authorities with genuine spikes. See ADR 0021.
close_reason: All child tasks complete: resumable pagination cursor shipped end-to-end (ADR 0021, spec docs/specs/polling-resumable-cursor.md).

title: [legal audit] Delete Auth0 user on account deletion
description: Privacy policy says self-service deletion removes data 'from our systems'. Auth0ManagementClient currently exposes only UpdateSubscriptionTierAsync — there is no DELETE /api/v2/users/{id} call. Add DeleteUserAsync to IAuth0ManagementClient + Auth0ManagementClient and invoke from DeleteUserProfileCommandHandler so the Auth0 identity is removed alongside Cosmos data. UK GDPR Article 17 (right to erasure). Found by /legal audit 2026-04-20.

title: [legal audit] Cascade account deletion to child records
description: Privacy policy now promises that account deletion 'removes your profile, preferences, watch zones, notifications, saved applications, and device registrations from our systems'. Currently DeleteUserProfileCommandHandler only deletes the UserProfile document; notifications, decision alerts, watch zones, saved applications, device registrations become orphaned by userId. Implement cascade so /v1/me DELETE removes all child records for the user. Found by /legal audit 2026-04-20.

title: Cap pages per authority per seed-poll cycle to bound budget
description:
Seed cycles on backlogged authorities currently thrash: PlanIt 429s mid-pagination, partial HWM saved, next cycle re-fetches the same same-day pages (because `different_start` is date-truncated per PlanIt docs — verified 2026-04-20), burns the rate budget before advancing. Net effect: only ~1 authority polled per seed cycle (vs designed ~8), 100% rate_limited termination, full rotation would take ~9 days not 26h.

## Design

Cap the per-authority pagination in `PlanItClient.FetchApplicationsAsync` at N pages per call (proposal: **N=3 → 300 apps max per authority per cycle**).

- Add `int? maxPages` param (nullable so existing `SearchApplicationsAsync` callers and tests aren't forced to specify).
- Exit the `do...while` pagination loop when `page > maxPages`, regardless of whether PlanIt signalled end-of-data.
- Handler sets `completedSuccessfully = true` on natural loop exit (existing behaviour). Page-capped exit looks the same to the handler.
- HWM already advances via `highWaterMark = max(LastDifferent)` from streamed apps (PollPlanItCommandHandler.cs:90-93, saved at line 148).

## Why this works

- **Deterministic breadth**: each seed cycle processes min(remaining_budget / per_authority_time, N_authorities). Backlog authorities can't eat the whole cycle.
- **Forward progress guaranteed**: bailing at page 3 (intentionally) saves HWM from app #300. Next rotation asks PlanIt for `different_start = date_of_app_300`, skips fully-processed days. Re-fetch overlap limited to same-day apps (dedupe via `HasSameBusinessFieldsAs` handles writes; read cost bounded).
- **Backlog drains over rotations**: 1,200-app authority drains in 4 rotations (~4-5 days at 26h/rotation). Matches seed-polling spec: 'catalogue deepens naturally over subsequent daily rotations'.
- **No PlanIt policy change needed**: we're reducing our demand, not negotiating.

## Sites to touch

- `api/src/town-crier.infrastructure/PlanIt/PlanItClient.cs` — add `maxPages` to `IPlanItClient.FetchApplicationsAsync` signature + implementation.
- `api/src/town-crier.application/PlanIt/IPlanItClient.cs` — update interface.
- `api/src/town-crier.application/Polling/PollPlanItCommandHandler.cs:86` — pass the configured cap.
- Config: new `Polling:MaxPagesPerAuthorityPerCycle` setting, wired via `town-crier.worker/Program.cs`. Default 3.
- Tests: TUnit cases for (a) cap hit mid-pagination (returns partial, no error), (b) cap not hit (natural exit unchanged), (c) null cap = unbounded (regression guard for Search path).

## Acceptance

- New config value defaults to 3 in prod appsettings.
- Unit test: 5-page fake PlanIt response + cap=3 yields exactly 300 records, HWM = LastDifferent of 300th app.
- Post-deploy telemetry (24h):
  - Seed cycles: termination shifts from 100% `rate_limited` → majority `natural`
  - `authorities_polled` per seed cycle ≥ 5 (up from 1)
  - PlanIt 429 rate drops

## Follow-up (out of scope)

- Fail-fast on 429 inside `PlanItClient.SendWithThrottleAsync` (plan B) — less urgent once C is in place, but still a resilience win.
- Consider per-authority time budget in addition to page cap.

title: Shrink seed-cycle first-touch backfill window from 24h to 1h
description:
Seed cycles are hitting 600s replica timeout after polling only ONE authority per 30-min window (vs designed ~8/cycle).

Telemetry (2026-04-20, ~4h post-deploy of #259-#261):
- Watched cycles: 100% natural, 8 authorities/cycle, ~33s per authority
- Seed cycles: 100% rate_limited, 1 authority/cycle, 242s per authority, FAILED with 429
- Apps ingested per seed authority: ~1,200 (first-touch backfill burst)
- 8× HTTP 429 from PlanIt in 12h, all in seed windows

Root cause: api/src/town-crier.application/Polling/PollPlanItCommandHandler.cs:84 — `lastPollTime ??= now.AddDays(-1)` gives first-touch authorities a 24h backfill window. PlanIt returns ~1,200 apps, Polly retries chew through the cycle budget on 429s, only 1 authority completes before timeout.

At 1 authority per 30-min seed window, full 417-authority rotation takes ~9 days instead of the designed ~26 hours.

Fix: change line 84 to `lastPollTime ??= now.AddHours(-1)`. Matches the 15-min cadence more realistically; catalogue deepens over subsequent rotations (acceptable per seed-polling spec: 'paying users expected no sooner than ~1 month out').

Spec: docs/specs/seed-polling.md (update the 'First-sync window' section from 24h → 1h).

Acceptance:
- Line 84 uses AddHours(-1)
- Spec updated
- No existing tests assert the 24h default (grep confirmed), so no test breakage expected
- Verify post-deploy: seed cycles terminate 'natural' and polled-authorities-per-cycle > 1
close_reason: Verified via PlanIt API docs (https://www.planit.org.uk/api): 'all dates in YYYY-MM-DD or DD/MM/YYYY format'. The differentStart param in PlanItClient.cs:108 is already formatted yyyy-MM-dd — changing the caller from AddDays(-1) to AddHours(-1) has no effect on the wire. Need a different strategy (cap apps/authority per cycle, short-circuit 429s, or page-limit per authority).

title: Remove Application Insights JS from web frontend
description: The web frontend initialises @microsoft/applicationinsights-web in src/telemetry.ts with enableAutoRouteTracking=true. Every page load sets telemetry identifiers (ai_user, ai_session) and sends per-user telemetry to Azure Monitor. Under PECR reg 6 this counts as non-essential first-party analytics and requires consent. Backend (server-side) App Insights is unaffected and remains in place on a legitimate-interests basis. Decision: remove web-side App Insights entirely. For a pre-live app the client-side telemetry provides near-zero value, and when real users arrive a minimal consent-free error reporter is a cleaner rebuild than trying to gate the current setup behind a banner. Found by /legal audit on 2026-04-19.
design: Remove web-side Application Insights entirely. (1) Delete web/src/telemetry.ts and any imports of it. (2) Remove @microsoft/applicationinsights-web and @microsoft/applicationinsights-react-js (if present) from web/package.json dependencies, then re-lock. (3) Remove the telemetry provider from web/src/App.tsx and any usages (e.g., useAppInsightsContext, withAITracking HOCs, TrackEvent calls). (4) Remove the VITE_APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING env var from .env files, Static Web App app settings, and Pulumi config — confirm nothing else reads it. (5) Verify build passes with no remaining references. Server-side App Insights (api + worker) is unaffected — do not touch. Do NOT replace with a minimal-mode config; the decision is removal, not reduction. When real users arrive a consent-free error reporter (e.g., Sentry with no cookies/IP/userId, or a custom endpoint) can be scoped as a separate bead.

title: Self-host Google Fonts to remove third-party US transfer from web
description: The web frontend loads the Inter font from fonts.googleapis.com and fonts.gstatic.com (index.html lines 21-25). Every page load transfers the user's IP address to Google (US) without consent. Under PECR and UK GDPR Chapter V this is a non-essential third-party transfer. Self-hosting eliminates the transfer entirely and avoids needing a cookie banner for it. Found by /legal audit on 2026-04-19.
design: Download the Inter woff2 subsets we actually use, place under web/public/fonts/, and replace the <link> tags in web/index.html with a local @font-face declaration in the global stylesheet. Remove the preconnect hints to Google domains. Verify font fallback still works in dev and build. This removes the only third-party resource on the web and removes Google as a processor from the privacy-policy disclosure.

title: iOS delete account must call DELETE /v1/me before clearing Auth0 session
description: iOS SettingsViewModel.confirmDeleteAccount currently only clears the local Auth0 session and keychain. It does NOT call the server-side DELETE /v1/me endpoint. Users who tap 'Delete Account' leave their server-side data intact, which breaks UK GDPR Art. 17 erasure. Found by /legal audit on 2026-04-19.
design: File: mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-presentation/Sources/Features/Settings/SettingsViewModel.swift around lines 113-132. Sequence must be: (1) call APIUserProfileRepository.delete() — this already exists and hits DELETE /v1/me; (2) only on 204 success, call Auth0AuthenticationService.deleteAccount() to clear keychain + session; (3) on failure, surface the error and do NOT clear local credentials (user stays logged in and can retry). Depends on the server-side cascade — only fully meaningful once the account-deletion-cascade bead lands. Add a unit test that verifies repository.delete is called before auth.deleteAccount and that local credentials are NOT cleared on server error.

title: Cascade user deletion across all Cosmos containers and Auth0
description: DeleteUserProfileCommandHandler currently deletes only the Users document. It does not cascade to WatchZones, DeviceRegistrations, SavedApplications, DecisionAlerts, or OfferCodes.RedeemedByUserId, and does not delete the Auth0 identity. Under UK GDPR Art. 17 (right to erasure), deleting an account must remove all associated personal data. Found by /legal audit on 2026-04-19.
design: On DELETE /v1/me, the handler must: (1) delete all WatchZones for userId; (2) delete all DeviceRegistrations for userId; (3) delete all SavedApplications for userId; (4) delete all DecisionAlerts for userId; (5) let Notifications TTL out naturally; (6) for OfferCodes where RedeemedByUserId==userId, anonymise the field (set to null or a sentinel like 'deleted-user') rather than deleting the ledger entry — preserve billing audit trail; (7) call Auth0ManagementClient to delete the Auth0 user. Each step idempotent. userId is the partition key on user-scoped containers, so these are point-deletes or per-partition queries. Rollback is not possible once started — document as intentional. Integration test: create data in every user-scoped container, call DELETE /v1/me, assert all containers empty for that user and Auth0 user is deleted.
notes: Autopilot: failed 2026-04-19. Reason: worker ran out of budget mid-implementation. Committed OfferCode.AnonymiseRedemption domain method (commits 58aefc0, 7638d73) but cascade delete (WatchZone/Device/SavedApp/DecisionAlert repositories, Auth0ManagementClient.DeleteUserAsync, DeleteUserProfileCommandHandler wiring) is uncommitted. Needs human triage — consider splitting into smaller beads: (a) add idempotent Delete methods to each user-scoped repository, (b) add Auth0ManagementClient.DeleteUserAsync, (c) wire cascade + OfferCode.AnonymiseRedemption into DeleteUserProfileCommandHandler.
close_reason: Superseded by tc-j2fk (Auth0 delete) and tc-sy7c (cascade delete) from /legal audit 2026-04-20.

title: Move Azure Communication Services to UK data location
description: Azure Communication Services (CommunicationService + EmailService + Domain) is currently provisioned with DataLocation="Europe" in SharedStack.cs (lines 226-252). Email content and recipient addresses are stored in EEA data centres, not the UK. This is inconsistent with our stated data residency (UK South for everything else) and creates an unnecessary cross-border transfer disclosure for UK GDPR. Found by /legal audit on 2026-04-19.
design: Azure does NOT allow mutating DataLocation on an existing ACS resource — it is immutable after creation. Migration path: (1) Provision new CommunicationService, EmailService, and Domain resources in SharedStack.cs with DataLocation="UK" under a different resource name (e.g., acs-town-crier-uk). Keep Location="global" (ACS control plane is always global). (2) Re-verify the towncrierapp.uk domain on the new EmailService — Azure issues new DKIM/SPF/TXT records that must be added to Cloudflare DNS since DomainManagement=CustomerManaged. (3) Update the ACS connection string secret in Key Vault and any app config referencing it (AcsEmailSender in api/src/town-crier.infrastructure/Notifications/). (4) Redeploy API + worker via CI/CD so they pick up the new connection string. (5) Destroy the old acs-town-crier-shared, email-town-crier-shared, and the Europe-hosted Domain resources. Pre-live users means no migration window concerns.
notes: Files to change: infra/SharedStack.cs (ACS + EmailService + Domain blocks). Connection string secret in Key Vault. Pulumi.shared.yaml may need a config flag if we want a gradual switchover, but for pre-live state a single atomic change is fine. After landing, re-run /legal to remove EEA disclosure from the privacy policy.

title: Dedupe planning applications before Cosmos upsert in poll cycle
description:
Poll cycle currently upserts every record PlanIt returns, including bulk reindex floods where thousands of records are semantically unchanged. Observed prod: 1 authority (auth 494 = British Islands cross-border grouping) returned 7326 records for a 1-day different_start window — most were 2021-2024 applications whose last_different field was bumped en masse by a PlanIt rescrape. Every one triggered a Cosmos upsert + per-record FindZonesContainingAsync lookup (~30-50ms each), blowing the 120s poll replica timeout.

Fix: before upserting, compare incoming PlanningApplication against the stored Cosmos document. Skip UpsertAsync AND the zone-matching/notification fan-out when nothing material changed. Comparison should be over the business fields (address, description, app_state, app_type, decided_date, etc.) — NOT last_different/last_changed/last_scraped which are PlanIt bookkeeping.

Affects PollPlanItCommandHandler.cs per-record loop. Applies to BOTH watched and seed cycles — reindex pollution affects any authority once it has an active watch zone. This is the load-bearing fix; bd tc-ethl replica-timeout follow-up is only a safety net.

Acceptance: reindex floods are ingested in time proportional to materially-changed records, not total records returned. Unit test with a handler scenario where repository returns the same app twice — second call must skip upsert + zone lookup.

title: fix(worker): ship appsettings.json so HttpClient/Polly log suppression actually applies
description:
The worker project (api/src/town-crier.worker/town-crier.worker.csproj) uses Microsoft.NET.Sdk, which does NOT auto-copy appsettings.json to publish output. Dockerfile.worker only publishes the AOT binary, so the config never lands in the container. Host.CreateApplicationBuilder falls back to Default: Information for all categories — the System.Net.Http.HttpClient / Polly suppression from PR #237 is a no-op in prod.

Evidence from Log Analytics (last 24h, AppRoleName=town-crier-worker):
- System.Net.Http.HttpClient.CosmosRest.LogicalHandler: 164,288 Info traces
- System.Net.Http.HttpClient.CosmosRest.ClientHandler: 164,287 Info traces
- Polly: 82,141 Info traces
The same filter works in town-crier-web (Sdk.Web auto-copies appsettings.json) — zero HttpClient traces there.

Impact: ~410K redundant Info traces/day in AppTraces plus duplicate lines in ContainerAppConsoleLogs_CL. Post-PR-#237 ingestion settled at ~0.77 GB/day on Apr 17, still close to the 1 GB cap. Projected drop after fix: ~0.4–0.5 GB/day (to ~0.3 GB/day total).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Add <Content Include="appsettings*.json" CopyToOutputDirectory="PreserveNewest" /> to town-crier.worker.csproj
- [ ] Verify appsettings.json is present at /app in the published image (docker build + inspect, or local publish)
- [ ] Also add Polly: Warning to town-crier.web/appsettings.json (minor — 429/day today)
- [ ] After prod deploy, confirm in AppTraces that System.Net.Http.HttpClient.CosmosRest.* and Polly Info traces drop to ~0 for AppRoleName=town-crier-worker
- [ ] Confirm daily Usage (DataType=AppTraces + ContainerAppConsoleLogs_CL) drops meaningfully vs. the Apr 17 baseline
close_reason: Shipped via PR #251 (commit 56d4481). Verified post-deploy: zero System.Net.Http.HttpClient.CosmosRest.* or Polly Info traces for AppRoleName=town-crier-worker in the 15 min after deploy (was ~410K/day). Only Microsoft.Hosting.Lifetime and TownCrier.Worker remain — the intended survivors.

title: fix(ios): decode saved applications as bare array to match API contract
description: The iOS APISavedApplicationRepository expects a JSON envelope {"savedApplications": [...]} but the API (and web client) use a bare array [...]. Decoding fails silently, so the saved filter on the applications list and map screens shows no results. Fix iOS to decode directly into [SavedApplicationDTO], matching the API contract and the working web client. Files: mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-data/Sources/Repositories/APISavedApplicationRepository.swift and mobile/ios/town-crier-tests/Sources/Features/APISavedApplicationRepositoryTests.swift.
close_reason: iOS saved-applications repository now decodes the bare JSON array returned by the API (matching the working web client). Tests RED then GREEN; full suite (922 tests) and swiftlint pass.

title: Further reduce Azure telemetry costs
description: Switch ContainerAppConsoleLogs_CL to Basic tier, filter out successful Cosmos dependency spans via OTel processor, set 1GB/day ingestion cap on Log Analytics workspace

title: Reduce Azure Log Analytics & App Insights costs
description: Cut unnecessary telemetry spend: suppress HttpClient Info-level traces in worker, reduce Log Analytics retention to 14 days, reduce App Insights retention to 14 days, slow dev poll job to every 3 hours

title: iOS: zone picker chips invisible but tappable on Applications page (regression)
description:
Zone picker renders as an empty gap between the navigation title and the status filter pills on the Applications page. Chips are tappable (zone selection works) but visually invisible across all appearance modes (light, dark, system, OLED). The status filter chips directly below — which use identical styling code (same colors, same .buttonStyle(.plain), same capsule shape, same modifier chain) — render correctly.

Previously filed as tc-29z1, which was closed as fixed by tc-nvgw (Color.themed() refactored to UIColor(dynamicProvider:)). That fix did not resolve the underlying rendering issue. The autopilot worker also flagged this as needing hands-on simulator debugging.

Root cause analysis: The only structural difference between the working filter chips and the broken zone picker is that ZonePickerView is a separate View struct, while filterHeader builds its chips inline in ApplicationListView. Both use ForEach→Button→Text with .font/.foregroundStyle/.padding/.background/.clipShape/.overlay/.buttonStyle(.plain). This points to a SwiftUI rendering bug where .foregroundStyle() and .background() inside Button labels are not applied when the Button is in a ForEach inside a ScrollView inside a separate View struct — possibly related to how SwiftUI optimizes rendering across View boundaries.

Files: ZonePickerView.swift (the invisible component), ApplicationListView.swift:82-92 (where it's embedded), MapView.swift:90-100 (same component used on Map tab — check if also affected).

Potential fixes to try in the simulator: (1) inline the zone picker code directly into ApplicationListView, bypassing the separate View struct; (2) replace Button+label with a view+onTapGesture to avoid buttonStyle interaction; (3) add explicit .tint() or .renderingMode() to force text rendering.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #241. Root cause: .navigationBarTitleDisplayMode(.large) hijacked zone picker ScrollView. Fix: single List as sole scroll container with inlined chips.

title: fix(ios): watch zone name edit not persisting in list view after save
description:
When editing a watch zone name in the iOS app, the PATCH API call succeeds (name is saved server-side), but the WatchZoneListView never refreshes to show the updated name. The user sees the old name until they force-reload (e.g., restart the app or switch tabs).

Root cause: AppCoordinator.makeWatchZoneEditorViewModel() line 252-258 — the onSave callback calls watchZoneRepository.loadAll() but discards the result (_ = try? await). The WatchZoneListViewModel is a separate object with its own zones array, and its load() is only called via .task on first appear. After the edit sheet dismisses, nobody tells the list view model to reload.

Fix: The coordinator needs to signal the WatchZoneListViewModel to reload after a successful zone edit. Options include adding a refresh callback, using .task(id:) with a trigger, or leveraging the sheet's onDismiss parameter to trigger viewModel.load().

title: iOS: zone picker chips invisible on Applications page (dark mode color bug)
description: On the Applications page, the zone selector is invisible — empty space where it should be, but clicking in the space selects a zone. User is in LIGHT MODE (not dark mode). Initial dark-mode color theory was ruled out — Color.themed() always returns light hex values, and the light palette should be visible (dark text 0x1C1917 on white 0xFFFFFF with tan border 0xE8E4DF). The filter header chips use identical styling (same colors, same button style, same capsule shape) and render correctly. ZonePickerView is a separate View struct; filterHeader is inline — otherwise structurally identical. Root cause needs hands-on simulator debugging. Files: ZonePickerView.swift, ApplicationListView.swift, ApplicationListViewModel.swift
notes: Autopilot: blocked 2026-04-14. Reason: root cause unidentified — needs hands-on simulator debugging. Worker cannot diagnose visual rendering issues without simulator.
close_reason: Fixed by tc-nvgw — Color.themed() now uses UIColor(dynamicProvider:) instead of raw SwiftUI Color(hex:), resolving the rendering issue in ScrollView/Button context

title: Bug: 'Something went wrong' error on both Applications and Map after saving
description: After saving an application from the list detail view, navigating to the Map tab shows a 'Something went wrong' error. After force-quitting and relaunching, BOTH the Applications and Map tabs show this error persistently. Error display uses ErrorStateView which renders DomainError.userTitle (ErrorStateView.swift:21). Both ViewModels have @Published var error: DomainError? that gets set via handleError() when loadApplications() fails. Since both screens share the same underlying APIClient (URLSessionAPIClient), a persistent API failure (e.g. expired auth token, server error) would cause both to error simultaneously. Interestingly, after the error the status filter chips DO appear on the map (meaning tier resolved correctly and canFilter is true), but zone filters are still missing (meaning zone loading failed — zones array is empty). The save operation itself (PUT /v1/me/saved-applications/{uid}) should not affect subsequent GET calls, unless it triggers an auth state change or the server starts returning errors. Investigation needed: (1) check API logs for errors around the time of the save, (2) check if auth token refresh is failing, (3) reproduce with network logging enabled. Key files: ApplicationListViewModel.swift:159-161, MapViewModel.swift:119-121, APISavedApplicationRepository.swift:20-30, ErrorStateView.swift, DomainError.swift:46-50.
notes:
## Investigation (updated with user context)

**User confirmed: persistent after restart, dev environment (simulator).**

### Shared infrastructure:
Both ApplicationListViewModel and MapViewModel use the same URLSessionAPIClient (via APIPlanningApplicationRepository and APIWatchZoneRepository). If the API client enters a bad state (e.g. expired auth token that can't be refreshed), all API calls fail simultaneously.

### Error propagation:
Both ViewModels have `@Published var error: DomainError?` set via handleError(). ErrorStateView renders DomainError.userTitle which returns 'Something Went Wrong' for .unexpected, .invalidCoordinate, etc. (DomainError.swift:46-50).

### Persistent across restart:
If the error persists after force-quit and relaunch, it's NOT transient (not a race condition). Possible causes:
1. Auth token stored in Keychain is expired/invalid and refresh fails → sessionExpired error on every API call
2. Dev API server is actually down or returning errors consistently
3. User profile or data is in a bad state server-side causing 500s

### Status filters showing after error:
User reports status filters appear on map after the error. This means:
- subscriptionTier resolved to non-.free at some point (at least once)
- The .id() modifier triggered view recreation with correct tier
- But subsequent API calls still fail → error state shows with filter chips above it

### Zone filters missing after error:
If loadApplications() fails at the zone loading step (watchZoneRepository.loadAll() throws), zones array stays empty → showZonePicker = false. The error is caught and shown in the list/map body area, but the zone picker area above stays empty.

### Trigger: save operation
The save operation (PUT /v1/me/saved-applications/{uid}) itself is unlikely to corrupt state. BUT: if the save call triggers a token refresh that fails, the API client might enter a bad state where subsequent calls use an expired token.

### Debug steps:
1. Check dev API health: curl https://api-dev.towncrierapp.uk/health
2. Check Xcode console for auth errors or HTTP status codes
3. Try logging out and back in to get a fresh token
4. Check if the error is 'Session Expired' or 'Something Went Wrong' specifically
close_reason: Fixed token refresh error handling: network failures during refresh now throw networkUnavailable (retryable) instead of sessionExpired; retry-after-refresh errors propagate correctly; sessionExpired is now retryable. 3 new tests added.

title: Bug: Status filter chips not rendering — only Saved chip shows
description: On both ApplicationListView and MapView, the filter bar shows only the Saved chip. Status filters (All, Pending, Approved, Refused, Withdrawn, Appealed) are gated behind viewModel.canFilter which returns tier != .free (ApplicationListViewModel.swift:34, MapViewModel.swift:37). If the subscription tier resolves to .free (either because the user IS on free tier, or because resolveSubscriptionTier fails and defaults to .free), status chips won't render. The .id(coordinator.subscriptionTier) modifier (TownCrierApp.swift:131,148) is supposed to force view recreation when tier resolves, but if all three tier sources (JWT, StoreKit, server profile) return .free or error out, the tier stays .free and .id() doesn't trigger recreation. Investigation needed: (1) confirm user's actual subscription tier, (2) check if resolveSubscriptionTier() is succeeding, (3) verify .id() modifier correctly recreates views when tier changes from .free to non-free. Key files: TownCrierApp.swift:108,131,148, AppCoordinator.swift:165-174, ApplicationListView.swift:102, MapView.swift:110.
notes:
## Investigation (updated with user context)

**User confirmed: Pro tier, 7 watch zones, dev environment (simulator).**

### Tier resolution pipeline (AppCoordinator.swift:165-174):
Three sources, all can silently fall back to .free:

1. **JWT claim** (JWTSubscriptionTierExtractor.swift:13-20): Extracts `subscription_tier` from access token. Returns .free if claim is missing. Requires an Auth0 Action to populate this claim — likely NOT configured for dev tenant.

2. **StoreKit** (StoreKitSubscriptionService): `currentEntitlement()` returns nil on simulator (no real purchases) → defaults to .free.

3. **Server profile** (AppCoordinator.swift:176-188): Calls `GET /v1/me` → ServerProfileDTO.toDomain() → SubscriptionTier(rawValue: tier.lowercased()). Catches ALL errors and returns .free. If the profile tier field doesn't match 'pro'/'personal' exactly, rawValue init returns nil → .free.

### Result:
`max(.free, .free, .free) = .free` → canFilter = false → only Saved chip renders.

### Most likely root cause:
On the simulator, the ONLY reliable tier source is the server profile (GET /v1/me). If this endpoint returns tier='pro', it should work. Debug by checking: (1) does GET /v1/me return a profile? (2) what tier string does it return? (3) does rawValue parsing succeed?

### Files:
- JWTSubscriptionTierExtractor.swift:13-20 (JWT claim extraction)
- AppCoordinator.swift:165-188 (triple-source resolution)
- APIUserProfileRepository.swift:23-34,97-105 (server profile fetch + tier parsing)
- TownCrierApp.swift:108,131,148 (.task trigger and .id modifier)
close_reason: Fixed tier resolution: fetchServerTier() now returns nil on error (not .free), preserving previous tier. Added UserDefaults-backed tier cache so paying users retain features across launches. 6 new tests.

title: Bug: ApplicationDetailViewModel never loads saved state from server
description: When the AppCoordinator creates an ApplicationDetailViewModel via makeApplicationDetailViewModel(), it passes savedApplicationRepository but never determines whether the application is already saved. The isSaved parameter defaults to false (ApplicationDetailViewModel.swift:54), so the bookmark button always shows as unsaved regardless of server state. This means: (1) the save icon is never highlighted when viewing a previously-saved application, (2) the saved filter appears empty because toggleSave() silently re-saves an already-saved app then sets local state, but on next detail open isSaved resets to false. Fix: either pass the correct isSaved value from the coordinator (by checking savedApplicationUids or calling loadAll) or load the saved state lazily in ApplicationDetailViewModel.init via an async task. Key files: AppCoordinator.swift:147-158, ApplicationDetailViewModel.swift:51-55.
close_reason: Added loadSavedState() to ApplicationDetailViewModel that checks server for saved status on view appear. Bookmark button now correctly reflects saved state. 5 new tests.

title: Fix: status filter chips never render — tier not passed to ApplicationListViewModel

All constructors default tier to .free, so canFilter is always false and the status filter chips (Pending/Approved/Refused/etc.) never appear. Fix: pass subscriptionTier from coordinator to both factory methods.

The API returns 404 because that zone doesn't exist. The old fetchViaAuthorities() bootstrap path was removed in d862514 (spatial filtering) but nothing replaced it. title: fix(ios): case-insensitive subscription tier parsing from JWT and sync Cosmos profile
description: JWTSubscriptionTierExtractor.extractTier() does case-sensitive rawValue matching against the SubscriptionTier enum (lowercase: free/personal/pro), but Auth0 stores and returns PascalCase values (Free/Pro). This means the JWT path ALWAYS returns .free for every user. The Auth0 Post-Login Action default is also PascalCase ('Free'). Fix: add .lowercased() in JWTSubscriptionTierExtractor.swift:16 before SubscriptionTier(rawValue:), matching what APIUserProfileRepository already does. Also grant christy@salter.uk Pro in Cosmos via the admin endpoint since app_metadata was set manually bypassing the API.
design:
## Scope

### Code fix (iOS)
- **File:** mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-data/Sources/Auth/JWTSubscriptionTierExtractor.swift:16
- **Change:** `SubscriptionTier(rawValue: tierString)` → `SubscriptionTier(rawValue: tierString.lowercased())`
- This matches the pattern already used in APIUserProfileRepository.swift:100
- One-line fix, no new files

### Tests to update
- Existing tests in JWTSubscriptionTierExtractor likely only test lowercase values. Add cases for PascalCase ('Pro', 'Personal', 'Free') and UPPERCASE to confirm case-insensitive parsing.

### Data fix (Cosmos)
- Call `PUT /v1/admin/subscriptions` with `{email: 'christy@salter.uk', tier: 'Pro'}` to sync Cosmos with Auth0
- This updates both Cosmos AND Auth0 via GrantSubscriptionCommandHandler, ensuring consistency

### Out of scope
- Auth0 Post-Login Action casing ('Free' default) — cosmetic since the iOS fix handles it
- Adding a reverse-sync from Auth0 → Cosmos on login — separate concern, separate bead if needed

title: iOS: manageSubscriptionsSheet crash — Task @MainActor async wrapper doesn't prevent race
description: ea576f2 wrapped the binding setter in Task { @MainActor in }, but this is asynchronous — it defers the write instead of executing immediately. StoreKit calls the setter from a background thread; the async dispatch creates a race window triggering _dispatch_assert_queue_fail. Fix: use DispatchQueue.main.async or MainActor.assumeIsolated for synchronous dispatch.

The JWT subscription_tier claim is extracted in Auth0AuthenticationService.mapToSession() but never consulted by SettingsViewModel. Fix: use JWT tier as fallback when API call fails, or at minimum log the error so silent failures are diagnosable.

title: iOS: Current Plan shows 'Free' for Pro subscribers
description: SettingsViewModel.loadSubscriptionStatus() only checks StoreKit via subscriptionService.currentEntitlement(). If the user purchased Pro through the web (not the App Store), StoreKit has no record and returns nil, defaulting to .free. The backend /v1/me endpoint returns the correct tier, and APIUserProfileRepository.swift can decode it, but SettingsViewModel never calls the backend API. Fix: SettingsViewModel should check the backend API profile tier as the source of truth, using StoreKit only as a secondary/complementary source for App Store purchases. Files: SettingsViewModel.swift (lines 73-79), APIUserProfileRepository.swift.

title: iOS: Map tab shows no applications despite data existing
description: MapViewModel.loadApplications() creates an empty LocalAuthority(code: '', name: '') and passes it to the repository, resulting in GET /v1/applications?authorityId= which returns zero results. The web app correctly fetches authorities first via GET /v1/me/application-authorities, then fetches applications per authority. APIApplicationAuthorityRepository exists in the iOS data layer but MapViewModel never calls it. Fix: MapViewModel should fetch authorities via ApplicationAuthorityRepository.fetchAuthorities(), then fetch applications for each authority — matching the web app pattern. See also: WatchZone.authorityId defaults to 0 (invalid).
close_reason: Fixed: MapViewModel now fetches authorities via ApplicationAuthorityRepository first, then fetches applications per authority — matching the web app pattern. Empty LocalAuthority workaround eliminated.

title: iOS: Applications tab shows 'No Connection' when internet is available
description:
All API errors (server errors, decoding failures, etc.) are misclassified as network connectivity issues. Every repository's catch block maps any non-DomainError to DomainError.networkUnavailable, including APIError.serverError and APIError.decodingFailed.

## Root Cause (confirmed via App Insights)

The iOS app sends GET /v1/applications?authorityId= (empty string) because TownCrierApp.swift initializes ApplicationListViewModel with LocalAuthority(code: '', name: '') in the release (non-DEBUG) build path. The API endpoint expects an int authorityId, so ASP.NET returns HTTP 400 Bad Request instantly — no handler code is reached.

APIPlanningApplicationRepository catches this 400 as a generic error and remaps it to DomainError.networkUnavailable, which the UI renders as the 'No Connection' WiFi-slash screen.

## App Insights Evidence (2026-04-12)

| Time (UTC) | Endpoint | Status | Duration |
|---|---|---|---|
| 07:34:44 | GET /v1/applications?authorityId= | 400 | 191ms |
| 07:34:46 | GET /v1/applications?authorityId= | 400 | 0.6ms |
| 07:34:50 | GET /v1/applications?authorityId=<valid> | 200 | 2896ms |
| 07:36:31 | GET /v1/applications?authorityId= | 400 | 0.5ms |

3 out of 4 requests failed with 400 due to empty authorityId. The one request with a valid authority ID succeeded.

## Two-Part Fix Required

1. **Application loading**: Replace the hardcoded empty LocalAuthority in the release build path (TownCrierApp.swift lines 70-71) with proper authority loading from GET /v1/me/application-authorities — same issue as tc-d6m.
2. **Error mapping**: Repositories should distinguish HTTP 400/500 (APIError) from actual network failures (URLError). A 400 Bad Request is not 'network unavailable'. The NWPathConnectivityMonitor exists but is never consulted.

## Files
- TownCrierApp.swift (lines 70-71) — empty LocalAuthority in release path
- APIPlanningApplicationRepository.swift (lines 19-23) — catch-all error mapping
- All 18 repository files with the same catch { throw DomainError.networkUnavailable } pattern
- URLSessionAPIClient.swift (lines 25-42) — only catches URLError, lets APIError bubble up
close_reason: Fixed: two-part fix — (1) Error+DomainMapping.swift correctly maps APIError vs URLError across all 8 repositories, (2) ApplicationListViewModel now loads authorities dynamically instead of using empty LocalAuthority. 24 new tests.

title: [SRE] Poll job killed by replicaTimeout — uncapped Retry-After delay
description:
**What:** Every prod poll job execution since 2026-04-06T23:00Z has failed (33+ consecutive failures). The job processes authorities for ~6 minutes, hits PlanIt 429 for authority 52, then blindly honors the Retry-After header (281-554 seconds). The 10-minute replicaTimeout kills the container mid-wait.

**KQL (console logs):**
```
ContainerAppConsoleLogs_CL
| where ContainerGroupName_s startswith 'job-town-crier-poll-prod'
| where Log_s has 'waiting' and Log_s has '429'
| project TimeGenerated, Log_s
| order by TimeGenerated desc
```

**Key data:**
- Last successful run: 2026-04-06T22:00Z (33h ago)
- replicaTimeout: 600s (10 min)
- Retry-After values from PlanIt: 281-554s (4.7-9.2 min)
- Authority 52 is the consistent trigger
- Both prod and dev environments affected
- Root cause: PR #197 (aeb3ea5) added Retry-After header parsing without a max cap

**Blast radius:** Complete data ingestion outage. Zero planning applications being synced to prod. Dashboard shows flatline for sync success/failure and applications ingested.

**Investigation starting point:** PlanItClient.SendWithRetryAsync() in api/src/town-crier.infrastructure/PlanIt/PlanItClient.cs — the Retry-After delay must be capped to a value well under the 600s replicaTimeout (e.g. 30-60s max). Consider also: (1) after capping, should the code skip the authority rather than retry? (2) should the handler break out of the polling loop on rate-limit like it does after 2 consecutive 429s?
close_reason: Fixed: capped Retry-After delay to 30s max via MaxRetryAfterSeconds on PlanItRetryOptions. 6 commits, 150 tests pass.

title: Update CreateUserProfileCommandHandler to backfill email on existing profiles
description: When POST /v1/me is called for an existing user, the handler returns early without updating email. If the profile was created with null email, subsequent calls never fix it. The handler should update email if the existing profile has a null email and the new request provides one.

Add a second job in EnvironmentStack.cs with WORKER_MODE=digest and a daily cron schedule (the handler filters by user's preferred digest day). Spec: same pattern as the existing polling job but with digest mode.

title: Fix null description crash on dashboard from PlanIt polling data
description: PlanIt API returns null descriptions for some applications. The polling service stores these nulls in Cosmos (bypassing C# non-nullable string via JSON deserialization). Frontend truncate() crashes on null.length. Fix both backend ingestion (null-coalesce) and frontend rendering (null guard). Evidence: App Insights shows 20 apps upserted at 16:02:02 UTC, crash at 16:02:53 UTC.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #177. Null-coalesced PlanIt description in backend ingestion and added null guard in frontend truncate().

title: Fix OTel providers not initialized before metrics recorded in worker
description: MeterProvider and TracerProvider are lazy singletons never resolved until ForceFlush (after all metrics recorded). Eagerly resolve both before handler runs.

title: Add per-authority error isolation in PollPlanItCommandHandler
description: PollPlanItCommandHandler.HandleAsync only catches HttpRequestException with 429 status. Any other exception (JsonException, non-429 HttpRequestException, etc.) propagates up and kills the entire poll cycle for all authorities. Fix: wrap the per-authority loop body in a general try-catch that logs the error and continues to the next authority, preserving partial progress via the existing per-authority save.
close_reason: Fixed: per-authority try-catch added to PollPlanItCommandHandler. Non-429 exceptions now logged and skipped, preserving partial progress. 327 unit tests pass.

title: Fix PlanItResponse nullable pagination fields
description: PlanIt API returns null for $.from (and potentially $.total, $.pg_sz) pagination fields, but PlanItResponse.cs declares them as non-nullable int. This causes System.Text.Json.JsonException on deserialization, crashing the entire poll cycle. Fix: change From, Total, PageSize to int? in PlanItResponse.cs. Spec: evidenced by 70+ consecutive dev job failures in Azure Log Analytics.
close_reason: Fixed: PlanItResponse.From/Total/PageSize changed to int?, null-coalescing added in PlanItClient. 326 unit tests pass.

title: Fix map popup text contrast in dark mode
description: Leaflet popups have a hardcoded white background but our CSS variables resolve to light colors in dark mode, making text invisible. Fix: override Leaflet popup styles (.leaflet-popup-content-wrapper, .leaflet-popup-tip) to use --tc-surface background and --tc-text-primary/--tc-text-secondary text colors. User prefers dark popups matching the app theme over hardcoded light text.

title: Fix stale optimistic state in save/unsave toggle
description: Bug: save → unsave → save leaves UID in both pendingSaves and pendingRemoves. Since removes run last in useMemo, repeat toggling breaks. Fix: clear from opposite set when saving/unsaving.

title: iOS: agileapplications.co.uk planning application links open portal home instead of application detail
description:
On iOS, tapping a planning application link routes to the local authority's portal home page instead of the application detail page. Web works correctly. Affects (at least) all Richmond council links served by agileapplications.co.uk.

Repro:
- Application PA26/0883 (Richmond)
  - Web: loads https://planning.agileapplications.co.uk/richmond/application-details/239120 correctly
  - iOS: lands on https://planning.agileapplications.co.uk/richmond (home/search page only)

Counter-example (works on both):
- Application 26/00326/CLC (Kingston) → https://publicaccess.kingston.gov.uk/online-applications/applicationDetails.do?activeTab=summary&keyVal=TACS1TNHMOG00 — fine on iOS and web.

Hypothesis: agileapplications.co.uk likely requires a session/cookie or referrer that the iOS in-app browser (SFSafariViewController / WKWebView) doesn't carry, OR the URL stored for these is the portal root rather than the deep link. Check what URL the API returns for Richmond applications vs. what iOS opens, and whether iOS is stripping query/path. Could also be a JS-driven redirect that depends on a session not present in fresh iOS browser state.

Not all councils affected — appears specific to agileapplications.co.uk-hosted portals. User has not exhaustively tested all councils but found multiple Richmond applications all broken.
notes:
## Investigation 2026-05-01 — H1 PARTIALLY RULED OUT

**New evidence (user, 2026-05-01):**
- Mac Safari Private Browsing: works fine with the deep link
- iPhone Safari (full Safari, NOT just SFSafariViewController): bumps back to portal home

This rules out the cold-context theory in its simplest form (Mac Private Browsing also has no session/cookies and works). The differentiator is **mobile vs desktop user-agent**, not in-app vs full-Safari context.

### Revised hypothesis (H4 — most likely)

**agileapplications.co.uk serves a mobile-specific response that doesn't honour deep links to `/application-details/<id>`.** Likely mechanisms:
- User-agent sniffing returns a stripped-down mobile SPA shell that always boots at the root and loses the path
- Or a mobile redirect rule explicitly routes `/<authority>/application-details/*` → `/<authority>` for unknown user-agents
- Or the SPA's mobile bundle has a router bug where the deep-link path isn't recognised on first load

This is a **third-party portal bug, not a Town Crier bug.** But Town Crier is the user-visible failure point, so we need a workaround.

### Hypotheses now ranked

**H4 (NEW, MOST LIKELY) — agileapplications.co.uk mobile responsive layer breaks deep links.** Confirmed by user evidence. Affects all iOS Safari traffic to that domain regardless of how Town Crier opens the URL. Web works because most Town Crier web users open the link in desktop Safari.

**H1 (RULED OUT)** — `SFSafariViewController` cold-context. Mac Safari Private Browsing has the same cold context and works.

**H2 — Stored `url` for Richmond IS the portal root, not the deep link.** Still possible but unlikely; the user has the deep link in hand and it works on desktop. Worth checking once anyway.

**H3 (RULED OUT)** — iOS uses `application.url` (the council deep link), not `application.link` (PlanIt URL). Confirmed at `mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-data/Sources/Repositories/APIPlanningApplicationRepository.swift:69`.

### Concrete reproduction (no code needed)

1. Open iPhone Safari (NOT the Town Crier app).
2. Paste `https://planning.agileapplications.co.uk/richmond/application-details/239120` directly.
3. Observe: bumps to `/richmond` home.
4. Same URL on Mac Safari (any mode): loads correctly.

Confirms the bug is in agileapplications.co.uk's mobile response, not in Town Crier's URL handling.

### Mitigation options for ios-tdd-worker

**Option A — Force desktop user-agent in SFSafariViewController.** Not directly supported; SFSVC inherits user UA. Skip.

**Option B — Render the URL through a council-specific deep-link template that bypasses the SPA.**
Some agileapplications portals expose a server-side detail URL pattern. E.g., test whether `https://planning.agileapplications.co.uk/richmond/applicationDetail.aspx?id=239120` (or similar) is server-rendered and survives mobile UA. **Worker action:** view-source on a working desktop deep link, look for ANY `<a href="...">` to a server-rendered detail URL and extract the pattern.

**Option C — Embed our own minimal application detail view on iOS for affected councils, deep-linking to the portal only as a "view raw" fallback.** Larger scope; defer unless A/B fail.

**Option D — Display an explanatory message on iOS when the council is agileapplications-hosted: "Council portals on this provider don't load on mobile browsers; tap to view in desktop Safari" with a copy-link button.** Pragmatic UX while we work the upstream fix.

**Option E — Report the bug to agileapplications.co.uk and wait.** No timeline; not actionable on our side.

### Discovery work the worker should do first

1. **Identify ALL agileapplications.co.uk councils** that PlanIt feeds us. Quick query in Cosmos:
   ```sql
   SELECT DISTINCT c.authorityName FROM c WHERE CONTAINS(c.url, "agileapplications")
   ```
   Tells us blast radius — is this Richmond only, or 5+ councils?

2. **Check view-source on a working desktop deep link for an alternative URL pattern** (option B viability check). 5 minutes manual.

3. **Verify a non-agileapplications mobile portal** (e.g., Kingston publicaccess) renders properly on iPhone Safari to make sure THIS class of issue is agileapplications-specific.

### Code paths (still relevant)

- API URL ingestion: `api/src/town-crier.infrastructure/PlanIt/PlanItClient.cs:127-128` (URL stored verbatim from PlanIt)
- iOS URL opening: `mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-presentation/Sources/Features/ApplicationDetail/ApplicationDetailView.swift:49-53, 125-133` (SFSafariViewController)
- iOS URL mapping: `mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-data/Sources/Repositories/APIPlanningApplicationRepository.swift:69` (uses `url`, not `link`)
- Web URL opening: `web/src/features/ApplicationDetail/ApplicationDetailPage.tsx:140-148` (`<a target="_blank">`)

### Recommendation

Pick discovery step 1 + 2 first (≤ 30 min total). Then choose between option B (server-rendered detail URL if one exists) and option D (UX workaround). Option C is overkill unless multiple major councils are affected and B fails. File a separate bead to report upstream to agileapplications.co.uk.

title: iOS: wire NotificationPayloadParser.renderBody into UNNotificationServiceExtension
description: tc-so3a.15 added DecisionVocabulary + NotificationPayloadParser.renderBody on iOS but did not wire it into the UNNotificationServiceExtension target. Push notifications still arrive with raw PlanIt app_state values in the body. Fix: in the UNNotificationServiceExtension (separate SPM target/bundle), call renderBody on the userInfo and override mutableContent.body when it returns non-nil.
notes: Autopilot 2026-04-30: blocked because mobile/ios has no UNNotificationServiceExtension target — adding one requires creating a new Xcode target with Info.plist + entitlements + bundle config, which is scaffolding outside autopilot's TDD-worker scope. Needs human triage to set up the target, then autopilot can wire the renderBody call.

title: I3: iOS push payload rendering + UK vocab mapping
description: Spec: https://github.com/AmyDe/town-crier/issues/337 — Wire DecisionUpdate event push payloads to render UK terms (Approved / Approved with conditions / Refused / Refusal appealed). Closes existing gap where decision states drop to .unknown.
notes: COMPLETED: Added DecisionVocabulary helper in town-crier-domain (Permitted->Approved, Conditions->Approved with conditions, Rejected->Refused, Appealed->Refusal appealed; case-insensitive; nil for unknown/blank). Mirrors API's UkPlanningVocabulary. Wired through NotificationPayloadParser.renderBody(from:) — new method that takes a userInfo dict and returns the UK-formatted body 'Application <name> was <UK term>' when payload is a DecisionUpdate event with a recognised decision; nil otherwise. 16 new Swift Testing tests added (7 vocab + 9 parser). All 1062 tests pass, swiftlint --strict clean, swift-format clean. IN PROGRESS: nothing. NEXT: A future bead can wire renderBody into a UNNotificationServiceExtension target (separate bundle/target — out of scope here) and/or extend NotificationItem/NotificationItemDTO + NotificationsView in-app rows to surface decision badges (also separate scope — needs API DTO changes). BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) Helper lives in town-crier-domain (pure logic, no Foundation-specific deps beyond String trimming). (2) Did NOT modify NotificationDelegate.willPresent — UNNotificationContent.body is immutable post-delivery; rewriting requires UNNotificationServiceExtension which is a separate target/bundle and clearly larger scope. The helper is the wiring point for any future extension or in-app cell. (3) Did NOT touch ApplicationStatus.displayLabel — that uses different vocabulary ('Granted' vs 'Approved') and is a separate concern from decision-update notification rendering. (4) Sentence shape 'Application <name> was <UK term>' taken from bead description verbatim.

title: A12: Telemetry — tag notifications counters with event_type, sources, tier
description: Spec: https://github.com/AmyDe/town-crier/issues/337 — Extend ApiMetrics.NotificationsCreated + NotificationsSent with KeyValuePair tags: event_type, sources, tier. Add digest_rows_emitted (cadence + event_type).
notes: COMPLETED: Telemetry tags added per spec issue #337. (1) ApiMetrics.NotificationsCreated now tagged with event_type+sources in DispatchNotificationCommandHandler (NewApplication+Zone) and DispatchDecisionEventCommandHandler (DecisionUpdate + Zone/Saved/'Zone, Saved'). (2) ApiMetrics.NotificationsSent tagged with event_type+sources+tier in both dispatchers. (3) New ApiMetrics.DigestRowsEmitted counter (towncrier.digest.rows_emitted) emitted per row from GenerateHourly/WeeklyDigestsCommandHandler tagged cadence (hourly/weekly) + event_type. IN PROGRESS: nothing. NEXT: orchestrator can close — bead complete. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (a) Tag VALUES use C# enum ToString(): 'NewApplication'/'DecisionUpdate', 'Zone'/'Saved'/'Zone, Saved' (Flags ToString), 'Free'/'Personal'/'Pro'. Keeps the tag pipeline reflection-free / Native AOT-safe. (b) Counter NAME for new metric is towncrier.digest.rows_emitted (matches existing towncrier.* prefix, plus underscore between digest. and rows_emitted to mirror the spec's 'digest_rows_emitted' wording). (c) Weekly handler emits per-row metric BEFORE branching push/email, since both paths consume the same notification list — emitting once per row per user-send avoids double-counting. (d) Pre-existing flake in web-tests Should_ExportExceptionEvent_When_EndpointThrows passed on isolated rerun; integration-tests fail by design without INTEGRATION_TEST_API_BASE_URL — neither relates to this bead.

title: I1: iOS per-zone 4-toggle settings UI

title: A9: Weekly digest — apply same layout treatment as hourly

title: iOS: 'All' zone chip + status filter shows empty list; only Saved populates it
description:
## Repro (iOS, 2026-04-29 build, post-PR #329)

Applications page:
1. Select a real zone (e.g. 'Plymouth') + status filter 'All' → 2 items shown ✅
2. Switch zone pill to 'All' (status still 'All') → list says 'nothing to show'
3. Select any status pill EXCEPT Saved (Pending, Approved, etc.) → list still empty
4. Select Saved → list populates with all saved applications
5. Switch status filter back to 'All' → list now populated

## Expected

When user picks 'All' zone, they expect to see applications across all zones. At minimum the 'All' chip should never combine with non-Saved status pills to silently render an empty list — the current behaviour reads as broken.

## Actual root cause

By design from tc-nb5u (mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-presentation/Sources/Features/ApplicationList/ApplicationListViewModel.swift:266-286):
- `selectAllZones()` sets `applications = []` unless Saved filter is already active.
- `selectAllZones()` does NOT load anything cross-zone — there is no API path that fans out across zones in the iOS client.
- The 'All' chip is *only* meaningful with Saved active — it was introduced as an escape hatch for orphan saves outside any active zone.

The empty state for `.allZonesNoSavedFilter` shows 'Pick a zone... or turn on Saved...' but:
- The user already picked 'All' (which they read as 'all zones'), so 'pick a zone' is confusing.
- Selecting a status pill while in this state remains inert — no state change, same empty list.

## Suggested fix (one of)

A) Rename the chip from 'All' to 'Saved' (or 'All saved') and only render it when Saved-eligible. Removes the misleading semantics and matches actual behaviour.
B) Disable status pills (greyed out) while 'All' zone is selected and Saved is off, with copy: 'Status filters apply within a zone.' Keeps current model but removes the dead-end interaction.
C) Auto-activate Saved when 'All' is tapped, so the chip is always self-consistent.

Suggest brainstorming before picking. Web (tc-kmcp) shipped a similar zone selector — check whether the web version exhibits the same dead-end before deciding.

## Files

- mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-presentation/Sources/Features/ApplicationList/ApplicationListViewModel.swift
- mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-presentation/Sources/Features/ApplicationList/ApplicationListView.swift
- ApplicationListAllZonesTests, ApplicationListViewModelTests

## Related

- tc-nb5u (introduced 'All' chip)
- tc-wnap (made Saved zone-scoped — the prior cross-zone merge that 'All' partially replaces)
- tc-kmcp (web equivalent)

title: iOS: add 'All' option to list-view zone selector (orphan-save escape hatch)
description:
Sibling of tc-kmcp. After tc-wnap shipped, iOS Saved filter is zone-scoped — saves outside any active zone (from Search screen, or zones whose radius shrank) are unreachable. Add a synthetic 'All' entry to the list-view zone selector.

Behaviour:
- 'All' + Saved active → all saved applications across all zones (zone-agnostic via existing GET /v1/me/saved-applications).
- 'All' + Saved inactive → empty state with copy: 'Pick a zone to see applications, or turn on Saved to see everything you've bookmarked.'
- Map view: NO 'All' option. Map stays strictly per-zone (geographic context required).
- List and map views maintain independent zone-selection state — switching views does not sync the selection (preserves existing UX).

Files (high-level):
- mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-presentation/Sources/Features/ApplicationList/ — selector + empty-state copy + ViewModel changes
- ApplicationListViewModel: handle the synthetic 'All' selection (no zone-id filter on the applications list; Saved filter operates on the global savedApplicationUids set without zone-scoping).
- Tests: ApplicationListViewModel covers 'All' + Saved → all saves, 'All' + no Saved → empty list / empty-state flag.

Acceptance: user can select 'All' on the list view; with Saved active they see every saved app regardless of zone or zone history; with Saved inactive they see the empty state. Map view selector is unchanged. Existing per-zone Saved filtering still works on real-zone selections.
notes: COMPLETED: ApplicationListViewModel exposes 'All' zone option (selectAllZones(), isAllZonesSelected, EmptyStateKind, allZonesSentinel '__all__' persisted via UserDefaults). 'All'+Saved active sources applications from SavedApplicationRepository payloads (saved.compactMap(\.application)) so cross-zone bookmarks reappear; 'All'+Saved inactive shows empty list with 'Pick a zone... or turn on Saved...' EmptyStateView. selectZone clears All-mode; deactivating Saved while in All clears applications so the discoverability prompt takes over. showZonePicker now true when zones.count >= 1 (was > 1) so the All chip is reachable with a single zone — kept Map's showZonePicker untouched per bead. ApplicationListView prepends an 'All' chip in the zone scroller and switches empty-state copy off viewModel.emptyStateKind. 15 new tests in ApplicationListAllZonesTests + 1 updated showZonePicker test in ApplicationListViewModelTests. swiftlint --strict clean; full iOS suite 1066 tests pass. IN PROGRESS: none. NEXT: orchestrator merges. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) Used a String sentinel '__all__' in UserDefaults rather than introducing an enum-shaped persisted state — keeps the existing zoneSelectionKey schema and avoids a migration. (2) Exposed EmptyStateKind enum on the VM rather than letting the View pattern-match raw flags — single source of truth for empty-state messaging. (3) showZonePicker now visible with one zone (previously hidden); the bead's escape-hatch purpose is exactly to let single-zone users with orphan saves reach 'All'. Map showZonePicker unchanged. (4) Did NOT touch MapViewModel — bead explicitly excludes map view from 'All'.

title: API: parallelise saved-application hydration in GetSavedApplicationsQueryHandler (N+1 → batched)
description:
GetSavedApplicationsQueryHandler.HandleAsync does sequential N+1 lookups: 1 saves query + N separate GetByUidAsync calls (one per save). With 8 saves on a cold connection this contributes the bulk of the ~10s first-load latency observed for user auth0|69c5af69d57c3071e8baa592 in tc-qz0j.

Two options, in increasing order of payoff:
  (1) Parallelise the per-save hydration with Task.WhenAll. Trivial change, ~Nx speedup bounded by Cosmos RU and connection pool. Native AOT compatible — no reflection.
  (2) Replace the per-uid loop with a single batched read. Either:
      - SELECT * FROM c WHERE c.uid IN (@u1, @u2, ...) cross-partition (Applications has /authorityCode partition key, so this is fan-out anyway), OR
      - Build a list of (id, partitionKey) and use IReadOnlyList<(string, PartitionKey)> with TransactionalBatch / point reads via parallel ReadItemAsync — most efficient for known-id lookups.

Recommendation: start with (1) for the quick win, measure, then consider (2) if it's still the dominant cost. Either is invisible to clients — the response shape is unchanged.

Note: server-side filtering of orphan saves (where the application can't be hydrated) must continue to work — this is not behaviour change, only latency.

Files:
- api/src/town-crier.application/SavedApplications/GetSavedApplicationsQueryHandler.cs:18-38 (the foreach loop)
- api/src/town-crier.infrastructure/PlanningApplications/CosmosPlanningApplicationRepository.cs (if option (2) — likely add a GetByUidsAsync(IEnumerable<string>) method)
- Add TUnit handler test for the parallel/batched path

Acceptance: handler returns identical results, plus integration test asserting hydration count matches N (no over- or under-fetching), plus cold-call latency measurably reduced.
notes: COMPLETED: GetSavedApplicationsQueryHandler now hydrates per-save lookups in parallel via Task.WhenAll (option 1 from bead). Order is preserved by indexing into a positional array; orphan filtering still works (null hydrated entries are skipped). Added a barrier-style fake (BarrierPlanningApplicationRepository) and a TUnit test (Should_HydrateApplicationsInParallel_When_UserHasManySaves) that proves N concurrent calls are in flight at peak and asserts no over-fetch. Also un-excluded SavedApplications/*Tests.cs from the .csproj — the stale exclusion was masking 4 existing passing tests. Full test suite: application 443/443, infrastructure 277/277, web 67/67. Integration tests fail without Auth0 env vars (pre-existing). IN PROGRESS: none. NEXT: option (2) — batched read via GetByUidsAsync — remains a future optimisation if cold-load latency is still dominant after option (1) lands; tracked in bead description, not yet filed as a separate bead. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (a) chose option (1) per bead recommendation — minimal change, Native AOT safe, no new repo method; (b) wrote a barrier fake instead of timing-based assertions — deterministic, dead-locks fast on a sequential regression; (c) re-enabled the SavedApplications tests file rather than working around it, since a parallelism test is meaningless if the test file is excluded from the build.

- 'All' + Saved active → all saved applications across all zones, regardless of origin.
- 'All' + Saved inactive → empty state with copy: 'Pick a zone to see applications, or turn on Saved to see everything you've bookmarked.'

(2) For 'real zone + Saved on', filter is intersection of zone-fetched applications with saved UIDs (mirrors iOS behaviour) — not a geo or authority match — keeps server contract zone-agnostic. (3) For 'All + Saved on', list is sourced from SavedApplication.application denormalised payload — no second fetch needed. (4) Status filter chips not tier-gated yet (web has no tier-aware UI plumbing here); separate concern. (5) Auto-selects first zone on first non-empty zones load (not persisted across sessions yet). 586/586 tests pass; tsc clean; vite build passes.

title: iOS: 'Saved' filter shows empty state during in-flight loadAll() instead of a spinner
description:
On a fresh app launch, tapping the 'Saved' filter chip shows an empty list for ~10 seconds before the saved applications appear. ApplicationListViewModel.activateSavedFilter() synchronously flips isSavedFilterActive = true, then awaits repository.loadAll() — but savedApplicationUids is still the empty Set<String>() default at that moment, so filteredApplications returns []. The view's empty state is indistinguishable from 'no saved applications'.

Fix: introduce an isLoadingSaved (or similar) Published flag; set true before the await in activateSavedFilter(), false on completion (both success and catch branches). The view should render a progress indicator / skeleton while isLoadingSaved is true, suppressing the empty state. Same treatment for MapViewModel.activateSavedFilter().

The slow handler (GetSavedApplicationsQueryHandler does N+1 Cosmos lookups: 1 saves query + N hydration queries) is a separate latency concern — file a follow-up if we want to address that, but the empty-state UX is fixable without touching the API.
