- mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-presentation/Sources/Features/Map/MapView.swift
notes: COMPLETED: Added @Published isLoadingSaved flag to ApplicationListViewModel (mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-presentation/Sources/Features/ApplicationList/ApplicationListViewModel.swift) and MapViewModel (mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-presentation/Sources/Features/Map/MapViewModel.swift). Set true synchronously before await in activateSavedFilter(); cleared in both success and failure branches. Updated isEmpty in both ViewModels to also check !isLoadingSaved so the misleading empty state is suppressed during loadAll(). Updated ApplicationListView and MapView to render skeleton/spinner while isLoadingSaved is true. Added 4 ViewModel tests per ViewModel (defaultsFalse, isTrueWhileLoadAllInFlight, clearsAfterSuccess, clearsAfterFailure). Created ControllableSavedApplicationRepository test double using CheckedContinuation pattern to assert in-flight state. All 1051 tests pass, swiftlint --strict clean. IN PROGRESS: none. NEXT: orchestrator to merge. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) Used a single isLoadingSaved flag rather than overloading isLoading, since isLoading already drives the initial-load skeleton — keeping them separate avoids accidentally hiding the chip rows. (2) Reverted swift-format reformat noise on unrelated files (Error+DomainMapping, AppCoordinator, etc.) to keep the bead's footprint minimal — those drifts pre-date this bead. (3) Did not file a side-quest for the GetSavedApplicationsQueryHandler N+1 latency — the bead description already calls out that 'separate latency concern'; assume orchestrator will file separately if desired.

title: iOS: 'Saved' filter should be scoped to the active watch zone (drop cross-zone merge)
description:
Currently in ApplicationListViewModel.filteredApplications (and MapViewModel.filteredAnnotations) the 'Saved' filter merges saved-in-current-zone with saved-in-other-zones — producing a global saved list whose order shifts depending on which zone is selected. Product intent is for filter pills (status, saved) to operate ON the current zone's applications, mirroring the other pills.

Fix: when isSavedFilterActive is true, filteredApplications should return only 'applications.filter { savedApplicationUids.contains($0.id.value) }'. Drop the 'fromSavedData' branch and the 'savedApplications' private cache. Apply the same change in MapViewModel.

Trade-off worth flagging: a save whose application sits in no current watch zone (e.g. PA26/0443 — Richmond — for user auth0|69c5af69d57c3071e8baa592 who has Home/Kingston + Plymouth zones) becomes unreachable from iOS until they add a matching zone or visit the web /saved page. Acceptable per product call.

Files:
- mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-presentation/Sources/Features/ApplicationList/ApplicationListViewModel.swift:50-64,183-200
- mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-presentation/Sources/Features/Map/MapViewModel.swift (equivalent merge)
- Update existing tests in mobile/ios/town-crier-tests/Sources/Features/ApplicationListSavedFilterTests.swift (savedFilter_showsSavedAppsNotInCurrentList, savedFilter_doesNotDuplicateAppsAlreadyInList) — these encode the old cross-zone behaviour and need to be replaced with zone-scoped expectations.
notes: COMPLETED: ApplicationListViewModel.filteredApplications now scopes the 'Saved' filter to current-zone applications only — dropped the cross-zone merge ('fromSavedData') and removed the savedApplications private cache. Updated ApplicationListSavedFilterTests: replaced savedFilter_showsSavedAppsNotInCurrentList + savedFilter_doesNotDuplicateAppsAlreadyInList + savedFilter_ignoresSavedAppsWithoutEmbeddedData with two zone-scoped tests (savedFilter_excludesSavedAppsNotInCurrentZone, savedFilter_showsAppOnceWhenInCurrentZoneAndSaved). MapViewModel was already zone-scoped — no change needed there. All 1050 iOS tests pass; swiftlint --strict clean. IN PROGRESS: none. NEXT: orchestrator merges. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) MapViewModel needed no change because filteredAnnotations only filtered the in-zone annotations array — the bead description anticipated symmetric merge logic but it never existed there. (2) Cross-zone saves (e.g. PA26/0443 in Richmond when user has Kingston+Plymouth zones) now unreachable from iOS Saved filter — acceptable per product call; users reach them via web /saved or by adding a matching zone.

title: iOS: tapping 'Notification Preferences' in Settings does nothing (no navigation)
description: On the iOS app, tapping the 'Notification Preferences' row in Settings does not navigate to a notification management screen. Repro: open iOS app, go to Settings, tap 'Notification Preferences'. Expected: pushes onto the navigation stack to a screen for managing notification settings. Actual: nothing happens — no navigation, no visible feedback. Investigate: (1) is the row's tap handler wired up at all (NavigationLink / Button action); (2) is the Coordinator missing the route; (3) is there a destination view but it's hidden behind a feature flag or empty state; (4) check the Settings view in town-crier-presentation for the row definition.
notes: COMPLETED: Fixed dead Settings row by wiring onNotificationPreferences callback in TownCrierApp.swift; row now opens iOS system Settings page for the app via UIApplication.openSettingsURLString. Added requestNotificationPreferences() test seam on SettingsView and isOpeningSystemNotificationSettings/showSystemNotificationSettings on AppCoordinator. 4 new tests, all 1054 tests green, swiftlint --strict clean. IN PROGRESS: nothing. NEXT: PR + merge. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: Chose to deep-link to iOS system Settings rather than build a new in-app preferences screen because (a) bead is filed as a P2 BUG not a feature, (b) per-zone alert toggles already exist via WatchZone -> ZonePreferencesView, (c) no global notification preferences screen had been designed, (d) opening UIApplication.openSettingsURLString is the standard iOS pattern for managing push permissions/banners/sounds. Used a Coordinator-published flag (isOpeningSystemNotificationSettings) + .onChange in TownCrierApp.body so the Coordinator stays UIKit-free while the wiring stays unit-testable.

title: Web: 'instant emails' toggle reverts to off after click, but hourly emails still arriving
description: On the web app, a Pro subscriber clicks the 'instant emails' toggle in Settings. The toggle briefly shows as ON, then flips back to OFF on its own — suggesting the PATCH/PUT request is failing or the response is overwriting local state with the old value. Separately, the user reports they ARE receiving hourly emails despite the toggle appearing off, which implies the underlying preference flag may be desynced from what the UI is rendering. Repro: log in as a Pro user on web, go to Settings, click the 'instant emails' toggle. Expected: toggle stays ON and persists across reload. Actual: toggle reverts to OFF within ~1s. Investigate: (1) network tab for the PATCH call status/response; (2) whether the server is returning the prior value due to a validation or auth failure; (3) whether the email-cadence backend is reading a different field than the UI is writing.
notes:
DECISION NEEDED before proceeding.

ROOT CAUSE: The 'Instant emails' toggle is a dead toggle — wired to a backend field that has been deprecated.

EVIDENCE:
1. api/src/town-crier.infrastructure/UserProfiles/UserProfileDocument.cs:19-21 explicitly comments EmailInstantEnabled as 'Legacy field — kept for backward compatibility with existing Cosmos documents. No longer written; ignored during domain reconstitution.'
2. The domain NotificationPreferences (api/src/town-crier.domain/UserProfiles/NotificationPreferences.cs) has only PushEnabled, DigestDay, EmailDigestEnabled — no EmailInstantEnabled.
3. The application's UpdateUserProfileCommand, UpdateUserProfileResult, GetUserProfileResult all OMIT EmailInstantEnabled.
4. The PATCH /v1/me endpoint never reads or writes the field.
5. The web frontend sends emailInstantEnabled in the body but the API ignores it. The PATCH response also has no emailInstantEnabled, so the deserialized response has 'undefined' for that field. useUserProfile.ts then setOptimisticProfile(updated) overwrites the optimistic ON state with undefined, the Toggle reads 'undefined ?? false' and renders OFF — exact symptom described.
6. 'Hourly emails' the user receives are actually the HourlyDigestEmails entitlement (Pro tier) gated on EmailDigestEnabled, not EmailInstantEnabled. So toggling 'Instant emails' has no effect on emails being sent at all.
7. iOS has no equivalent toggle (no emailInstant references in /mobile/ios).

OPTIONS:
A) Remove the toggle entirely from the web Settings page. Drop emailInstantEnabled from web's UserProfile, UpdateProfileRequest, fixtures, and tests. Pure web change, in-scope. (Optionally file a backend bead to remove the Cosmos legacy field once all clients are off it.)
B) Keep the toggle and re-introduce 'Instant emails' as a real backend feature: add EmailInstantEnabled to NotificationPreferences, the UpdateUserProfileCommand, the response DTOs, and a new EmailInstantNotifier worker. Out of scope for this web-only bead — would require a new backend bead.
C) Keep the toggle in the UI but mark it as 'Coming soon' / disabled until backend support lands. Half-measure.

MY RECOMMENDATION: A. The bead reports the toggle as broken, the root cause is that the backend feature was deleted, and the user expectation is being violated. Removing the dead toggle eliminates user confusion. The 'still receiving hourly emails' complaint is then redirected to the correct toggle (Email Digest), which is what actually controls those emails for Pro users. If the product wants instant per-application emails as a feature, that's a new feature bead, not a bug fix.

STOPPED awaiting human decision (SendMessage tool not available in this worker; recording here per escalation-protocol intent).

COMPLETED: investigation, root cause identified.
IN PROGRESS: awaiting human decision A/B/C.
NEXT: implement chosen option via TDD.
BLOCKER: human decision required — removing a user-visible UI element is a product call.
KEY DECISIONS: web toggle is wired to a deleted backend field; the apparent 'hourly emails arriving' is an unrelated correct behaviour (HourlyDigestEmails entitlement gated on EmailDigestEnabled).
Superseded by per-zone notifications work (parent bead + spec to be filed). Single global 'Instant emails' toggle is being replaced by per-WatchZone pushEnabled + emailInstantEnabled toggles.

title: Wire APNs registration: register, capture device token, POST to backend
description:
The Push Notifications capability is wired (aps-environment entitlement, App ID configured for Sandbox & Production) but the app never actually registers with APNs, so no device tokens flow to the backend and TestFlight/prod pushes don't reach users.

Required:
- Call UIApplication.shared.registerForRemoteNotifications() after the user grants notification permission (currently UNNotificationPermissionProvider asks but doesn't register).
- Implement application(_:didRegisterForRemoteNotificationsWithDeviceToken:) — convert the Data token to a hex string.
- Implement application(_:didFailToRegisterForRemoteNotificationsWithError:) — log + report (don't crash).
- POST the device token to a backend endpoint (e.g. POST /v1/devices) with the user's Auth0 ID. Backend stores it for use when sending push notifications via APNs auth key (.p8) for matched watch zones.
- Re-register on each app launch and on token refresh (APNs can rotate tokens — handle the new token by re-POSTing).
- Handle token registration before vs after auth: if user is anonymous, queue the token and POST after auth completes; if user logs out, the backend may want to forget the token (separate decision).

Out of scope for this bead:
- Backend endpoint to receive/store device tokens (likely a separate bead in /api).
- Backend code that reads the .p8 from Key Vault and pushes to APNs (separate bead).
- Notification payload schema and tap-handling (NotificationDelegate is wired, but the actual deep-link flow needs end-to-end testing once tokens flow).

This is needed before external TestFlight testers can validate that notifications actually arrive — internal testers can still test the rest of the app without it.
notes: COMPLETED: APNs registration wired end-to-end. Added AppDelegate (UIApplicationDelegateAdaptor) that calls UIApplication.registerForRemoteNotifications() on every launch and forwards didRegister/didFail callbacks. Added PushNotificationRegistrar actor (presentation pkg) that holds the auth-aware queue: registers immediately when authenticated, queues otherwise; flushPendingRegistration() drains the queue on login. Added Data.hexEncodedString() and RemoteNotificationRegistering domain protocol with UIApplicationRemoteRegistrar concrete. CompositeNotificationService now triggers registerForRemoteNotifications() when permission is granted (so onboarding/settings re-permission both trigger token flow). LoginViewModel exposes onAuthenticated callback, fired from both login() and checkExistingSession() success paths; TownCrierApp wires this to registrar.flushPendingRegistration(). 9 PushNotificationRegistrar tests + 5 hex tests + 4 LoginViewModel callback tests + 2 Composite registrar tests. Total: 1028 tests passing, swiftlint --strict clean, xcodebuild iphonesimulator clean. IN PROGRESS: nothing. NEXT: orchestrator merges; backend bead to receive POST /v1/me/device-token already exists (APINotificationService consumes it). BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) Registrar lives in town-crier-presentation as a use-case orchestrator alongside Coordinators; (2) RemoteNotificationRegistering protocol added to domain so Composite can trigger registerForRemoteNotifications() without importing UIKit, keeping macOS test compilation working; (3) onAuthenticated callback on LoginViewModel rather than direct registrar coupling — keeps presentation pkg unaware of UIKit concerns; (4) re-register on every launch (didFinishLaunching) rather than gating on first-time permission since APNs may rotate tokens silently; (5) didFailToRegister logs only — does not surface as user-visible error, app remains usable without push.

title: iOS: editing a watch zone's radius leaves Map ring + Applications list stale until app cold-resume
description:
Reported by the user (real flow on simulator, Pro tier, 5 zones: Home/Romberg/Park View/Dads/Mum). Editing the radius of an existing watch zone in-place is not propagated to the Map and Applications views — only to the Zones list view-model. The two affected views show stale or empty data until the app is backgrounded long enough that the OS suspends + cold-resumes the process; only then do the views correctly reflect the new radius.

## Repro

1. Fresh app launch with at least one existing watch zone ("Home" centred KT2 6EJ, radius 300m, 6 applications inside the zone).
2. On the Map tab the 300m ring is drawn and 6 pins are visible.
3. Open the Zones tab → tap Home → editor sheet → change radius to 200m → save. Sheet dismisses.
4. Switch back to the Map tab. **Bug A:** application count drops 6 → 2 (correct) but the radius ring stays drawn at 300m (wrong).
5. Switch to a different tab and back to Map. The ring then shrinks to 200m (workaround: forces a view rebuild).
6. Switch to the Applications tab with Home pill + All status pill selected. **Bug B:** the list is empty (no applications shown), even though the Map view shows 2 inside Home. Other zone pills (Romberg/Park View/Dads/Mum) still show their applications correctly — the bug is scoped to the edited zone only. Pull-to-refresh / re-tapping the Home pill: not tested in this session, would be useful to verify next time.
7. After several minutes idle (long enough that the OS suspended and cold-resumed the app process), the Apps view correctly shows the 2 applications under Home.

## Root cause analysis

Both `MapViewModel.loadApplications()` and `ApplicationListViewModel.loadApplications()` reload the zones array from `watchZoneRepository.loadAll()` but only refresh `selectedZone` if its id is no longer present in the loaded list:

```swift
// MapViewModel.swift:104-108, mirrored in ApplicationListViewModel.swift:155-160
let loadedZones = try await watchZoneRepository.loadAll()
zones = loadedZones
if selectedZone == nil || !loadedZones.contains(where: { $0.id == selectedZone?.id }) {
  selectedZone = resolveInitialZone(from: loadedZones)
}
```

When a zone is edited in place, the id is unchanged, so `selectedZone` retains the *old* value-typed copy with the old radius/centre. `MapViewModel` then assigns `radiusMetres = zone.radiusMetres` (line 117) from the stale copy, which is what the `MapCircle` in MapView.swift binds to (lines 63-71). Hence the stale ring.

The applications fetch endpoint (`GET /v1/me/watch-zones/{zoneId}/applications`) only takes the zone id and uses the server's persisted radius — that's why the *count* refreshes immediately but the *ring* doesn't.

## Open question for Bug B (zero applications, not stale six)

The `selectedZone` staleness above only explains why the local copy carries the old radius — it does NOT explain why the Apps view shows 0 instead of 6. The Apps view goes through `OfflineAwareRepository` (15-min TTL, cached by zone id), so a cache hit for the freshly-launched session should return the previously-fetched 6 (300m) results. The Map view, by contrast, uses the bare `PlanningApplicationRepository` and so always sees the live server count.

Possible explanations for the 0-count (worker should investigate):
- Apps view's `.task` did not run before the user opened it post-edit (Apps tab is the first tab, but maybe the user landed elsewhere — verify), so no cached 6 ever got stored. Then when `.task` did run after the edit, OfflineAwareRepository cache miss + server fetch should have returned 2 — why didn't it?
- A subtle interaction inside `InMemoryApplicationCacheStore` (mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-data/Sources/Repositories/InMemoryApplicationCacheStore.swift) — perhaps the cache key uses the full WatchZone (not just the id), so the post-edit zone with new radius is a cache miss but something else fails or returns empty.
- Network/connectivity flap on simulator that returned an empty list, mis-cached.
- A path where `applications` is reset to `[]` and then the fetch silently fails or no-ops.

## Repository asymmetry to be aware of

`AppCoordinator.makeMapViewModel()` (line 97) injects the bare `PlanningApplicationRepository`. `AppCoordinator.makeApplicationListViewModel()` (line 133) injects the `OfflineAwareRepository` (when available). This means the two views have different freshness semantics for application data, and a fix must reason about both.

## Suggested fix direction (non-binding — for the worker)

Make `selectedZone` refresh from `loadedZones` whenever the zone is found, not only when it is missing — e.g.:

```swift
if let updated = loadedZones.first(where: { $0.id == selectedZone?.id }) {
  selectedZone = updated
} else {
  selectedZone = resolveInitialZone(from: loadedZones)
}
```

Apply in both `MapViewModel.loadApplications()` and `ApplicationListViewModel.loadApplications()`. Add unit tests that prove a radius change on the currently selected zone propagates into the published `radiusMetres` / `selectedZone` properties without requiring re-selection.

Separately, consider whether editing a zone should invalidate the applications cache for that zone id — the cache key is currently zone id only, so a stale-radius cache hit could in principle outlive a radius change for up to 15 minutes (`OfflineAwareRepository.swift`, TTL 900s). The Apps-view-zero question above may be related.

## Affected files (entry points for investigation)

- mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-presentation/Sources/Features/Map/MapViewModel.swift (loadApplications, selectZone)
- mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-presentation/Sources/Features/Map/MapView.swift (MapCircle binding)
- mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-presentation/Sources/Features/ApplicationList/ApplicationListViewModel.swift (loadApplications, selectZone)
- mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-domain/Sources/Entities/OfflineAwareRepository.swift (cache key + TTL)
- mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-data/Sources/Repositories/InMemoryApplicationCacheStore.swift (cache key implementation)
- mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-presentation/Sources/Coordinators/AppCoordinator.swift (lines 97, 133 — repository injection asymmetry; lines 256-274 — onSave callback only refreshes the Zones list view-model)
notes: COMPLETED: Fixed Bug A (stale Map ring + Apps zone pill after in-place zone edit). Both MapViewModel.loadApplications() and ApplicationListViewModel.loadApplications() now refresh selectedZone from the reloaded list whenever the id matches, instead of only when the id is missing — so an in-place edit (same id, new radius/centre) propagates to centreLat/centreLon/radiusMetres. Two new test files (MapViewModelStaleZoneTests.swift, ApplicationListViewModelStaleZoneTests.swift) cover the regression. All 1012 tests + swiftlint --strict pass. IN PROGRESS: nothing. NEXT: orchestrator merges PR. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) Implemented bead's primary suggested fix verbatim — refresh selectedZone when id is found. (2) Did NOT pursue the bead's secondary 'cache invalidation on zone edit' question — filed tc-9vid as discovered-from for that, since it requires touching ApplicationCacheStore protocol + InMemoryApplicationCacheStore + OfflineAwareRepository + AppCoordinator wiring, which is a wider blast radius than this bug fix justifies. (3) Bug B (Apps shows 0 not 6 stale) is still an open question per the bead — could be a transient .task-reload race, simulator network flap, or cache miss + later fetch error. Cannot reproduce from code alone; the cache key is zone.id only so a cache hit at the moment of zone edit would return 6, not 0. The selectedZone fix above is necessary regardless and may help — re-test on device. (4) swift-format default style conflicts with the repo's existing 'trailing brace on same line' convention for multi-line if-let chains, so reverted swift-format's reformatting of unrelated files.

title: Wire DispatchDecisionAlertCommand into polling pipeline
description: Spec: docs/specs/decision-state-vocabulary.md#dispatch
notes: COMPLETED: Wired DispatchDecisionAlertCommand into the polling pipeline. Added IDecisionAlertDispatcher port (Application) + DispatchDecisionAlertViaHandler adapter (Infrastructure) mirroring INotificationEnqueuer/DispatchNotificationEnqueuer. PollPlanItCommandHandler detects transition from non-decision to {Permitted,Conditions,Rejected,Appealed} (case-insensitive IsDecisionState helper) and dispatches; first-time inserts of already-decided apps trigger; same-decision-class changes (Permitted->Conditions) and HasSameBusinessFieldsAs skip-path do NOT dispatch. DI wired in both worker (load-bearing — polling runs there) and web (symmetry). NoOpDecisionAlertPushSender registered as IDecisionAlertPushSender fallback until APNS-backed sender lands. New tests in PollPlanItCommandHandlerDecisionAlertTests cover all 4 decision states + 3 non-decision states + same-class-change + business-fields-unchanged. All 807 unit tests pass. IN PROGRESS: none. NEXT: nothing for this bead — closing. Sibling beads tc-f71q.1/.2/.3 (vocabulary alignment in iOS/web/API tests) are independent and may proceed in parallel. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) Created IDecisionAlertDispatcher port rather than injecting DispatchDecisionAlertCommandHandler directly into the polling layer — keeps the precedent set by INotificationEnqueuer and avoids Application->Infrastructure dependency leak. (2) Registered NoOpDecisionAlertPushSender (not the spec's hypothetical APNS impl) because no APNS implementation exists for IPushNotificationSender either — the gap is consistent across both push abstractions; alerts still get persisted as DecisionAlert docs. (3) IsDecisionState is case-insensitive to tolerate provider drift but matches the canonical PlanIt strings exactly (Permitted/Conditions/Rejected/Appealed).

title: Align web state vocabulary with PlanIt (Permitted/Conditions/Rejected)
description: Spec: docs/specs/decision-state-vocabulary.md#web
notes: COMPLETED: Web vocabulary aligned with PlanIt — ApplicationStatus union now Permitted|Conditions|Rejected|Undecided|Withdrawn|Appealed|Unresolved|Referred|Not Available; legacy Approved/Refused removed and rejected by isApplicationStatus. CSS tokens renamed (--tc-status-approved → --tc-status-permitted, --tc-status-refused → --tc-status-rejected, added --tc-status-conditions). New statusDisplayLabel helper maps wire vocab → UK user-facing copy ('Granted', 'Granted with conditions', 'Refused'). ApplicationCard and ApplicationDetailPage render display labels. All fixtures renamed (approvedApplication → permittedApplication, savedApprovedApplication → savedPermittedApplication, approvedWithDecision → permittedWithDecision). 576 vitest tests pass; tsc --noEmit clean; production build succeeds. IN PROGRESS: nothing. NEXT: nothing — ready for orchestrator merge. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) Kept user-facing labels 'Granted'/'Refused' via statusDisplayLabel helper rather than changing wire vocab in copy — matches spec line 113 and how UK residents speak about planning outcomes. (2) Picked deeper orange for --tc-status-conditions (#FF9500 dark / #A85A0A light) so Conditions reads distinct from Pending's amber-yellow. (3) Renamed --tc-status-refused even where it's used as a generic 'error red' (e.g. SettingsPage delete button) — spec told us to rename globally and the semantic 'error/destructive' colour pairs cleanly with the rejected status hue. (4) Added pass-through case in statusDisplayLabel for unknown values rather than throwing, since appState is typed as string on the wire.

title: Align iOS state vocabulary with PlanIt (Permitted/Conditions/Rejected)
description: Spec: docs/specs/decision-state-vocabulary.md#ios
notes: COMPLETED: iOS state vocabulary now matches PlanIt's wire vocabulary. ApplicationStatus is String-backed with raw values 'Permitted/Conditions/Rejected/Withdrawn/Appealed/Undecided/Unresolved/Referred/Not Available'. Decision is permitted|conditions|rejected. APIPlanningApplicationRepository.toDomain() decodes via ApplicationStatus(rawValue:) ?? .unknown — no more switch/string-table indirection. Display labels use UK-resident vocabulary ('Granted', 'Granted with conditions', 'Refused'). Added tcStatusPermitted/tcStatusConditions/tcStatusRejected color tokens; removed tcStatusApproved/tcStatusRefused. All 1008 iOS tests pass. SwiftLint --strict passes 0 violations. IN PROGRESS: nothing. NEXT: parent epic tc-f71q's web/api/dispatch tracks. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) kept .unknown as the explicit no-rawValue fallthrough sentinel — still useful for forward-compat with PlanIt adding new states; rawValue init returns nil for those, repo defaults to .unknown. (2) display vocabulary diverges from wire vocabulary by design — UK residents say 'Refused' even though PlanIt sends 'Rejected'. (3) renamed fixtures: .approved -> .permitted, .refused -> .rejected, approvedWithHistory -> permittedWithHistory, refusedWithHistory -> rejectedWithHistory. (4) added Conditions to filter chips in MapView and ApplicationListView so the 13% of apps in that state are reachable. (5) swift-format not installed locally so only SwiftLint --strict was run; CI will format-check.

title: Make decision tracking work end-to-end

title: Add never_polled_count metric and update verify-polling skill for fairness drain
description:
Surface the tc-ews7 regression class directly in observability and the /verify-polling skill so future starvation incidents are caught in dashboards rather than ad-hoc Cosmos queries.

Scope:

1. Add new metric `towncrier.polling.never_polled_count` (Gauge<long>) emitted at cycle start in PollPlanItCommandHandler. Value = activeAuthorityIds.Count - polledSet.Count (the count of authorities with no PollState document). Tagged by cycle.type. Implementation needs a small refactor of CosmosPollStateStore.GetLeastRecentlyPolledAsync (or a new method on IPollStateStore) so the polled-document count is exposed to the handler. Add tests covering: emits at cycle start, value matches authorities-without-state, emits for both Seed and Watched cycle types.

2. Update .claude/commands/verify-polling.md:
   - Section 3e: rename prose from 'oldest HWM age' to 'oldest LastPollTime age'. Add one-line note about the legacy metric name (oldest_hwm_age_seconds is from pre-PR #298). Filter the existing query with never_polled == 'false' so it reports lag for polled authorities only.
   - New section 3g: Backlog drain rate (never-polled cohort). Query the new metric over a 24h window, expect monotonic decrease to 0. Cite tc-ews7 as canonical regression.
   - Report format: add never-polled count to Invariants table and Totals-since-deploy table.

Acceptance: 
- Metric appears in customMetrics in App Insights post-deploy.
- /verify-polling skill references the new metric and asserts on drain.
- Tests pass.
- Discovered-from tc-ews7.
notes: COMPLETED: Item 1 of bead — added towncrier.polling.never_polled_count gauge (long) declared in PollingMetrics.cs and emitted at cycle start in PollPlanItCommandHandler.HandleAsync, tagged with cycle.type. Refactored IPollStateStore.GetLeastRecentlyPolledAsync to return a new LeastRecentlyPolledResult record (sorted ids + never-polled count) so the count comes from the same in-memory polledSet pass — no duplicate Cosmos query. Updated CosmosPollStateStore + FakePollStateStore + the one infrastructure test that consumed the old IReadOnlyList<int> return type. New test file PollPlanItCommandHandlerNeverPolledMetricTests.cs covers the four required cases (5/10 not polled tagged seed, drain end-state=0, fresh-deploy=total, watched cycle tag). dotnet build clean (0 errors), application tests 423/423 pass, infrastructure tests 269/269 pass, dotnet format --verify-no-changes exit 0. IN PROGRESS: Nothing — item 1 is feature-complete on branch feat/tc-ifdl. NEXT: Orchestrator merges feat/tc-ifdl. Item 2 (verify-polling.md) is on the same branch (commit 71a9df1) handled separately by orchestrator. Post-deploy verify the metric appears in App Insights customMetrics named 'towncrier.polling.never_polled_count'. BLOCKER: None. KEY DECISIONS: Chose option (a) — refactor return type — over (b) new IPollStateStore.CountNeverPolledAsync method. Option (b) would have caused a duplicate Cosmos query in CosmosPollStateStore (no inter-call cache). Option (a) leverages the existing polledSet computation; the API breakage is contained to one production call site, one fake, and one infrastructure test. Gauge typed as long (count) per spec, not double; tags only cycle.type per spec (not authority_code) since the cohort is system-wide.

title: Raise RetryAfterCap from 30 min to 3 hours
description:
Spec: PollNextRunSchedulerOptions.RetryAfterCap caps the parsed PlanIt Retry-After at 30 minutes. Change to 3 hours so we honour aggressive backoffs.

Why: /verify-polling 2026-04-24 found a recurring pattern where ~30-min gaps between cycles end with the next cycle's first PlanIt response being 429. Working hypothesis: PlanIt has noticed our polling pattern and is sending Retry-After values larger than 30 min as a de-escalation signal. Capping at 30 min causes us to retry early, get another 429, and confirm to PlanIt that we are a misbehaving client — risking further escalation or IP blocks.

Honouring up to 3h gives us safe headroom for legitimate aggressive backoffs while still protecting against malformed/buggy values from future PlanIt changes.

Where: PollNextRunSchedulerOptions.cs:14 — change TimeSpan.FromMinutes(30) to TimeSpan.FromHours(3). Update the XML doc comment on the class (line 6) to match. Update PollNextRunSchedulerTests where the cap is asserted.

Bootstrap interaction is safe: bootstrap's safety-net skips reseed when a scheduled message is on the queue (verified via Safety-net skipped reseed traces 2026-04-24), so a long-future scheduled message keeps bootstrap no-opping for the full wait.

Pairs with: companion bead tc-6nkn (emit Retry-After metric) — ship together.

Acceptance: cap raised, tests updated, no regressions in PollNextRunSchedulerTests / PollTriggerOrchestratorTests. After deploy, /verify-polling cycles_with_first_429 should approach 0 (assuming the metric bead confirms PlanIt is the source of large backoff values).
notes: COMPLETED: Raised RetryAfterCap default from 30 min to 3 hours in PollNextRunSchedulerOptions.cs:14, updated XML doc comment, updated PollNextRunSchedulerTests cap test (renamed to Should_CapRetryAfterAt3Hours, retryAfter bumped to 5h to actually trigger cap, added explicit assertion that the option default is 3h). All 413 application tests pass. IN PROGRESS: nothing — bead complete pending merge. NEXT: orchestrator merges PR and closes bead. Pairs with tc-6nkn (already on this branch). BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: Retained RetryAfter * cap test rather than splitting into two tests. Used 5h retryAfter (not 4h) to leave a safe margin against the cap.

title: Emit Retry-After header value as polling metric
description:
Spec: When PlanIt returns 429, record the parsed Retry-After value as a metric so we can root-cause first-call-429 cycles in /verify-polling.

Why: We currently capture rate_limited count but not the actual Retry-After value PlanIt sends. /verify-polling 2026-04-24 surfaced 3+ cycles where the next scheduled run hit 429 immediately on its first PlanIt call (cycles_with_first_429). We hypothesise PlanIt may be sending aggressive >30min backoffs that hit our RetryAfterCap, but cannot confirm without seeing the actual values.

Where: emit metric at PollPlanItCommandHandler.cs:241 (the PlanItRateLimitException catch). Use a histogram or gauge named towncrier.polling.retry_after_seconds with tags:
- header_present (true/false) — distinguish null Retry-After from a small value
- authority_code — for diagnosing per-authority patterns

Acceptance: After deploy, /verify-polling can query the metric and report PlanIt's actual Retry-After distribution. The handler test PollPlanItCommandHandlerMetricsTests should cover both branches (header present, header absent).

Related: companion bead raises RetryAfterCap from 30m to 3h to honour aggressive backoffs.
notes: COMPLETED: emit towncrier.polling.retry_after_seconds histogram on 429 from PollPlanItCommandHandler. Tagged with cycle.type, polling.authority_code, header_present (true/false). Records 0 with header_present=false when PlanIt omits Retry-After. Two new tests in PollPlanItCommandHandlerMetricsTests.cs cover both branches. IN PROGRESS: nothing — ready for orchestrator to merge. NEXT: companion bead raises RetryAfterCap from 30m to 3h (separate bead). BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) chose Histogram<double> over Gauge — gives P50/P90/P99 distribution analysis; (2) emit zero-value sample for absent header so the metric is always present in dashboards and 'no header' is distinguishable from 'small backoff'; (3) recorded inside both 429 catch blocks (typed PlanItRateLimitException and the HttpRequestException 429 fallback).

title: Split PollState.LastPollTime from HighWaterMark so quiet authorities don't starve scheduler
description:
Spec: docs/specs/poll-state-split-last-poll-time.md

Root cause: PollPlanItCommandHandler.cs:290 saves highWaterMark (= max LastDifferent seen, typically days-old for quiet LPAs) as LastPollTime. CosmosPollStateStore.cs:86 then sorts by LastPollTime ascending. Quiet authorities (e.g. prod authority 156, 6d 14h stale per /verify-polling 2026-04-24) are re-selected as stalest every cycle, starving others.

Fix: add HighWaterMark as a separate field on PollState; always set LastPollTime=now on any non-skip branch; use HighWaterMark as the PlanIt different_start cursor. Backward compat via read-fallback to old LastPollTime when HighWaterMark is absent.

title: Emit oldest-HWM age metric from poll orchestrator + wire into /verify-polling
description: Polling pipeline exposes no way to check how far behind the slowest authority is. Emit towncrier.polling.oldest_hwm_age_seconds (gauge) and oldest_hwm_authority_id (dimension/tag) from the orchestrator at cycle_completed time. Update the /verify-polling skill to query this metric and report the lag. Spec: discussed in conversation 2026-04-24 — uses App Insights customMetrics, same emission pattern as existing counters in PollCycleHandler. See /docs/specs/polling-hwm-metric.md if created.

title: Alternative ACR cleanup strategy for Basic SKU (scheduled manifest purge)
description: tc-v467 tried to enable RetentionPolicy via Pulumi but Policies are Premium-only. With Basic SKU we need a scheduled cleanup (e.g. GitHub Actions cron running 'az acr manifest list-metadata' + 'az acr manifest delete' for untagged manifests > 30 days). Goal unchanged: keep acrtowncriershared below the 10 GB Basic quota. Premium upgrade would cost ~£12/mo — more than the overage we're trying to save.
notes: Superseded by tc-rrj8 (commit ef01f8a, PR #310). The post-push 'keep newest 5 manifests' prune in .github/actions/acr-build-push/action.yml achieves the same goal (keep acrtowncriershared below 10 GB Basic quota) more aggressively than a scheduled age-based purge. No scheduled cleanup workflow needed.

title: Upgrade OpenTelemetry packages — close NU1902 GHSA-g94r-2vxg-569j
description:
Hotfix #292 demoted NU1902 from error to warning to ship the prod fix. Need to actually upgrade OTel packages (1.15.x → patched version) and revert the suppression in api/Directory.Build.props.

References:
- Suppression introduced: PR #292 commit 3074edd

OpenTelemetry.Exporter.InMemory in town-crier.web.tests already uses floating 1.15.* and resolves to 1.15.3. Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.Exporter 1.7.0 unchanged (already latest; transitively resolves Extensions.Hosting up to 1.15.3). Removed WarningsNotAsErrors NU1902 block from api/Directory.Build.props. Build clean (0 warnings, 0 errors). All unit test suites pass: domain 31/31, application 433/433, infrastructure 277/277, web 67/67. Verified OpenTelemetry.Api 1.15.3 (patched) is now the resolved transitive version across all 4 projects. IN PROGRESS: none. NEXT: orchestrator to merge. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: Chose 1.15.2 for AspNetCore (latest available 1.15.x — 1.15.3 not yet published) and 1.15.1 for Http instrumentation (latest stable). All paths now satisfy the GHSA-g94r-2vxg-569j requirement that OpenTelemetry.Api >= 1.15.3 transitively. Integration tests fail without env vars but that is preexisting external/end-to-end behaviour, unrelated to this change.

title: Worker safety-net mode: publish bootstrap message if queue empty
description:
## Context

tc-gaxg landed `WORKER_MODE=safety-net` as the timer-driven fallback path in `town-crier.worker/Program.cs`. It runs the `PollPlanItCommandHandler` directly under the Cosmos lease guard. The original intent (missed during bead decomposition) was for the safety-net to also **re-seed the Service Bus queue** if it's empty — so the SB-coordinated cycle self-heals.

Currently in prod (verified 2026-04-21 post v0.9.3):
- `job-tc-poll-prod` (Event trigger, `WORKER_MODE=poll-sb`) is primed but dormant — queue is empty, KEDA never scales.
- `job-tc-poll-safety-net-prod` (Schedule `*/30 * * * *`, WORKER_MODE unset/safety-net) runs timer path, does NOT publish next SB message.

Result: SB cycle never bootstraps. Polling still happens, but only via the safety-net path every 30 min. The whole point of the SB-coordinated cycle (jittered adaptive scheduling via `PollNextRunScheduler`) is bypassed.

## What to change

In `api/src/town-crier.worker/Program.cs` in the `safety-net` branch (around the existing handler invocation):

1. After `PollPlanItCommandHandler` completes successfully (and the Cosmos lease is released), check the queue state.
2. If the queue has no active or scheduled messages, **publish a next poll-trigger message** using the same `PollTriggerOrchestrator` / `PollNextRunScheduler` / `IPollTriggerQueue` chain the poll-sb path uses. Compute `ScheduledEnqueueTimeUtc` via the orchestrator so the delay is jittered/adaptive.
3. Do not publish if a message already exists (any of: active, scheduled, in-flight). That avoids duplicate seeds if two safety-net runs race.

## Queue-empty check

`IServiceBusRestClient` doesn't currently expose a peek or count primitive (it's Publish/ReceiveOne/Complete only). Two options:
- **a)** Add a `PeekAsync` / `GetMessageCountAsync` method to `IServiceBusRestClient` (REST: `GET /{queue}/messages/head` or management REST for counts).
- **b)** Attempt to `ReceiveOneAsync` with a very short timeout. If it returns null → queue empty, publish seed. If it returns a message → abandon the lock (don't complete) so the real poll-sb job picks it up.

Option (b) has a TOCTOU window (another worker could grab the message between our abandon and re-peek) but is acceptable for a seed — worst case we publish a duplicate, the queue has max delivery count 10 and DLQ-on-expire, and `PollTriggerOrchestrator` is idempotent under the lease guard.

Recommend (a) for clarity — a small addition to the REST client.

## Acceptance

- Unit test in `town-crier.worker.tests` (or integration tests) covers: safety-net run on empty queue publishes a bootstrap message with a scheduled enqueue time.
- Unit test covers: safety-net run on non-empty queue does NOT publish.
- Unit test covers: a failure to check the queue does NOT prevent the handler from running (the safety-net's primary job is to poll, reseeding is best-effort).
- `dotnet build` clean, `dotnet test` all green.

## Acceptance — post-deploy

- After next CD Production tag, within 30 min (one safety-net cron tick) the queue `poll` on `sb-town-crier-prod` has `activeMessages + scheduledMessages > 0`.
- KEDA scales `job-tc-poll-prod` and it starts consuming.
- Logs show: `poll-sb` job receiving → handling → publishing-next → acking.

## Out of scope

- Changing the safety-net schedule cadence.
- Any Pulumi changes.
- App Insights alerting on prolonged queue emptiness (file separately if wanted).

title: Pulumi: set WORKER_MODE=poll-sb env var on job-tc-poll-prod
description:
## Context

CD Production for v0.9.2 succeeded and provisioned the Service Bus poll queue (PT5M lock), RBAC grant, Event trigger on job-tc-poll-prod, and safety-net job. However verification of the deployed state on 2026-04-21 showed that **neither job has `WORKER_MODE` env var set**.

The worker code (town-crier.worker/Program.cs, landed in tc-gaxg) branches on `WORKER_MODE`:
- `poll-sb` → Service-Bus-triggered loop (receive → handler → publish-next → ack)
- `safety-net` or **unset** → timer-driven path (direct handler call with Cosmos lease guard)

Since `job-tc-poll-prod` (Event trigger on KEDA azure-servicebus) has no `WORKER_MODE`, when KEDA scales it up in response to a queue message the worker will run the timer-driven path, ignore the message, and the message will time out → redeliver → eventually DLQ after 10 delivery attempts.

## What to change

Grep for where env vars are set on the `job-tc-poll-{env}` Container App Job in `infra/` (likely `EnvironmentStack.cs` near the Service Bus additions). Add:

```csharp
new EnvironmentVarArgs { Name = "WORKER_MODE", Value = "poll-sb" }
```

Only on the main Event-triggered job, NOT on the safety-net job (which intentionally uses the unset/timer path).

## Acceptance

- `dotnet build` clean in `/infra`.
- After next CD Production tag: `az containerapp job show -n job-tc-poll-prod -g rg-town-crier-prod --query 'properties.template.containers[0].env[?name==\`WORKER_MODE\`]'` returns `[{name: WORKER_MODE, value: poll-sb}]`.
- `job-tc-poll-safety-net-prod` still has no WORKER_MODE (unset = timer path).

## Follow-up (out of scope for this bead)

- Bootstrap: the queue is currently empty. Once WORKER_MODE is correct, we still need one initial message to kick off the cycle. Options: (a) manual publish via portal, (b) have safety-net path publish a next SB message on success, (c) startup hook. File separately after this ships.

title: Pulumi: reduce poll queue LockDuration to 5m for Basic tier
description:
## Context

CD Production on v0.9.1 failed creating queue `sbq-poll-prod` on `sb-town-crier-prod` namespace:

```
Status=400 Code="MessagingGatewayBadRequest"
The supplied lock time exceeds the allowed maximum of '5' minutes.
Actual value was 00:10:00. Parameter name: LockDuration.
```

`sb-town-crier-prod` is a **Basic tier** Service Bus namespace, which caps LockDuration at 5 minutes. The Pulumi code specifies 10 minutes (from tc-q8t5 bead spec, which contradicted itself).

## What to change

- File: `infra/...` (wherever `sbq-poll-prod` queue is declared; grep for `LockDuration` or `sbq-poll`).
- Change `LockDuration` from `TimeSpan.FromMinutes(10)` (or equivalent ISO-8601 `PT10M`) to `PT5M` / 5 minutes.

## Acceptance

- Pulumi preview shows the queue creating with `lockDuration: PT5M`.
- `dotnet build` clean in `/infra`.
- Next CD Production run on a new tag completes without the 400 MessagingGatewayBadRequest.

## Out of scope

- Upgrading the namespace to Standard tier.
- Other Pulumi fixes.

## Related

- tc-q8t5 (prod apply — unblocked once this ships AND CD re-runs).
- User Access Administrator grant on rg-town-crier-prod for the GH Actions SP: already applied manually 2026-04-21 via az CLI.

title: Complete tc-vboy: Program.cs wiring, SB poll entrypoint, integration test
description:
## Context

Follow-up to tc-vboy (blocked 2026-04-21 with partial delivery). The adapter (`ServiceBusPollTriggerQueue`) and DI extension (`AddPollingInfrastructure`) landed cleanly in the main branch with 242 unit tests passing. This bead closes the remaining scope with architectural decisions made 2026-04-21.

## Depends on

- tc-vboy (adapter + DI merged; bead still marked blocked pending this work — close tc-vboy once this ships).

## What needs to ship

### 1. DI wiring in `town-crier.worker/Program.cs` (NOT web)

Add after the existing polling DI block (around line 186):

```csharp
builder.Services.AddServiceBusRestClient(builder.Configuration);
builder.Services.AddPollingInfrastructure(builder.Configuration);
```

**Do NOT wire these in `town-crier.web/Program.cs`** — the web API host does not consume or publish the poll queue. Adding them there would create an unused DI registration and an unnecessary Service Bus AAD scope grant.

### 2. SB-triggered poll entrypoint as a second run mode in the worker

Inside `town-crier.worker/Program.cs`, branch on env var `WORKER_MODE`:

- `WORKER_MODE=poll-sb` → new SB-triggered loop: receive one message via `IPollTriggerQueue.ReceiveOneAsync` → run `PollPlanItCommandHandler` → hand outcome to `PollTriggerOrchestrator` → orchestrator computes next delay via `PollNextRunScheduler` and publishes next message via `IPollTriggerQueue.PublishAsync` with `ScheduledEnqueueTimeUtc` → ack (`CompleteAsync`). **Publish-before-ack semantics must hold** (from tc-8uoj).
- `WORKER_MODE=safety-net` (or unset for backwards compat) → the existing timer-driven path that calls `PollPlanItCommandHandler` directly, relying on the Cosmos lease guard.
- On `PlanItRateLimitException` in the SB path: bubble Retry-After, publish next message with that delay, ack.

Keep it in one project — two run modes, matches the existing pattern.

### 3. Integration test in `town-crier.integration-tests`

Not in infra tests (would require copying 8 fakes). Not a brand-new project (overkill for one test). The existing `town-crier.integration-tests` project already has cross-layer refs and is the right home.

Test scenarios (fake `IServiceBusRestClient`):
- Receive → handler success → publish-next with computed ScheduledEnqueueTimeUtc → ack. Assert publish happens BEFORE ack.
- Receive → handler rate-limit (`PlanItRateLimitException`) → publish-with-retry-after → ack.
- Receive empty queue (null) → no handler run, no publish, no ack.

## Acceptance

- `dotnet build -c Release` clean: 0 warnings 0 errors with `<IsAotCompatible>true</IsAotCompatible>` preserved on worker + infra.
- `dotnet publish src/town-crier.worker -r linux-x64 -c Release` clean — no IL trim warnings.
- All existing unit tests green.
- New integration test green (the 3 scenarios above).
- Worker `poll-sb` mode can be selected via env var and wires end-to-end with real adapter + fake REST client.

## Out of scope

- tc-q8t5 (prod apply: queue provisioning, RBAC grant, job trigger swap, safety-net job) — separate bead.

## References

- tc-vboy notes (blocked 2026-04-21) for what was shipped.
- `api/src/town-crier.worker/Program.cs` lines 108–260 — existing poll timer loop, DI layout.
- `api/tests/town-crier.integration-tests/` — existing fixture pattern.

comments.author: AmyDe
comments.text: Implementation decision: the bead directs integration-tests project but that project has NO project references (it's a live-API e2e test suite, not a cross-layer in-process test project). The bead's factual premise was wrong. Placing the 3 integration scenarios in infrastructure.tests instead because (a) InternalsVisibleTo already grants access to IServiceBusRestClient, (b) FakeServiceBusRestClient already exists there, (c) no new project references or fake-copying needed. Test will exercise real ServiceBusPollTriggerQueue + real PollTriggerOrchestrator + real PollPlanItCommandHandler with fake REST client + fakes for application-layer handler dependencies copied in minimally.

title: Complete tc-4g7c prod apply: poll queue, RBAC, job trigger swap, safety-net job
description:
## Context

tc-4g7c was closed on the basis of Pulumi code landing in main, but the prod apply was never run. Audit on 2026-04-21 via az showed only the Service Bus namespace exists (and that's in ukwest — tc-ds1e). Everything else the bead promised is absent from prod. Polling is still running on the old Schedule trigger + pre-#275 worker image, which is fine as a holding pattern but means the new Service-Bus-driven loop is not live.

This bead is the execution step: run `pulumi up` against the prod stack once the worker image containing tc-vboy's adapter + DI wiring is published to ACR.

## Depends on

- tc-vboy (adapter + DI + entrypoint) — the image must contain the `IPollTriggerQueue` implementation or the ACA job will throw on startup when the DI container resolves the port.
- tc-ds1e (namespace location to uksouth) — should ideally land in the same apply so the recreated namespace comes up in the right region and the queue/RBAC/env-vars all target it.

## What the prod apply must produce

1. **Queue `poll`** on `sb-town-crier-prod`:
   - Basic tier (namespace is Basic).
   - Default message TTL: 1h.
   - Lock duration: 10 min.
   - Max delivery count: 10.
   - Dead-lettering on message expiration: enabled.
   - Verify with `az servicebus queue show -n poll --namespace-name sb-town-crier-prod -g rg-town-crier-prod`.

2. **RBAC grant**: `Azure Service Bus Data Owner` on the namespace scope for managed identity `id-town-crier-cosmos-data` (principal `09dd6994-a3d8-4e83-abbd-c4d82888a700`).
   - Verify with `az role assignment list --assignee 09dd6994-a3d8-4e83-abbd-c4d82888a700 --scope <namespace resource id>`.

3. **`job-tc-poll-prod` reconfigured**:
   - Trigger type: `Event` with KEDA `azure-servicebus` scale rule targeting `sb-town-crier-prod/poll`.
   - New image tag (output of the worker image build on the commit containing tc-vboy).
   - Env vars added: `ServiceBus__Namespace=sb-town-crier-prod`, `ServiceBus__QueueName=poll`.
   - `AZURE_CLIENT_ID=739d5a0a-00a5-4f21-a55f-de1857a73b02` already present — keep.
   - Old schedule trigger removed.

4. **`job-tc-poll-safety-net-prod` created**:
   - Schedule trigger, cron `*/30 * * * *`.
   - Same image as the main poll job.
   - Same env + identity assignments, minus any Service-Bus-specific bits not needed by the direct-handler path (or include them — cheap either way; the safety-net path just doesn't use the queue).
   - Calls the safety-net entrypoint that skips the orchestrator and relies on the Cosmos lease guard.

## Acceptance

- Prod `pulumi up` succeeds (run via whatever mechanism currently triggers prod apply — do not deploy directly via CLI per CLAUDE.md).
- All 8 items from the 2026-04-21 audit checklist turn green:
  1. Namespace in uksouth (via tc-ds1e).
  2. Queue `poll` exists with the specified config.
  3. `job-tc-poll-prod` on Event trigger with the new image.
  4. `job-tc-poll-safety-net-prod` exists with cron `*/30 * * * *`.
  5. Recent executions of both jobs succeed (check `az containerapp job execution list` for 24h after deploy).
  6. Role assignment present.
  7. Env vars populated on the running job.
  8. Logs show the worker receiving from the queue, publishing next M, and acking — no startup DI exceptions.

## Out of scope

- Worker image build — that's downstream of tc-vboy merging; CI handles it.
- Namespace region fix — tc-ds1e.
- Rolling back to the old Schedule trigger — not needed; old path stays intact until apply.

## Rollback plan

If the apply fails or the new image crash-loops: `pulumi up` with the trigger reverted on `job-tc-poll-prod`. Because the old code path is what's currently running and the namespace/queue are empty, rollback is a single Pulumi change with no data loss. The worker image tag would need to revert to `4e0f6bb3d8b80b955c1c4327f7694ffec2e2ca47` to match the old-path code.
notes:
Autopilot 2026-04-21: cannot execute. Bead is prod-apply (pulumi up + Azure verification), not a code change. CLAUDE.md and bead itself prohibit direct CLI deployment. Requires human to trigger CI/CD prod apply.
Autopilot 2026-04-21: both prod-apply blockers now resolved — (1) LockDuration fixed to 5m via tc-q2ts (merged to autopilot/2026-04-21T210348Z, pending ship/tag); (2) User Access Administrator on rg-town-crier-prod granted to github-actions SP 8efcb7cf-f17e-4a93-aab5-df7bc3c2c2cc via az CLI. Next tagged release should complete CD Production end-to-end.
2026-04-21 post-apply verification on v0.9.2: queue (PT5M, 10 delivery, DLQ-on-expire, Active), RBAC (Azure Service Bus Data Owner on namespace to id-town-crier-cosmos-data), Event trigger + new worker image on job-tc-poll-prod, safety-net job (cron */30 * * * *) — all confirmed via az. REMAINING GAP: WORKER_MODE=poll-sb env var missing on job-tc-poll-prod — filed as tc-mqxt. Without it the worker runs timer path and ignores SB messages. Queue is currently empty so no DLQ yet; safety-net job continues polling on schedule.

title: ServiceBusPollTriggerQueue adapter + polling DI wiring + poll entrypoint
description:
## Context

Supersedes the remaining half of tc-6ty2. With tc-7hsp providing an AOT-clean `IServiceBusRestClient`, this bead wires it into the polling chain and turns on end-to-end Service Bus-driven polling in prod.

## Depends on

- tc-7hsp (ServiceBusRestClient + auth) — must land first so `IServiceBusRestClient` is available for DI.

## What needs to ship

1. `api/src/town-crier.infrastructure/Polling/ServiceBusPollTriggerQueue.cs` — sealed, implements `IPollTriggerQueue` (port from application layer). Thin wrapper over `IServiceBusRestClient`:
   - `PublishAsync(IPollTriggerMessage, DateTimeOffset scheduledEnqueueTimeUtc, ct)` → serialises payload via source-gen `JsonTypeInfo`, calls `IServiceBusRestClient.PublishAsync` on the configured queue name.
   - `ReceiveOneAsync(ct)` → calls `IServiceBusRestClient.ReceiveOneAsync`, wraps body + lock URL into an `IPollTriggerMessage` instance with an `AckAsync` callback that invokes `CompleteAsync` on the lock URL.

2. `api/src/town-crier.infrastructure/Polling/PollingServiceExtensions.cs` — new `AddPollingInfrastructure(IServiceCollection, IConfiguration)` extension wiring:
   - `CosmosPollingLeaseStore` as `IPollingLeaseStore` (already shipped in tc-57k4).
   - `SystemRandomPollJitter` as `IPollJitter` (already shipped in tc-57k4).
   - `ServiceBusPollTriggerQueue` as `IPollTriggerQueue`.
   - `PollNextRunScheduler` and `PollTriggerOrchestrator` (application-layer types from tc-8uoj).

3. `api/src/town-crier.web/Program.cs` — call `services.AddServiceBusRestClient(configuration)` (from tc-7hsp) and `services.AddPollingInfrastructure(configuration)`.

4. Poll entrypoint composition. The ACA Service-Bus-triggered job entry should:
   - Receive one message via `IPollTriggerQueue.ReceiveOneAsync`.
   - Run `PollPlanItCommandHandler` (application layer).
   - Hand the outcome to `PollTriggerOrchestrator`, which uses `PollNextRunScheduler` to compute the next delay and publishes next M via `IPollTriggerQueue.PublishAsync` with ScheduledEnqueueTimeUtc.
   - Ack the received message (Complete).
   - On `PlanItRateLimitException`: bubble Retry-After, publish with that delay, ack.
   - Publish-before-ack semantics (from tc-8uoj) must be preserved.
   
   The cron safety-net job entry calls `PollPlanItCommandHandler` directly — no orchestrator, no queue publish — relying on the Cosmos lease guard to avoid overlap with the main loop.

## Acceptance

- `dotnet build -c Release` clean: 0 warnings 0 errors, `<IsAotCompatible>true</IsAotCompatible>` preserved.
- All existing unit tests green.
- Integration test (fake `IServiceBusRestClient`) exercising: receive → handler success → publish-next → ack; receive → handler rate-limit → publish-with-retry-after → ack.
- Deployed prod job (once tc-ds1e lands uksouth namespace + the queue) consumes `sb-town-crier-prod/poll` and re-enqueues itself; safety-net job at `*/30` fires a no-op when the lease is held.

## Out of scope

- The REST client itself (tc-7hsp).
- Moving the namespace to uksouth (tc-ds1e).
- Provisioning the `poll` queue (infra — tc-4g7c follow-up, still missing per today's audit).
- Application-layer logic (tc-8uoj, done).

## References

- tc-8uoj close note — application layer shape.
- tc-57k4 close note — lease store + jitter already shipped.
- tc-6ty2 (superseded) — original combined scope.
notes: Autopilot 2026-04-21: partial delivery. Shipped: ServiceBusPollTriggerQueue adapter, PollingServiceExtensions DI, tests (242 pass). MISSING: (1) Program.cs wiring (AddServiceBusRestClient + AddPollingInfrastructure), (2) ACA poll-job entrypoint composition (receive->handler->publish-next->ack with publish-before-ack + rate-limit Retry-After path), (3) end-to-end integration test. Worker got stuck in scope-decision loop on the integration test and did not complete. Needs human triage or follow-up bead.

title: ServiceBusRestClient + auth (AOT-safe, mirrors CosmosRestClient)
description:
## Context

Supersedes part of tc-6ty2. The infrastructure project has `<IsAotCompatible>true</IsAotCompatible>` which turns any trim warning into a build error, and `Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus` produces trim warnings that the autopilot worker could not resolve in one run. The project has no Cosmos SDK either — it talks to Cosmos via `CosmosRestClient` (api/src/town-crier.infrastructure/Cosmos/). Mirror that pattern for Service Bus: pure HttpClient + REST, managed identity via the already-referenced `Azure.Identity` package.

No new package references — everything needed is already in town-crier.infrastructure.csproj (Azure.Identity 1.19.0, Microsoft.Extensions.Http, Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Resilience).

## What needs to ship

New folder `api/src/town-crier.infrastructure/ServiceBus/` (or `Polling/` if co-located), mirroring the files in `Cosmos/`:

1. `ServiceBusAuthProvider.cs` — copy of `CosmosAuthProvider`. Scope: `https://servicebus.azure.net/.default`. Returns plain `Bearer {token}` (no URL-encoded type=aad wrapper; simpler than Cosmos). Caches AccessToken, refreshes 5 min before expiry, semaphore-guarded.

2. `ServiceBusRestClient.cs` (internal sealed) with methods:
   - `PublishAsync<T>(string queueName, T payload, DateTimeOffset? scheduledEnqueueTimeUtc, JsonTypeInfo<T> typeInfo, CancellationToken ct)` — POST to `/{queue}/messages?api-version=2015-01`. When scheduledEnqueueTimeUtc is set, include `BrokerProperties` header with `{"ScheduledEnqueueTimeUtc":"..."}`.
   - `ReceiveOneAsync(string queueName, TimeSpan timeout, CancellationToken ct)` — POST to `/{queue}/messages/head?timeout={seconds}&api-version=2015-01` (PeekLock). Returns body stream + lock URL (from `Location` header).
   - `CompleteAsync(Uri lockUrl, CancellationToken ct)` — DELETE {lockUrl}.
   - (Optional) `AbandonAsync(Uri lockUrl, CancellationToken ct)` — PUT {lockUrl} for retry paths.

3. `ServiceBusRestOptions.cs` — `Namespace` (e.g. `sb-town-crier-prod`), `QueueName` (e.g. `poll`). Base URL computed as `https://{Namespace}.servicebus.windows.net`.

4. `IServiceBusRestClient.cs` — internal port abstraction for testability.

5. `ServiceBusJsonSerializerContext.cs` — `[JsonSerializable(typeof(BrokerProperties))]` source-gen context for the BrokerProperties DTO. Any domain payload types use their own JsonTypeInfo passed in by the caller.

6. `ServiceBusServiceExtensions.cs` — `AddServiceBusRestClient(IServiceCollection, IConfiguration)`:
   - Read `ServiceBus:Namespace` and `ServiceBus:QueueName` (env vars `ServiceBus__Namespace` / `ServiceBus__QueueName` on the ACA job).
   - Singleton `ServiceBusAuthProvider` with `new DefaultAzureCredential()` (same pattern as Cosmos).
   - Named HttpClient `ServiceBusRest` with resilience handler: retry on 429/408/503 with exponential backoff.
   - Factory-based `IServiceBusRestClient` registration.

## Acceptance

- `dotnet build -c Release` clean: 0 warnings 0 errors with `<IsAotCompatible>true</IsAotCompatible>` preserved.
- `dotnet publish -r linux-x64 -c Release` clean — no IL trim warnings.
- Unit tests in town-crier.infrastructure.tests using a fake HttpMessageHandler cover: publish, publish with ScheduledEnqueueTimeUtc, receive-one, complete, 404 on receive (empty queue), auth header format.
- No new NuGet package references added.

## Out of scope

- The `ServiceBusPollTriggerQueue` port adapter — that's the dependent bead.
- DI wiring from Program.cs — dependent bead.
- Infrastructure (namespace/queue) — already shipped in tc-4g7c (queue still needs to be provisioned, separate concern).

## References

- `api/src/town-crier.infrastructure/Cosmos/CosmosRestClient.cs` — template.
- `api/src/town-crier.infrastructure/Cosmos/CosmosAuthProvider.cs` — token-caching pattern.
- `api/src/town-crier.infrastructure/Cosmos/CosmosServiceExtensions.cs` — DI wiring pattern.
- Service Bus REST API: https://learn.microsoft.com/rest/api/servicebus/ (send, peek-lock, delete).

description:
