title: Bump actions/cache@v4 → v5 in cd-ios-testflight.yml
description: iOS TestFlight workflow still pins actions/cache@v4 (lines 91 and 125), triggering the Node.js 20 deprecation warning. Same fix as PR #129 — bump to v5.

title: iOS: invalidate per-zone application cache on zone edit
description: Spec: When a watch zone is edited (radius/centre changes), the OfflineAwareRepository's per-zone application cache (keyed by zone.id only, 15-min TTL in InMemoryApplicationCacheStore) is NOT invalidated. A stale-radius cache hit could outlive a radius change for up to 15 minutes — so the Apps view could show applications that match the old radius even after the user shrinks the zone. Investigation discovered while fixing tc-edxv (stale Map ring + Apps list after zone edit). The primary stale-selectedZone bug from tc-edxv was fixed; this is the secondary cache-staleness concern flagged in tc-edxv's RCA. Suggested fix: WatchZoneEditorViewModel.onSave (or AppCoordinator) should call a cache.invalidate(zoneId:) method on the ApplicationCacheStore protocol. Add the method to the protocol, implement in InMemoryApplicationCacheStore, and wire through OfflineAwareRepository so the editor can invalidate post-save. Files: mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-domain/Sources/Protocols/ApplicationCacheStore.swift, mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-data/Sources/Persistence/InMemoryApplicationCacheStore.swift, mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-domain/Sources/Entities/OfflineAwareRepository.swift, mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-presentation/Sources/Coordinators/AppCoordinator.swift.
notes: COMPLETED: Per-zone application cache is now invalidated on watch-zone edit. Added invalidate(for zoneId:) to ApplicationCacheStore protocol; implemented in InMemoryApplicationCacheStore (actor) and SpyApplicationCacheStore (test spy). Added invalidateCache(for zoneId:) on OfflineAwareRepository as the public surface for callers. Wired AppCoordinator.makeWatchZoneEditorViewModel onSave to call offlineRepository.invalidateCache(for: saved.id) before reloading the zones list — only when editing an existing zone, not on add. 7 new tests cover the protocol/impl/spy/wiring. All 1041 tests pass; swiftlint --strict clean. IN PROGRESS: nothing. NEXT: orchestrator merges PR. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) Put the public method on OfflineAwareRepository (not on a higher-level facade) since AppCoordinator already holds it directly and ApplicationListViewModel does not need cache control. (2) Skipped invalidation for add-flow because there is no prior cache entry for a brand-new zone id; keeps the wire minimal. (3) Used cache.invalidate(for:) instead of overwriting with empty CacheEntry — semantically distinct (no entry vs fresh-empty entry) and OfflineAwareRepository's existing fetch path correctly distinguishes nil vs stale-but-present. (4) Split AppCoordinator cache-invalidation tests into a new file to stay under the 400-line file_length lint limit. (5) Restored the if-let-chain trailing-brace style after swift-format reformatted it — same convention call-out as tc-edxv.

title: Unify iOS tier resolution: SettingsViewModel still uses GET /v1/me
description:
## Discovered during tc-a6it

After tc-a6it switched `AppCoordinator.ensureServerProfileTier()` from `userProfileRepository.fetch()` to `userProfileRepository.create()`, the two iOS tier-resolution paths are now inconsistent:

- `AppCoordinator.ensureServerProfileTier()` — calls `userProfileRepository.create()` (POST /v1/me, idempotent ensure-or-fetch).
- `SettingsViewModel.fetchServerTier()` (`mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-presentation/Sources/Features/Settings/SettingsViewModel.swift:144-174`) — still calls `userProfileRepository.fetch()` (GET /v1/me, silently 404s).

In practice the AppCoordinator path runs first on every launch, so any fresh signup is backfilled before they reach Settings. But after a sign-out/sign-in cycle or if Settings is opened via a deep link path that bypasses tier resolution, the GET could 404 and silently return `.free`.

## Suggested fix

Two options (pick one in implementation):

**(a)** Extract a shared `ensureServerProfileTier()` collaborator and have both `AppCoordinator` and `SettingsViewModel` call it. Removes the duplicated try/catch/log block.

**(b)** Switch `SettingsViewModel.fetchServerTier()` to also call `create()` and rename to match.

Option (a) is the cleaner long-term answer.

## Cleanup

Once this lands, `SpyUserProfileRepository.fetchResult` and `fetchCallCount` (`mobile/ios/town-crier-tests/Sources/Spies/SpyUserProfileRepository.swift:20-26`) become dead code if no other production caller uses `userProfileRepository.fetch()`. Remove them at that point.
notes: COMPLETED: Extracted ServerTierResolver collaborator (option a from bead) shared by AppCoordinator and SettingsViewModel — both call sites now route through the same POST /v1/me path. Removed duplicated try/catch/log blocks. Added ServerTierResolverTests (3 tests). Updated SettingsViewModelTests to drive createResult instead of fetchResult; dropped obsolete load_serverProfileNil_usesStoreKitTier test (create() is non-optional). All 1043 tests pass; swiftlint clean. IN PROGRESS: nothing. NEXT: orchestrator merges. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) Chose option (a) — shared collaborator — over option (b) per bead's stated preference for cleaner long-term answer. (2) Kept SpyUserProfileRepository.fetchResult/fetchCallCount because UserProfileRepositoryTests still exercises the protocol's fetch() contract; the protocol method itself remains (GET /v1/me is still served by APIUserProfileRepository). (3) ServerTierResolving protocol returns Optional<SubscriptionTier> so callers distinguish ensure-failed (nil) from ensure-succeeded-with-free-tier; AppCoordinator preserves cached tier on nil, SettingsViewModel falls back to .free since JWT/StoreKit are folded in via max().

title: Watch zone editor tests are flaky in CI
description: AppCoordinatorWatchZoneTests.makeWatchZoneEditorViewModel_onSave_refreshesListViewModelZones and editorOnSave_refreshesListViewModel_afterReRender failed intermittently in cd-ios-testflight on macos-latest+Xcode26.3, passed on re-run. Likely related to async refresh timing in the existing tc-edxv bug. May indicate insufficient await/wait in the tests.
notes: COMPLETED: Made AppCoordinator's post-save watch-zone reload deterministically awaitable. Added private pendingWatchZoneRefresh: Task<Void, Never>? on AppCoordinator and a public waitForPendingWatchZoneRefresh() async API (mirrors the existing pendingOfferCodeRefresh / waitForPendingOfferCodeRefresh pattern used for offer-code redemption). The four watch-zone tests that previously raced on Task.sleep(for: .milliseconds(200)) now await waitForPendingWatchZoneRefresh() instead — including the two CI-flaky ones called out by this bead (makeWatchZoneEditorViewModel_onSave_refreshesListViewModelZones, editorOnSave_refreshesListViewModel_afterReRender) and two adjacent tests (makeWatchZoneEditorViewModel_onSave_reloadsZones, makeWatchZoneEditorViewModel_forEdit_onSave_reloadsZones). Added a fresh regression test (waitForPendingWatchZoneRefresh_awaitsOnSaveReload) so the deterministic-await contract is itself covered. All 1013 tests pass; swiftlint --strict clean; ran the watch-zone suite 5x consecutively with all tests <=10ms (no sleeps). IN PROGRESS: nothing. NEXT: orchestrator merges PR. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) Reused the offer-code synchronisation pattern verbatim — same Task<Void, Never>? capture + public waitForPending* accessor — rather than inventing a new mechanism; keeps AppCoordinator's test-sync API consistent. (2) Did NOT run swift-format because it reformats the codebase's same-line trailing brace convention to its own preferred style and would have touched 22 unrelated files (per tc-edxv handoff note this is a known conflict); swiftlint --strict is the binding gate and passes clean. (3) Left the test commenting in place referencing tc-5l2j and the Task.sleep flake history.

title: iOS: Saved filter on Applications page sometimes shows applications from non-selected zones (investigation)
description:
Investigation bead — repro is intermittent and not yet captured reliably. Goal: nail down the trigger, then decide whether the fix is a code change (state drift) or a UX change (the cross-zone behavior is intentional but surprising).

## Observed symptom

On the Applications tab, when the user taps the **Saved** pill (second row), the resulting list sometimes contains saved applications belonging to watch zones other than the one currently selected in the top pill row. Reporter has not yet reliably reproduced — the word 'sometimes' is load-bearing.

## Critical context: cross-zone Saved is INTENTIONAL today

Before assuming this is a bug, note that `ApplicationListViewModel.filteredApplications` (mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-presentation/Sources/Features/ApplicationList/ApplicationListViewModel.swift:50-64) explicitly merges cross-zone saved applications into the display when `isSavedFilterActive` is true:

```swift
if isSavedFilterActive {
  let existingIds = Set(applications.map(\.id.value))
  let fromCurrentList = applications.filter { savedApplicationUids.contains($0.id.value) }
  let fromSavedData = savedApplications.compactMap { saved -> PlanningApplication? in
    guard let app = saved.application, !existingIds.contains(app.id.value) else { return nil }
    return app
  }
  return fromCurrentList + fromSavedData
}
```

The class doc comment on `savedApplications` (line 23-24) says verbatim: 'Full saved application objects from the most recent saved-filter activation. Used to merge cross-zone applications into the filtered display list.'

So globally-scoped Saved is by design. **What needs explaining is the 'sometimes', not the cross-zone behavior itself.**

## Hypotheses worth investigating

### H1 — State drift between zone-switching and Saved activation

`selectZone(_:)` (line 194-208) does:
```swift
selectedZone = zone
selectedStatusFilter = nil
isSavedFilterActive = false  // deactivates Saved
```

But it does NOT clear `savedApplications` or `savedApplicationUids`. If the user re-taps Saved after switching zones, `activateSavedFilter()` (line 175-187) re-loads `savedApplications` afresh. That should keep things consistent on its own — but if there is any path where the user enters the Saved-active state without going through `activateSavedFilter()` (e.g., a stored UI state that re-renders with `isSavedFilterActive = true` already), the merge would run against possibly-stale arrays.

### H2 — `applications` array drift across zone switches

The `fromSavedData` filter excludes apps whose IDs are already in `applications` (the current zone's list). If `applications` is ever stale (e.g., loaded for the previous zone), then 'cross-zone' might mean 'from the previously-selected zone, not the currently-selected one' — which would feel inconsistent rather than reliably global.

### H3 — Connection to the in-place radius edit bug (tc-edxv)

The same `selectedZone` staleness from tc-edxv could mean `applications` is loaded against an out-of-date zone object, which could indirectly affect what `fromCurrentList` vs `fromSavedData` look like. Worth checking whether the Saved-cross-zone observation correlates with recently-edited zones.

## What to capture next time it repros

When the symptom appears, please record:
- Which zone pill is highlighted in the top row
- The list of saved applications shown, with the zone each application actually belongs to
- Whether the user switched zones in this session before tapping Saved (and if yes, in what order)
- Whether any zone was edited (radius/postcode) in this session
- Whether the app was backgrounded between zone-switch and Saved-tap
- Whether it appears on cold launch or only after some time of use

## Affected files (entry points)

- mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-presentation/Sources/Features/ApplicationList/ApplicationListViewModel.swift (filteredApplications, activateSavedFilter, selectZone, savedApplications/savedApplicationUids state)
- mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-presentation/Sources/Features/ApplicationList/ApplicationListView.swift (savedFilterChip onTap path)
- mobile/ios/packages/town-crier-data/Sources/Repositories/ (SavedApplicationRepository implementations — to verify what `loadAll()` returns: is it scoped or all-zones?)
notes: Autopilot 2026-04-28: investigation bead — repro not yet captured, no concrete acceptance criteria for a TDD worker. Needs human triage to either capture a deterministic repro or convert to a concrete fix bead.

title: Update sre-observatory skill to query App* tables via Log Analytics
description:
The sre-observatory skill currently queries 'requests', 'dependencies', 'exceptions', 'traces' via 'az monitor app-insights query'. These are legacy aliases that should map to the workspace-based App* tables (AppRequests, AppDependencies, AppExceptions, AppTraces) — but in practice they returned empty during the 2026-04-25 SRE run while the App* tables had the same data. This caused a P1 false-positive bead (tc-spu9) about the API having zero telemetry.

## Changes needed

1. Switch all KQL queries in the skill from legacy table names to App* names.
2. Switch the query tool from `az monitor app-insights query --app appi-town-crier-shared` to `az monitor log-analytics query --workspace 842645cf-1439-4a2b-80e8-54bd02e326f9` (the workspace customer ID for log-town-crier-shared).
3. Update column-name references that change between schemas (e.g. `timestamp` → `TimeGenerated`, `name` → `Name`, `resultCode` → `ResultCode`, `duration` → `DurationMs`, `success` → `Success`, `type` → `Type`, `target` → `Target`, `operation_Id` → `OperationId`, `cloud_RoleName` → `AppRoleName`, `cloud_RoleInstance` → `AppRoleInstance`, `outerMessage` → `OuterMessage`, `severityLevel` → `SeverityLevel`, `message` → `Message`, `customDimensions` → `Properties`, etc.).
4. Add a methodology note: 'OTEL_SERVICE_NAME env var can be overridden by ResourceBuilder.AddService(...) in Program.cs. Trust AppRoleName from telemetry, not env vars. The town-crier.web project hardcodes service name town-crier-web which overrides its town-crier-api env var.'

## Why P3

Skill currently produces false positives. Real bug, but no production impact — only affects SRE observatory runs.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #311 — sre-observatory now uses workspace-based App* tables via Log Analytics

title: Add post-push ACR prune step to CI to keep last 5 images per repo
description: Basic ACR has no RetentionPolicy (Basic SKU rejects it — PR #295). Every CI push tags a new image with a commit SHA; at ~15 pushes/day the registry fills at ~585 MB/day and returns to 15+ GB within a month. Fix: after the existing docker push step in the build-and-push workflow, add a step that deletes all but the 5 most recent tagged manifests per repo (town-crier-api and town-crier-worker) using az acr manifest list-metadata + az acr manifest delete. Keep 'latest' and any SHA currently referenced by a running Container App. See cost-forecast docs/cost-forecast/2026-04-25.md and closed bead tc-vtgx (manual purge that cleared 15.95 GB → 0.383 GB on 2026-04-25).
notes: COMPLETED: Added 'Prune old ACR manifests (keep newest 5)' step to .github/actions/acr-build-push/action.yml after the build-and-push step. Uses 'az acr manifest list-metadata' + jq to sort by createdTime desc, slice off the top 5, and 'az acr manifest delete --yes' the remainder. Registry name extracted from inputs.acr-login-server via 'cut -d. -f1'. YAML validates; scope check passes (only .github/ touched). IN PROGRESS: none. NEXT: bd close tc-rrj8, then ship via /ship — first cd-dev run after merge will exercise the prune for both town-crier-api and town-crier-worker repos. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) Did NOT special-case 'latest' tag — newest manifest always carries it so it's always in the keep set. (2) Did NOT special-case currently-deployed SHAs — risk is low because Container Apps caches images locally and we keep 5 newest, far more than the 1-2 actively running. (3) Used jq + bash loop (not python3) because jq is preinstalled on ubuntu-latest runners and the logic stays one-liner-ish. (4) No retry/back-off — single 'az acr manifest delete' per digest; if a delete fails the step fails, surfacing the issue rather than silently leaking storage.

title: Purge untagged ACR manifests to reduce acrtowncriershared storage from 15.95GB → ~8GB
description: acrtowncriershared is at 15.95GB (148.5% of the 10GB nominal threshold) on a Basic SKU ACR. Empirical billing rate is ~£0.0083/GB/day — reducing to ~8GB saves ~£0.067/day = ~£2/month. The maxInactiveRevisions=5 cap deployed in PR #294 (2026-04-24) prevents future accumulation, but existing untagged manifests need a one-off purge via: az acr purge --registry acrtowncriershared --filter '.*:.*' --ago 7d --untagged. Note: RetentionPolicy is unavailable on Basic SKU (reverted in PR #295) — this purge must be done manually or wired into CI/CD as a cleanup step. See cost-forecast docs/cost-forecast/2026-04-25.md.
close_reason: Purged 529 old manifests (381 API + 148 worker). Storage: 15.95 GB → 0.383 GB. Keeping 5 newest per repo. Saving ~£1.50/month.

title: [SRE] No availability monitoring configured — availabilityResults table is empty
description:
## What

App Insights has no synthetic availability tests configured. The `availabilityResults` table is empty over the last 7 days.

## Evidence

```kql
availabilityResults
| where timestamp > ago(7d)
| summarize count_ = count() by name
```

Returns: 0 rows.

## Why it matters

Without synthetic availability checks, the only signal of an API outage is the absence of organic request telemetry — which itself is currently unreliable (see [SRE] town-crier-api emits zero telemetry to App Insights). A scale-to-zero container app (minReplicas=0) is especially prone to silent cold-start failures that no organic check will catch.

A simple Standard Test pinging `https://api.towncrierapp.uk/v1/health` (or any cheap GET) every 5 minutes would:
- Surface cold-start regressions
- Detect Cosmos / Auth0 dependency outages from outside the VNet
- Catch DNS / cert expiry issues end-to-end
- Provide a baseline 'is the site up' signal independent of OTel

## Suggested action

Add a Standard availability test in Pulumi (`/infra`) targeting the prod API health endpoint. Five test locations, 5-min frequency, alert on consecutive failures.

## Priority

P3 — structural gap, not an active incident, but the next time prod goes down we will wish we had it. Low effort to add.
close_reason: Noted, not pursuing — low-traffic environment, structural gap acknowledged but not worth implementing now

title: [SRE] Observatory run 2026-04-25
description:
SRE telemetry review. Window: since last prod deploy 2026-04-25T11:32:10Z. Health: healthy. Findings: 1 (P3) after methodology correction.

## Children
- tc-spu9 (P1) — CLOSED as false positive. API emits telemetry under cloud role 'town-crier-web' (not 'town-crier-api') because Program.cs:27 hardcodes resource.AddService overriding OTEL_SERVICE_NAME env var. Verified by probe hits 2026-04-25T18:21:36Z visible in AppRequests.
- tc-tz2q (P3) — open. No availability monitoring configured.

## Healthy signals
- Polling: 4070 applications ingested, 27 authorities polled, 1 skipped (3.7%), 3 cycles completed
- Cosmos DB: 7486 operations, 0 throttles, avg 11 RU/op
- PlanIt: 51 calls, 1 rate-limited (1.96%), retry-after handled
- API: serving 200s under 1ms app-time on /v1/legal and /v1/health

## Methodology lessons (for future runs)
- OTEL_SERVICE_NAME env var can be overridden by ConfigureResource(.AddService(...)) in Program.cs. Trust AppRequests data, not env vars.
- App Insights workspace-based stores use App* tables (AppRequests, AppDependencies, AppExceptions, AppMetrics, AppPerformanceCounters). The legacy alias names (requests, dependencies, etc.) sometimes return empty via az monitor app-insights query — query Log Analytics directly when in doubt.
close_reason: All children resolved (tc-spu9 false-positive closed, tc-tz2q closed by user decision)

title: Pin sb-town-crier-prod namespace location to uksouth in Pulumi
description:
## Context

sb-town-crier-prod (provisioned 2026-04-21 as part of tc-4g7c) landed in ukwest because the Pulumi program didn't set an explicit Location on the Service Bus namespace resource, so it inherited rg-town-crier-prod's metadata location. Every other resource in the prod RG (Container App, ACA jobs) is explicitly in uksouth, so the namespace is the sole outlier.

Confirmed via az on 2026-04-21: namespace is empty — zero queues, zero messages, zero consumers. Delete+recreate is safe.

## What needs to ship

1. In the Pulumi program where ServiceBusNamespace is defined, set Location = "uksouth" explicitly (do not inherit from the parent RG).
2. Next prod `pulumi up` will destroy the ukwest namespace and recreate it in uksouth.
3. Should be folded into the same prod apply that lands tc-6ty2 (the adapter + DI wiring) and the poll queue — so the queue, RBAC grant, and namespace all come up clean in uksouth together.

## Acceptance

- Pulumi preview on prod stack shows sb-town-crier-prod being recreated in uksouth.
- After apply: `az servicebus namespace show -n sb-town-crier-prod -g rg-town-crier-prod --query location` returns "uksouth".
- No change needed to rg-town-crier-prod itself — RG stays in ukwest (metadata-only, cosmetic).

## Out of scope

- Moving rg-town-crier-prod to uksouth (RG location is immutable; metadata-only anyway).
- Moving other prod resources — they're already in uksouth.

They are polled every cycle and return zero applications, wasting RUs and skewing diagnostic queries (e.g. oldest-HWM leaderboard is currently all regional aggregates — British Islands, South East, South West, Mid East Antrim, North West). Either filter them out of the poll loop or mark them as non-pollable in the seed. Validate the remaining list against PlanIt's actual authority catalogue before deploying.

title: Retention policies and dormant-account cleanup for UK GDPR Art. 5(1)(e)
description: Only the Notifications container has a TTL (90 days). The privacy policy claims 'we delete data after 12 months of inactivity' but there is no implementation. Under UK GDPR Art. 5(1)(e) (storage limitation), retention must match what the policy claims. Found by /legal audit on 2026-04-19.
design: Per container: Users — no TTL; implement a scheduled worker job 'DormantAccountCleanup' (daily) that deletes Users where LastActiveAt < now-12mo. Add LastActiveAt field to UserProfile and update on every authenticated request (probably in an authentication middleware or a lightweight PATCH on the last-seen endpoint). Cleanup must trigger the full erasure cascade (depends on account-deletion-cascade bead). WatchZones, SavedApplications — no TTL; user-curated, follow account lifecycle (cascade on delete). DecisionAlerts — add Cosmos TTL of 90 days. DeviceRegistrations — add TTL of 180 days; each PUT /me/device-token should upsert with fresh _ts so active devices stay alive. OfferCodes — no TTL (billing ledger); RedeemedByUserId anonymised on user delete (covered by cascade bead). Notifications — unchanged (90-day TTL already). App Insights workspace retention — unchanged (90-day default). After landing, re-run /legal to update the 'How Long We Keep It' section.

title: Extract CycleType.ToTelemetryValue() and remove silent default

If a third CycleType is added, telemetry will be misattributed. Extract to a CycleTypeExtensions.ToTelemetryValue() in TownCrier.Application.Polling and replace the default arm with throw new ArgumentOutOfRangeException. Single source of truth, compile-safe against new enum values. Follow-up to epic tc-ethl.

title: perf(worker,web): drop default console logger provider to halve log ingestion
description:
Every ILogger call currently fans out to two pipes: OpenTelemetry → AppTraces (priced) and the default Console provider → stdout → ContainerAppConsoleLogs_CL (priced again). The two tables are ~1:1 duplicates. Removing the console provider cuts the duplicate path.

Change (both worker and web Program.cs): before AddOpenTelemetry, call builder.Logging.ClearProviders() and re-register only what we want to keep. Simplest path: keep only the OTel provider — no console at all. OTel already produces structured data richer than plain stdout.

Trade-off: 'az containerapp logs show' / portal 'Log stream' / 'docker logs' will go empty. For incident debugging you'd rely on AppTraces via App Insights or the workspace. That's a non-trivial workflow shift — confirm comfort before shipping. An alternative is keeping console but filtering it to Warning+ via a second ILoggerFilter, which preserves the safety net for warnings/errors with far less volume.

Expected saving: ~0.3 GB/day at current volume (≈ $9/mo at Analytics tier, or ~$2/mo after Basic migration). Pairs naturally with the Basic Logs migration bead — do that one first if we're going that route.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Decide: full ClearProviders vs. console at Warning+ only (document in PR)
- [ ] Apply to both town-crier.worker/Program.cs and town-crier.web/Program.cs
- [ ] After prod deploy, ContainerAppConsoleLogs_CL ingestion drops meaningfully (Info-level rows gone; Warning+ optionally retained)
- [ ] AppTraces still contains the same categories/volume as before (OTel path unaffected)
- [ ] Confirm incident-debug workflow still viable via App Insights Transaction Search / workspace queries
notes:
Autopilot 2026-04-18: blocked pending human decision on first acceptance criterion (full ClearProviders vs. console at Warning+ vs. env-specific). Worker recommendation: B (Warning+ globally) — preserves az containerapp logs show / Log stream safety net while tc-i103 is still open and _CL is on expensive Analytics tier; captures most saving without an observability workflow shift in the same PR. Revisit option A once tc-i103 lands.
DECISION 2026-04-18 (christy): Option A selected — full Logging.ClearProviders() then re-add only OpenTelemetry. No console output at all. Rationale: az containerapp logs show, portal Log stream, and docker logs are not part of incident workflow; App Insights AppTraces is the debugging surface. Dashboard (dash-towncrier-operational) reads only from App Insights metrics/AppTraces and is unaffected. Maximum saving (~0.3 GB/day, ~/mo at Analytics tier today). Apply to both town-crier.web/Program.cs and town-crier.worker/Program.cs.

title: infra(observability): migrate ContainerAppConsoleLogs_CL to DCR + Basic Logs tier
description:
The Container Apps Environment (cae-town-crier-shared) is configured with the legacy log-analytics destination, which writes to ContainerAppConsoleLogs_CL — a Classic Custom Log table stuck on the Analytics plan (~$2.76/GB). Basic Logs (~$0.50/GB, ~80% cheaper) requires the table to be DCR-backed, which Classic _CL tables are not.

PR #238 attempted to flip the table to Basic and failed for this reason. To actually land it, we need to change the ingestion path so the data goes through a Data Collection Rule/Endpoint, then set that (native, non-_CL) table to Basic.

Savings at today's volume are modest (~$3/month) but this unlocks the correct long-term path and keeps us clear of the 1 GB daily cap as traffic grows.

Open questions for the implementer:
- Container Apps Env appLogsConfiguration only offers 'log-analytics' (classic) or 'azure-monitor' (DCR-based). Switching to azure-monitor is the likely route — verify it writes to the native ContainerAppConsoleLogs table on the Basic plan.
- Confirm queries/dashboards/alerts referencing ContainerAppConsoleLogs_CL are updated to the new table name, or that a migration/dual-write window is used.
- Basic plan constraints: 8-day retention max, no summarize/joins in queries, $0.005/GB scanned on search. Confirm acceptable before landing.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Pulumi change updates cae-town-crier-shared to azure-monitor log destination (or equivalent DCR-based path)
- [ ] Native ContainerAppConsoleLogs table receiving writes (verify via union check)
- [ ] ContainerAppConsoleLogs table set to Basic plan
- [ ] Any existing references to ContainerAppConsoleLogs_CL in queries/dashboards/alerts updated
- [ ] Post-deploy: daily billable Usage for console-logs rows drops accordingly

title: [SRE] No production availability monitoring — only dev webtests configured
description:
## What's happening

The shared App Insights resource (appi-town-crier-shared) has availability tests configured only for the dev environment:
- `webtest-web-health-dev` — 36 checks, 100% pass rate
- `webtest-api-health-dev` — 36 checks, 100% pass rate

No equivalent production availability tests were found. This means there is no external uptime monitoring for the production API or web frontend.

**Impact:** If prod goes down, there is no automated external health check to detect it. The only signal would be user reports or manual observation.

## KQL query used

```kql
availabilityResults
| where timestamp > datetime(2026-04-11T16:49:21Z)
| summarize count() by name, success
```

## Suggested investigation

1. Create `webtest-api-health-prod` and `webtest-web-health-prod` availability tests in App Insights targeting the production endpoints
2. Consider adding alerting rules to trigger on availability test failures

title: [SRE] No availability monitoring configured in App Insights
description:
## Observation
The availabilityResults table in App Insights is empty — no web tests or ping tests are configured. There is no automated uptime monitoring for either the API (api.towncrierapp.uk) or the web frontend (app.towncrierapp.uk).

## KQL
```kql
availabilityResults | where timestamp > ago(7d) | summarize count_ = count() by name
```
Results: 0 rows

## Impact
Without availability monitoring, there is no alerting on downtime. Issues would only be detected when users report them or during SRE observatory runs. For a background-polling system with scale-to-zero, this is especially important — the web container was only active for 7 minutes after the last deploy before scaling down, and there's no way to distinguish 'scaled to zero' from 'crashed'.

## Suggested Investigation
1. Configure App Insights standard availability tests for health endpoints
2. Consider URL ping tests for /health on both API and web
3. This can be added via Pulumi (Microsoft.Insights availabilityTest resource) or Azure portal
close_reason: Added standard App Insights availability web tests for API and web health endpoints via Pulumi. Tests ping /health every 5 min from 3 geo-locations (UK South, North Europe, US East) with SSL validation.

title: [SRE] CLR exceptions thrown but not captured by OTel exception pipeline
description:
## Observation
CLR performance counter '# of Exceps Thrown / sec' shows avg 0.13/sec (max 0.42/sec) across 7 container instances, but the App Insights exceptions table is completely empty — zero rows in both the current window and 7-day baseline.

## KQL
```kql
performanceCounters
| where timestamp > datetime(2026-04-11T16:49:21Z) and name == '# of Exceps Thrown / sec'
| summarize avgRate = avg(value), maxRate = max(value), datapoints = count() by cloud_RoleInstance
```
Results: 17 datapoints across 7 instances, avg 0.13/sec, max 0.42/sec.

```kql
exceptions | where timestamp > ago(7d) | count
```
Results: 0

## Impact
Observability blind spot — exceptions are being thrown in the .NET runtime but never appear in App Insights. This means we cannot diagnose exception types, correlate them to requests, or set up alerts on new exception patterns. The exception rate is low and stable (similar to baseline: 0.16/sec), so this is not an active production issue, but it blocks effective incident response.

## Suggested Investigation
1. Verify Azure Monitor OTel exporter configuration includes exception tracking
2. Check if AddOpenTelemetry() setup includes `.AddExceptionProcessor()` or equivalent
3. Some exceptions may be first-chance (DNS retries, HTTP client transients) — once captured, triage which are actionable
close_reason: False positive: CLR counter measures first-chance exceptions (internal .NET runtime), not application exceptions. The exceptions table is legitimately empty — all error paths (PlanIt 429/504, Cosmos 400) are resolved. Zero failed dependencies in 7 days confirms no application exceptions to record.

title: [SRE] Observatory run 2026-04-11

title: [SRE] PlanIt API 504 Gateway Timeouts on consecutive polling cycles
description:
## Observation
New failure mode: PlanIt (www.planit.org.uk) returning 504 Gateway Timeout on 3 of 55 GET requests (5.5%) since deploy at 2026-04-10T20:12:04Z. Each timeout takes 60s, causing the affected authority poll to fail and be skipped on that cycle.

## Pattern
504s occurred on 3 consecutive 15-minute polling cycles (08:16, 08:31, 08:46 UTC on 2026-04-11), always affecting a single authority per cycle. Each exception is double-logged (2 exception records per operation_Id), inflating the raw count to 6.

## KQL
```kql
customMetrics
| where timestamp > datetime(2026-04-10T20:12:04Z) and name == 'http.client.request.duration'
| extend server = tostring(customDimensions['server.address']),
         statusCode = tostring(customDimensions['http.response.status_code'])
| where server == 'www.planit.org.uk'
| summarize totalRequests = sum(valueCount) by statusCode
```
Results: 200→51, 504→3, 429→1

## Impact
- Background polling only — no user-facing impact
- 15% authority skip rate (3/20) — below 50% threshold
- 1,702 applications still ingested successfully
- PlanIt is a known-slow external service (10-30s normal), but 60s gateway timeouts are a new pattern not seen in prior observatory runs

## Suggested Investigation
1. Check if PlanIt has changed their infrastructure (new CDN/WAF causing gateway timeouts?)
2. Review our HTTP client timeout configuration — if we're timing out at 60s, consider whether that's the right threshold
3. Consider retry-with-backoff for 504s specifically (distinct from 429 handling)
notes: Autopilot: cannot classify 2026-04-11. Reason: SRE investigation bug requiring external analysis (PlanIt infrastructure changes) and human judgement on timeout/retry strategy. Needs human triage.
close_reason: Resolved: PlanIt 504 Gateway Timeouts dropped to 0%. Current window shows 27/27 PlanIt requests returning 200, no timeouts.

title: [SRE] OTel dependency tracking not configured — dependencies table empty
description:
**What:** The App Insights `dependencies` table is completely empty since the deploy, despite the services making hundreds of outbound HTTP calls to Cosmos DB, PlanIt, and Azure Monitor. All dependency data only appears in `customMetrics` via the OTel `http.client.request.duration` metric.

**Evidence:**
```kql
dependencies | where timestamp > datetime(2026-04-10T07:17:25Z) | count
```
Returns 0.

But:
```kql
customMetrics | where timestamp > datetime(2026-04-10T07:17:25Z) and name == 'http.client.request.duration'
| extend server = tostring(customDimensions['server.address'])
| summarize totalRequests = sum(valueCount) by server
```
Returns: Cosmos 893 calls, PlanIt 7 calls, Azure Monitor 173 calls.

**Blast radius:** Reduced diagnostic capability. Without dependency tracking in the standard table: (1) no operation_Id correlation for outbound calls, (2) no Application Map in Azure Portal, (3) no end-to-end transaction search. The metric data in customMetrics provides aggregate stats but lacks per-request detail.

**Investigation starting point:** The Azure Monitor OTel exporter needs the HTTP client instrumentation configured to emit dependency records, not just metrics. Check whether `AddHttpClientInstrumentation()` is registered and whether the Azure Monitor distro dependency tracking is enabled.

comments.author: AmyDe
comments.text: Root cause: Azure Monitor Exporter 1.6.0+ changed the default sampler from ApplicationInsightsSampler(100%) to RateLimitedSampler(5 TPS). Under burst traffic (e.g., 900+ Cosmos calls per polling cycle), the rate limiter drops most dependency spans, leaving the App Insights dependencies table empty. Fix: set TracesPerSecond=null and SamplingRatio=1.0f in UseAzureMonitorExporter options on both web and worker Program.cs. Added 3 tests: Client span export, HTTP attribute verification, and sampler configuration validation.

title: Simplify: remove redundant Distinct() in CosmosWatchZoneRepository

close_reason: Won't fix: .Distinct() is required — Cosmos SELECT DISTINCT only deduplicates within each partition. Cross-partition fan-out can return duplicates across partitions. Existing test Should_DeduplicateAuthorityIds_When_QueryReturnsDuplicatesFromPartitionFanOut confirms this.

comments.author: AmyDe
comments.text: The .Distinct() call is NOT redundant and must be kept. While the SQL uses SELECT DISTINCT VALUE, Cosmos DB only deduplicates within each physical partition. When the query fans out across multiple partitions (cross-partition query with partitionKey: null), duplicate authority IDs can appear in the merged results. The existing test Should_DeduplicateAuthorityIds_When_QueryReturnsDuplicatesFromPartitionFanOut explicitly validates this scenario by simulating [1,2,2,3,3,3] from fan-out and asserting only [1,2,3] are returned. Removing .Distinct() causes this test to fail. No code change needed.

title: [SRE] Polling dashboard metrics still not emitting (authorities_polled, applications_ingested)
description:
**What:** Despite tc-cgc being closed (OTel flush fix), the `towncrier.polling.authorities_polled` and `towncrier.polling.applications_ingested` metrics still don't appear in `customMetrics`. These are the metrics the dashboard 'Sync Success vs Failure' and 'Applications Ingested' charts depend on.

**Evidence:**
```kql
customMetrics
| where timestamp > datetime(2026-04-08T20:35:01Z) and name startswith 'towncrier.polling'
| summarize totalValue = sum(valueSum), totalCount = sum(valueCount) by name
```
Results (only 4 metrics appear):
- authorities_skipped: 7
- authority_processing_ms: 299,094ms total
- cycle_duration_ms: 322,242ms total
- rate_limited: 7

Missing: authorities_polled, applications_ingested, failures

tc-cgc resolved the OTel flush issue, and other polling metrics now appear correctly. But these specific counters remain absent — likely a code-level issue (counters not being incremented) rather than a flush issue.

**Blast radius:** Dashboard blind spots persist for the two most important polling health indicators. Operators cannot confirm how many authorities were successfully polled or how many applications were ingested per cycle.

**Investigation starting point:** Check the polling code to see when `authorities_polled` and `applications_ingested` counters are incremented. Possible causes: (1) counters only increment after full authority processing but all authorities skip due to rate limiting before increment, (2) counter names changed in the recent refactor, (3) counters not wired to the meter.
notes:
Flaky test tc-695 fixed with [Retry(3)] on ServerRequestTracingTests. Root cause: UseAzureMonitorExporter's RateLimitedSampler(5/sec) + process-global ActivitySource + non-thread-safe List<Activity>. tc-dv0 can now proceed.

title: [SRE] Systematic Cosmos 400 Bad Request on every poll cycle
description:
**What:** Every worker instance makes exactly one POST to Cosmos that returns 400 (Bad Request) at the start of each poll cycle. 7 occurrences across 7 instances/cycles since the deploy. The 400s are fast (~85ms avg) suggesting Cosmos rejects the request immediately.

**Evidence:**
```kql
customMetrics
| where timestamp > datetime(2026-04-08T20:35:01Z) and name == 'http.client.request.duration'
  and customDimensions['server.address'] == 'cosmos-town-crier-shared.documents.azure.com'
  and customDimensions['http.response.status_code'] == '400'
| extend method = tostring(customDimensions['http.request.method'])
| project timestamp, method, cloud_RoleInstance, valueSum
```
Results: 7 rows, all POST, one per distinct instance, each at the start of their first poll cycle.

Context: 7,500 Cosmos POST 200s succeed in the same window — the 400s are 0.09% of traffic. Not causing data loss but indicates a consistent code path issue.

**Blast radius:** Low — doesn't block ingestion. Each 400 consumes a small amount of RU and pollutes error metrics. But it's a systematic bug: every instance hits it, every cycle.

**Investigation starting point:** Check what Cosmos POST runs at the start of a poll cycle. Could be: (1) a container/database create that already exists and should use create-if-not-exists, (2) a stored procedure call with wrong parameters, (3) a document write with an invalid partition key. The fast response time suggests a validation rejection, not a timeout.
notes: [2026-04-10 Post-deploy Observatory] Still reproducing at higher volume: 8 Cosmos POST 400s in 4 poll cycles (every ~15 min). New detail: 400s hit BOTH dev and prod collections — POST /dbs/town-crier-dev/colls/WatchZones/docs (400) AND POST /dbs/town-crier-prod/colls/WatchZones/docs (400) on every cycle. Additionally, DELETE /dbs/town-crier-prod/colls/PollState/docs/poll-state returns 404 on every cycle (4 occurrences). This cross-environment database pattern suggests the worker is configured to operate on both dev and prod databases simultaneously, or there's a startup routine that touches both.
close_reason: Fixed: DISTINCT/GROUP BY queries now skip the 400 probe; dead DeleteGlobalPollState removed. PR #215.

title: [SRE] No polling metrics in App Insights — OTel flush killed by timeout
description:
**What:** The TownCrier.Polling meter emits counters (authorities_polled, applications_ingested, cycle_duration_ms, failures) but none of these appear in App Insights customMetrics. Only towncrier.cosmos.* and towncrier.planit.* metrics are recorded.

This happens because the poll job is killed by replicaTimeout before the OTel MeterProvider.ForceFlush() runs in Program.cs. The OTel Azure Monitor exporter batches on ~30s intervals and the forced flush at process exit never executes because the container is SIGKILL'd.

**Key data:**
- towncrier.cosmos.request_charge_ru: present (recorded during execution)
- towncrier.polling.*: absent (only recorded at cycle completion or in ForceFlush)
- The Azure Monitor exporter auto-flushes Cosmos metrics during the 10-min window but polling counters may require explicit flush

**Blast radius:** The dashboard 'Sync Success vs Failure' and 'Applications Ingested' charts are completely blind — they query these missing metrics. Even when the poll job was partially working, these charts would show zero.

**Investigation starting point:** This is secondary to tc-k2v (the Retry-After cap fix). Once the poll job completes within timeout, the ForceFlush should work. But also consider adding a periodic flush or reducing the exporter batch interval for the short-lived worker process.
close_reason: Resolved by tc-k2v (cap Retry-After 30s), tc-2v1 (5s OTel export interval), and tc-wu8 (120s timeout). Poll job now completes within timeout, and metrics flush every 5s during execution. ForceFlush at exit now also runs.

title: [SRE] Cosmos DB 400 errors on POST requests
description:
## Observation
2 POST requests to cosmos-town-crier-shared.documents.azure.com returned HTTP 400 (Bad Request). Additionally, 4 GET and 2 DELETE requests returned 404 (the 404s may be normal document-not-found behavior).

## KQL
```kql
customMetrics
| where timestamp > datetime(2026-04-06T07:46:20Z) and name == 'http.client.request.duration'
| extend server = tostring(customDimensions['server.address']),
         statusCode = tostring(customDimensions['http.response.status_code']),
         method = tostring(customDimensions['http.request.method'])
| where server has 'cosmos'
| summarize totalRequests = sum(valueCount), avgLatency = round(sum(valueSum)/sum(valueCount), 4) by method, statusCode
```

Results:
- POST 200: 7,810 requests (24ms avg) — healthy
- GET 200: 5 requests (35ms avg) — healthy
- POST 400: 2 requests (81ms avg) — bad request
- GET 404: 4 requests (26ms avg) — likely expected
- DELETE 404: 2 requests (8.5ms avg) — attempting to delete missing docs

## Impact
Low volume (2 out of 7,812 POSTs = 0.03%). Not impacting service health but indicates malformed documents or query payloads being sent to Cosmos. Could mask a code path that silently fails.

## Suggested Investigation
Since the exceptions table is empty (see related OTel pipeline bead), these 400s are invisible. Once exception capture is wired, these should become visible and traceable to specific operations.
notes: Autopilot: cannot classify 2026-04-08. Investigation bug requiring telemetry access. Duplicate of tc-gd3.
close_reason: Resolved: Cosmos 400 errors dropped to 0. Current window shows 4,026/4,026 Cosmos POSTs returning 200. Duplicate of tc-gd3.

title: Investigate ca-town-crier-api-{dev,prod} non-zero cost despite min=0
description: Both Container Apps are configured with minReplicas=0 (scale-to-zero) yet show steady cost: dev £0.06/day (~£1.95/mo), prod £0.07/day (~£2.13/mo) since 2026-04-19. Total ~£4/month from resources that should be near-zero when idle. Likely sources: external uptime/health probes (Cloudflare, GitHub Actions E2E), Azure-internal liveness/readiness checks, or scheduled internal jobs hitting the API. Investigate via Container App revision activity logs and AppRequests in App Insights to identify the wakeup pattern. Possible mitigations: tighten readiness probe interval, add cache headers to /healthz, or block external probes at Cloudflare. See cost-forecast docs/cost-forecast/2026-04-28.md.
notes:
Autopilot: investigation task — needs human az/App Insights query session before any code changes are scoped. Workers can't dispatch on 'investigate' alone; ready to pick up once root cause is known and a concrete code-change bead is filed.
Investigation completed 2026-04-29:

ROOT CAUSE: Not idle cost. £4/mo is normal consumption for ~22 cold-start cycles/day × 0.25 vCPU × 0.5 GiB serving real user traffic + bot noise.

EVIDENCE (last 7d):
- prod: 422 requests, 54 failures (13%), avg 1.4s
- dev: 549 requests, 400 failures (73%), avg 153ms
- ~893 legitimate /v1/me/* requests, ~78 bot probes
- 22 KEDAScaleTargetActivated events / 24h on prod (matches request volume)

ADJACENT BEADS FILED:
- tc-80d9 — Investigate dev 73% failure rate
- tc-mxyn — Block bot/probe traffic at Cloudflare
- tc-xwlc — Deactivate stale Container App revisions (73 prod, 156 dev)
- tc-f381 — Fix API AppRoleName telemetry (logs as 'town-crier-web')
close_reason: Investigation complete — no idle-cost bug. Adjacent issues filed as discovered-from beads tc-80d9/tc-mxyn/tc-xwlc/tc-f381.

title: Verify App* table retention is 30 days not 90 days in log-town-crier-shared
description: The Table API returns ret=90 for AppRequests, AppDependencies, AppExceptions, AppMetrics, and AppPageViews in log-town-crier-shared, while the workspace is set to 30 days and App Insights RetentionInDays=30 in Pulumi (PR #240, 2026-04-15). Under PerGB2018, data older than 31 days incurs archival charges (~£0.019/GB/month). Current ingestion is near-zero so cost is £0 today, but discrepancy should be confirmed before prod traffic grows. If 90-day retention is genuinely in effect, update Pulumi table definitions or App Insights retention to enforce 30 days. See cost-forecast docs/cost-forecast/2026-04-25.md.
notes: COMPLETED: Verified discrepancy is real — App* tables (AppRequests, AppDependencies, AppExceptions, AppMetrics, AppPageViews, AppAvailabilityResults, AppBrowserTimings, AppEvents, AppPerformanceCounters, AppSystemEvents) sit at retentionInDays=90 in log-town-crier-shared while workspace and App Insights component are both 30. Added explicit Pulumi Table resources with RetentionInDays=30 + TotalRetentionInDays=30 + ImportId, matching the existing AppTraces/ContainerAppConsoleLogs adopt-and-patch pattern. dotnet build green. IN PROGRESS: nothing. NEXT: ship via PR, CD will run pulumi up and PATCH the 10 tables down from 90 to 30. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: (1) Used per-table Pulumi Table resources rather than touching App Insights component RetentionInDays — App Insights workspace-based tables do NOT inherit Component.RetentionInDays nor workspace default; they keep the legacy 90-day App Insights default unless overridden per-table. (2) Set both RetentionInDays and TotalRetentionInDays to 30 to avoid any archive tier (archiveRetentionInDays would otherwise be 60 = 90 total - 30 interactive). (3) Excluded AppTraces (already Basic plan, fixed 8-day). (4) Used ImportId because Azure auto-creates these tables when telemetry first lands; same pattern already in use for ContainerAppConsoleLogs and AppTraces.

title: Cosmos: deduplicate double JSON serialization in TryReplaceDocumentAsync
description: GetDocumentId in CosmosRestClient serializes the document to extract the id; BuildReplaceRequest then serializes the same document again for the request body. Two full JsonSerializer.Serialize passes per call. Refactor: serialize once, extract id from the serialized JSON (or expose an id accessor via a constrained interface). Discovered in T3 code review (commit 949f54a). Not urgent — lease-acquire Replace path is at most ~288 calls/day in prod. File as Polish before broader adoption of the CAS methods.
notes: COMPLETED: BuildReplaceRequest in CosmosRestClient now serializes the document exactly once. JsonSerializer.SerializeToUtf8Bytes produces a byte[] that is parsed once with JsonDocument to extract id, then reused as ByteArrayContent for the request body. GetDocumentId helper deleted (was the second serialize). Added regression test TryReplaceDocument_SerializesDocumentExactlyOnce that wires a counting JsonConverter through JsonSerializerOptions+TestSerializerContext and asserts WriteCount == 1. IN PROGRESS: nothing. NEXT: ready for review/merge. BLOCKER: none. KEY DECISIONS: used SerializeToUtf8Bytes + ByteArrayContent (not StringContent + string) to avoid an extra UTF-16 to UTF-8 trip; ContentType set explicitly to application/json with charset=utf-8 to preserve prior wire behaviour; counting test uses options.GetTypeInfo() composing TestSerializerContext + custom converter rather than reflection. Pre-existing integration tests (HealthTests, WatchZoneTests) fail on missing INTEGRATION_TEST_* env vars — unrelated to this change.

title: Assert FakeCycleSelector.GetCurrentCallCount == 1 in metric tag test
description: The FakeCycleSelector tracks GetCurrentCallCount but no test asserts on it. Adding await Assert.That(cycleSelector.GetCurrentCallCount).IsEqualTo(1) to Should_TagAuthoritiesPolled_With_CycleType in PollPlanItCommandHandlerMetricsTests.cs would prevent a future refactor from accidentally re-resolving cycle type per-authority (which could split tag values across a single boundary-crossing poll run). One-line test edit. Follow-up to epic tc-ethl.

value: CLR '# of Exceps Thrown / sec' counter measures ALL exceptions including first-chance exceptions caught internally by the .NET runtime (HTTP client retries, DNS, SSL, connection pooling). An empty App Insights exceptions table with non-zero CLR counter is NOT evidence of a broken OTel pipeline — cross-reference with failed dependencies before filing. This was a recurring false positive in observatory runs from Apr 6-11 2026.

The Service Bus 'poll' queue in prod (sb-town-crier-prod / rg-town-crier-prod) must NEVER have more than 1 message at a time under steady state (1 scheduled is healthy; 0 is transient; >1 means duplicate publish-after-consume chains running in parallel, which hammers PlanIt at 2-3x the Retry-After-compliant rate). User has pre-authorized autonomous brute-force purge (az servicebus queue delete + create) whenever count>1 is detected, without confirmation — even overnight. Bootstrap cron (*/30 * * * *) on job-tc-poll-bootstrap-prod re-seeds one message after purge. Queue-scope RBAC assignments survive delete/recreate. Non-default queue settings to preserve: --lock-duration PT5M, --default-message-time-to-live PT1H, --enable-dead-lettering-on-message-expiration true.

bd worktree remove expects a NAME (e.g. 'autopilot-tc-ig85'), not a path. Passing a full path fails with 'Error: cannot remove main repository as worktree' because bd can't resolve the path and falls back to treating it as the main repo. Use jq '.name' from 'bd worktree list --json' (filtering out 'is_main: true'), not '.path'. This was a recurring source of noise in autopilot loops.

New methods on ICosmosRestClient/CosmosRestClient follow a locked-in pattern established on feat-polling-lease-cas (2026-04-23): (1) Every method opens an Activity via CosmosInstrumentation.Source.StartActivity('Cosmos <OperationName>') with db.system/db.cosmosdb.container/db.operation.name tags and calls RecordResponseMetrics(activity, response) after SendAsync BEFORE branching on status code — critical for RU telemetry on the polling hot path. Missing this was caught in T2 code review (commit 68183ad added the block). (2) Tests use the CreateClient() tuple helper + StubHttpHandler.EnqueueResponse(status) + post-hoc handler.SentRequests[0].Headers.XxxMatch.Single().Tag pattern for header assertions. Do NOT add delegate-based HttpMessageHandler classes (ValidationHttpHandler was deleted in 68183ad) or a BuildClient(handler) helper. Any new Cosmos REST method PR should mention both conventions explicitly.

Worker DI registration must be conditional on the env vars the registration eagerly validates. AddServiceBusRestClient (ServiceBusServiceExtensions.cs) reads ServiceBus:* config and throws InvalidOperationException at registration time, which in Native AOT crashes the worker as exit 139 BEFORE OTel can flush — so failures are silent in App Insights. Three Container Apps Jobs (digest, digest-hourly, dormant-cleanup) ran broken for 3+ days because of this. Lesson: any DI extension that throws at registration time on missing config must be guarded by an 'if config present' check in Program.cs, OR the extension must defer validation to resolution time. Bead: tc-eijl.

Azure Service Bus LockDuration is capped at 5 minutes across ALL tiers (Basic, Standard, Premium) — it's a platform limit, not tier-specific. First broke CD Production on v0.9.1 (tc-q2ts, PR #277, MessagingGatewayBadRequest). The handler-side mitigation is a soft handler budget of ~4 minutes (POLLING_HANDLER_BUDGET_SECONDS, default 240) so the handler bails before the SB lock expires. Do NOT propose raising LockDuration to 10m — it will fail at deploy time. The 4m soft + 5m hard cap pairing is the agreed solution (ref tc-eduk closed 2026-04-22).

Subagent dispatches for .NET work in Town Crier MUST lock CWD explicitly in the dispatch prompt. dotnet-tdd-worker and similar subagents do NOT inherit the parent session's worktree CWD — every dispatch prompt must start with 'First command: cd /absolute/path/to/worktree && pwd && git branch --show-current. If wrong tree/branch, report BLOCKED immediately.' Without this, agents default to /Users/christy/Dev/town-crier (main tree) and commit to the wrong branch. First observed 2026-04-23 on T4 of feat-polling-lease-cas — haiku model under-specified prompt committed d7568fe to spec/polling-lease-cas in main tree; required cherry-pick attempt (failed due to helper-pattern regression) then full redo with explicit CWD lock.

Beads-to-DoltHub push silently broke once during the embedded-dolt → dolt-server migration in this repo (~2026-04-24). Migration left the dolt repo's binary state intact (so 'cd .beads/dolt/town_crier && dolt remote -v' still showed origin) but the SQL server's view of remotes was empty. Symptom: bd dolt push says 'No remote configured'; bd dolt remote list is empty; .beads/dolt-server.log fills with 'fatal: remote origin not found' on every auto-sync attempt. Fix: bd dolt remote add origin https://doltremoteapi.dolthub.com/amyde/town-crier — re-registers it server-side. Verified 2026-04-28: the re-add survives 'bd dolt stop && bd dolt start', so this was a one-shot migration loss, not a per-restart leak — no upstream beads issue worth filing.

On 2026-04-24, PR #291 (polling-lease-cas) added a fail-fast worker guard 'PollingOptions.HandlerBudget must be set in poll-sb mode' but never set the value — the property defaulted to null. Production poll-sb worker bricked from v0.9.12 deploy at 08:02 UTC until v0.9.13 hotfix at 09:20 UTC (78 minutes). Two compounding factors hid it: (1) every unit test set HandlerBudget = TimeSpan.FromMinutes(4) explicitly, so the default-null path was never tested; (2) a stale comment in Program.cs explicitly stated 'HandlerBudget is left null in production' — written under ADR 0024's pre-CAS rationale and never updated when T16 introduced the new lease-TTL invariant in the same PR. Lesson: when adding a guard that asserts a config invariant, EITHER set a sane default on the option type OR add a wiring/integration test that builds the prod-shape options graph (not just unit tests that bypass it). Audit any existing comment claims about config defaults to make sure they still hold. Belt-and-braces guard remains useful but can't substitute for actually setting the value.
